**REPORT OF THE**

**COMMITTEE ON MISSION TO THE WORLD**

_See 52-32 (p. 58) for the report of the Committee of Commissioners_

_and the Assembly’s action_

**Vision:** _**The gospel of the kingdom advancing throughout the world**_

We want the gospel to spread throughout the world, the Church to
grow, Satan’s kingdom destroyed, and Christ’s reign extended to the
ends of the earth.

**Mission:** _**Making disciples among all nations**_

We are called to be obedient to the Great Commission by teaching
people to follow Jesus as Lord and Savior, to be baptized, and to
obey all that Jesus commands.
**Values:**

- _**Church**_
The establishment, growth, and maturity of the Church in all our
ministry efforts.

- _**Grace-based**_

Community life and ministry shaped by God’s grace for us in His
Son, Jesus Christ.

- _**Reformed and Covenantal**_
A ministry that is guided, inspired, and shaped by our theology.

- _**Mercy, Justice, and the Love of God**_
A love for God that is demonstrated through acts of mercy and
justice.

**STRATEGIC PRIORITIES**

It has been 10 years since I was first elected coordinator of MTW. When I
began, I was asked by the committee at the time to focus on three strategic
priorities: mobilization, diversity, and globalization. This is what God has
done over these past 10 years.

**Mobilization** —

- Since 2015, we have seen God mobilize

<a id="ga52-p376"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=378 printed_page=376 -->

o 486 new missionaries

o 787 new interns

- We have established a mobilization presence across the U.S. which
has resulted in greater engagement with PCA churches in mission.

- We have mobilized prayer with the 1% Challenge and, in 2019, a
survey of 171 responding churches indicated that 0.96% of their adult
members were mobilized.

—
**Diversity**

- Since 2015, we have seen God make our mission force more
accurately reflect the ethnic diversity in the PCA, mobilizing more
missionaries across our denomination.

- We have established the diversity network to introduce minority
candidates to churches interested in supporting minority candidates,
enabling more missionaries to get to the field faster.

- We have training for all new missionaries on intercultural
competencies and are developing training for existing missionaries to
help foster healthier teams.
**Globalization** —

- Since 2015, we have seen God increase our national partners more
than ever before. We now have 1,252 national church planting
partners.

- We have 10 international associate units in MTW. These are non-U.S.

citizens who have gone through the same application, vetting, and
training process as U.S. citizens serving with us.

- We have seen an increase of cooperative international teams made up
of MTW missionaries and missionaries from other countries

(Australia, Brazil, etc.).
Please give thanks to God with us for His faithfulness in hearing our prayers
and raising up workers for His harvest field.

—
**Looking Ahead**
As we think about what MTW as an organization should prioritize in the next
10 years, we have put our field goals [1] before us and asked, “What are those
leading indicators that move the mission forward? What are those things that

1 MTW fields goals: From 2020 to 2030 we want to see: 63 new countries
engaged; 192 new cities engaged; 486 new churches planted; 29 new campus
ministries started; 38 new unreached people groups engaged; 212 new church
planting support ministries started.

<a id="ga52-p377"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=379 printed_page=377 -->

are needed to accomplish new churches being planted, new unreached people
groups being reached, new church planting support ministries started?” The
three strategic priorities we believe are needed for the next season are:
_**mobilization**_, _**developing leaders,**_ and _**healthy teams**_ .

**Mobilization** —

While we are grateful for the many new workers who have answered the call,
we need to continue to pray for more laborers for the harvest. Not only is this
something the Lord has commanded (Matt. 9:35-38), but more workers are
needed to accomplish our field goals. And so we will continue to prioritize
working with the church to send more missionaries. Please continue to pray
for the 1% to be mobilized for global missions.

—
**Developing Leaders**
Even if we have many new missionaries who want to go, without strong
leaders to lead, develop, and care for these new missionaries, we will not be
able to progress in our mission. Not only do we need to recruit strong leaders,
but we also need to develop leaders within our MTW community. What we
are talking about is not simply more training, which is important, but creating
a culture of developing leaders. We need excellent leaders to recruit, retain,
and inspire the army of missionaries we want to see engaged in kingdom
advancing work. This is why we need to focus on developing leaders. Please
pray that God would raise up godly leaders to serve our teams and that we
would develop a culture of developing leaders.

—
**Healthy Teams**
Another leading indicator to achieving our field goals are healthy teams. What
often hinders the work of the gospel are dysfunctional teams. The teams that
advance our mission are teams that are healthy. Having a healthy team is not
only about having a healthy leader—which is important—it is about every
member being invested in the team and committed to biblical conflict
resolution when needed. Healthy teams also mean individual missionaries and
staff practicing self-care, personal development, accessing member care and
counseling resources, and prioritizing rest and healthy boundaries. We believe
healthy teams are vital in helping us achieve our field goals. Please pray that
our field and staff teams would be healthy and that every member would be
committed to developing healthy teams.

<a id="ga52-p378"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=380 printed_page=378 -->

**Global Missions Conference** —

In November 2024, we hosted the PCA Global Missions Conference. This was
a highlight of the year for MTW. Our staff and missionaries went above and
beyond the call of duty to make this event possible. Consistent, frequent prayer
meetings for almost a year gave birth to a Spirit-filled event. We had over
1,800 total participants and over 200 college students. We heard great
feedback on the plenary sessions, the seminars, and especially the
collaboration and unity with MNA and RUF. This event has brought great
hope and excitement for the future of the PCA. Please pray that the fruit of this
event will continue to lead to new missionaries, new enthusiasm for global
missions, and greater collaboration and unity in the PCA.

**Overture 33** —
In response to Overture 33 of the PCA’s fifty-first general assembly, CMTW
has prepared this report answering the questions related to Sarah Young's
book, _Jesus Calling_ :

1. Examine MTW’s relationship with the book, knowledge of its
content, and any counsel given to the author.

The author and her husband served with MTW for 41 years, first
in Japan, then in Australia, and finally in Nashville until the
author’s passing on August 31, 2023. Her widower, Stephen
Young, continues to serve with MTW. As an organization, we
miss her dearly but rejoice that she’s in the arms of her precious
Savior.

While MTW had a missionary/sending agency relationship with
the Youngs for many decades, MTW had no relationship or
connection with the writing or publication of the book itself. The
author did not write or publish the book at MTW’s request or with
MTW’s involvement, and MTW did not review, edit, or approve
the book in advance of its release.

After MTW’s good faith investigation of the matter addressed by
the Overture—bearing in mind the amount of time that has passed
since the book was written and published—MTW was unable to
ascertain whether the author asked any individuals in MTW to

<a id="ga52-p379"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=381 printed_page=379 -->

give any counsel regarding the content of the book. To the best of
MTW’s knowledge, the author wrote this book independently.

2. Consider actions that MTW and the General Assembly should
take in light of this study of the book and of the agency’s
relationship to it.

Since MTW had and has no formal relationship with the book and
its author is now with the Lord, it has no recommendations
regarding actions related to the book.

Lloyd Kim, Coordinator

<a id="ga52-p380"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=382 printed_page=380 -->

**2024 GLOBAL MINISTRY HIGHLIGHTS**

**A** **SIA** **/P** **ACIFIC**

2024 has been a year of significant growth and encouragement across the
MTW Asia/Pacific region. Here are some key highlights of what God has been
doing in our community:

**Leadership Development Through Monthly Leadership Cohorts** —
We launched monthly leadership cohorts designed to develop and equip team
leaders and emerging leaders across the region. These cohorts have provided
a valuable space for skill development, accountability, and the cultivation of
godly leadership rooted in Scripture. It has been deeply encouraging to see
how the participants are growing together as leaders. Please pray for continued
growth and maturity, that these leaders would be marked by humility and a
steadfast dependence on the Holy Spirit.

**Area Retreat** —

In July 2024, the Asia/Pacific region held its much-anticipated area retreat.
This gathering brought together missionaries and their families from across the
region for a time of worship, fellowship, and renewal. The retreat offered a
much-needed opportunity to step away from the demands of daily ministry,
reconnect with other missionaries, and hear from God’s Word. These retreats
are vital for the health of our community, providing encouragement and
strengthening perseverance with joy. We also cherish the connections fostered
among our missionary kids during this time. Please pray for the health of
marriages, families, and teams. Cross-cultural ministry is challenging, and we
rely on the Lord to sustain our missionary families.

**The Formation of Prayer Cohorts** —
Recognizing the need to grow in prayer as a region, intentional steps were
taken to address this. At the area retreat, a representative from Paul Miller’s
ministry, See Jesus, trained us in prayer. This initial training was so impactful
that we began rolling out prayer cohorts, starting with leadership. These
cohorts have not only strengthened spiritual bonds but have also fostered a
deeper dependence on the Lord. Please pray for continued reliance on the
Spirit, that our missionaries would be a praying people, and that God would
hear and answer the prayers of His people. Pray, too, as these cohorts are

<a id="ga52-p381"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=383 printed_page=381 -->

expanded to more missionaries and partners, that there would be an eagerness
to seek the Lord in prayer.

We are grateful for God’s work in these areas and remain committed to seeing
the gospel advance in the Asia-Pacific region. Thank you for your faithful
prayers and partnership in this mission!

**E** **UROPE**

—
**Leadership Development**
Several important events and opportunities for leadership development in
Europe occurred in 2024. MTW leaders from all over Europe gathered in
Edinburgh in October for the European Leadership Forum (ELF). They heard
teaching from Dr. Dan Strange based on his book, _Making Faith Magnetic_,
and benefitted from interacting with MTW leaders, Cartee Bales and Mark
Bates.

But events like ELF weren’t the only contexts for growth in 2024: MTW
Europe also celebrated new leadership opportunities on more individual levels.
Phil and Wendy Stogner (church planting trainers/assessors) conducted the
first-ever church planting assessment with Jamie and Jennifer Burkemper
(Madrid, Spain), approving them for church planting work there. Vincenzo and
Judit Coluccia (Lecce, Italy) participated in a leadership assessment in advance
of Vincenzo being named a new team leader; Brian and Megan Moore
(Freiburg, Germany) also took part in a leadership assessment that saw Brian
welcomed to the Europe Strategy Team as the newest regional director,
overseeing the works in Germany and Poland.

**Growing Partnership between MTW and the International Presbyterian**
**Church (IPC)** —
We rejoice in any gospel-centered church or denomination’s growth and
multiplication; one such denomination and its relationship with MTW Europe
in 2024 is the IPC. MTW missionaries working in current IPC churches or in
churches who are pursuing a relationship with the IPC are the following:

- Rob/Jenny Ilderton, Todd/Liz Crusey, and Kersten Kazen (London,
England)

- Brett/Valerie McAlpin (Leeds, England)

- Julian/Rachel Bacon and Daniel/Katy Brink (Brussels, Belgium)

<a id="ga52-p382"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=384 printed_page=382 -->

- Dawson/Shanna Bean (Prague, Czechia)

- Andrew/Laura Kate Lupton and Jonas/Pattie Stava (Oslo, Norway)

- Jon/Tanya C. (sensitive location)

- Derek/Shannon Ebbers (Brasov, Romania)

- National Partners in Gdansk, Poland; Kuopio, Finland; Sibiu,
Romania

**Ukraine War Update** —
February 24, 2025, marks three years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of
Ukraine in 2022. We continue to grieve with our brothers and sisters there and
also to proclaim that God remains on His throne.

As of this writing (January 20, 2025), here are the numbers of the Crates for
Ukraine initiative distributed since the start of the war:

- 3,503 crates: ~ 84 tons of aid valued at ~$4.7 million

- Aid delivered to 3,500 locations has helped over 120,000 people

We continue to pray for a just end to this war.

**S** **UB** **-S** **AHARAN** **A** **FRICA**

**International Director** —

Victor Nakah continued in his role as international director for sub-Saharan

Africa in 2024. One of the highlights was his participation with the Lausanne
movement as the co-chair of the Theology Working Group leading to the
congress in Seoul, Korea. while it was a challenge to work with a diverse
group of evangelical theologians, serving the global Church in this way was a
real privilege.

Another highlight was sub-Saharan Africa’s first internship program, which
was held in Kigali, Rwanda. Just the fact that it happened is worth celebrating.
After an evaluation, plans are in place for the 2025 program. The broader
vision is to bring together interns from PCA churches and our national partners
as one way to train the next generation of missionaries. We also plan to
transform this into our field training hub for missionaries.

**Member Care** —

Dan M.* continued in his role as member care coordinator for sub-Saharan

Africa. Janet, his wife, assists Dan in member care ministry. Dan and Janet

<a id="ga52-p383"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=385 printed_page=383 -->

serve as the member care associates for the West Africa region. They met with
team members who serve as member care associates for Ethiopia and Southern
Africa. In 2024, the member care associates for East Africa retired and Dan
filled that role as he was able. Pray for the Lord to raise up member care
associates for East Africa.

**West Africa** —

Jim W.*, based in West Africa with his wife and regional administrative
assistant, Karen, continued his third year as regional director for West Africa.
In this role, Jim coordinated MTW’s strategic partnership West Africa
Reformed Mission with six partner denominations in the region, recruited
missionaries, raised funds for various church planting and theological
education projects, spoke at GMC breakout sessions and mission conferences,
and provided oversight and care to MTW field workers in the region.

In the past year, Timothy House programs in Senegal and The Gambia
graduated nine church planters; a third program in another sensitive country
strengthened; 11 men were ordained to gospel ministry; a second RUF-Global
ministry was started in The Gambia aided by Collin J.* and his wife, Zury; a
new curriculum for small business training was adapted, and a new center-city
church plant launched in Dakar, Senegal. Donnie W.* transitioned to the role
of team leader in Senegal and continued mentoring church planters working
among an unreached people group, and his wife, Kara, assisted the church
planting through an ESL outreach.

MTW West Africa is grieving the heavy loss of Heather J.*, wife of Ian J.*
and mother of three, who passed away in early June from an undiagnosed, rare
form of leukemia while serving with her family in West Africa. Ian is currently
residing in Chattanooga with his children and providing leadership for the
ministry in West Africa from a distance. Ian’s brother and teammate, Evan,
and his family also left the field in West Africa on an approved seminary (RTSJackson) study leave in the U.S. While based in the U.S., Evan continues to
provide leadership for agricultural training in the region.

A recent graduate from Westminster Theological Seminary, Charles T.*, and
his wife, Irene, born and raised in West Africa before immigrating to the U.S.,
have started raising support to serve in Sierra Leone. MTW West Africa
welcomed its first vocational missionary family serving with MTW’s 18.26
Network and is preparing to welcome a second family early in 2025.

<a id="ga52-p384"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=386 printed_page=384 -->

**East Africa** —

The past year has been a season of transitions for the East Africa region. Bruce
and Pam Sinclair in Uganda, Don and Fran McNeill in East Africa, along with
the regional director of East Africa, Frank and Cindy Sindler, retired after
many years of faithful service. In August 2024, Sam Kang assumed the role of
regional director. He and his wife Gloria, and their two children will be
relocating to Kenya, where Sam will fulfill RD responsibilities and establish a
new ministry team in the country.

The board for East Africa, previously known as PREACH (Presbyterian
Reformed East Africa Council with the Horn), was renamed RMEA
(Reformed Mission of East Africa) to align with other MTW Africa boards.
The board’s purpose remains unchanged: to resource ministry efforts in East
Africa and the Horn. Several new members have joined the reorganized board.

In Kigali, Rwanda, John and Rachel Clifford have successfully facilitated the
first sub-Saharan Africa internship program. This program, which the region
and MTW Africa plan to host annually, has been a significant milestone. The
Cliffords are also prayerfully considering launching a church plant in Kigali.

In Uganda, Ben and Kim Church recently helped the Christ Our Refuge church
plant transition to a permanent location purchased by the congregation. This
move has expanded the church’s capacity to serve the local community
effectively.

**Southern Africa** —

MTW’s support for church planting and presbytery development continued in
2024. In Malawi, the church-planting internship program under Confex
Makhalira’s church continued to invest in seven interns. One intern is now

actively planting a church in the Blantyre area, and another is preparing to
plant in another major city. A third intern is in the U.S. completing theological
training, and the other four interns are in hands-on ministry under Confex’s
direct supervision at his church. The expectation is that a presbytery will be
formed as these church plants multiply and eventually become particularized.

In Zimbabwe, Jorum Mugari developed ordination standards and processes for
TEs and REs, as well as requirements for licensure for the Presbytery of
Bulawayo. Evah Mugari was instrumental in organizing two major events that

<a id="ga52-p385"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=387 printed_page=385 -->

contributed significantly to the development of her presbytery’s women’s
ministry: a women’s conference of 165 attendees focusing on the topic of
spiritual mothering, and a women’s leadership retreat involving 20 attendees.

In South Africa, after 18 fruitful years with MTW, Susan Newkirk returned to
the U.S. and accepted a children’s director position at a PCA church in the St.
Louis area. We are very grateful for her faithful service over these many years.
Coby and Pamela McGinty and Hunter and Laura Quinn have each joined
church-planting teams connected to plants of the Presbyterian and Reformed
Church of South Africa (PARCSA). These two church plants are in
communities with major universities. The intent is to start Reformed
University Fellowship (RUF) ministries at these schools through these plants
as they get established in their respective communities. This is part of a broader
vision to establish RUF on five campuses in Southern Africa in the next couple
of years.

Much time has been devoted to planning a Southern Africa Reformed
Presbyterian Leadership Conference in October 2025. The conference will
incorporate all TEs, REs, interns, and key women leaders from the presbyteries
MTW works with in South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Malawi. The speaker will
be Chad Van Dixhoorn of RTS Charlotte. Keynote and workshop sessions will
focus on topics designed to deepen Reformed Presbyterian doctrine and
practice. To further aid presbytery development, some of our missionaries also
participated ina project to create a book of church order for PARCSA.

**A** **MERICAS**

—
**Bogotá, Colombia**
The Resurrection church plant that has grown out of the RUF ministry received
its first 39 members in October and is moving toward particularization and
more robust community life. The church plant also received funding for 2025
from the giving focus at the Global Missions Conference. Moving forward, the
church is poised to celebrate the Lord's Supper, receive a few more members,
and engage in cultivating better small group spaces that are vital to evangelism
and discipleship. Efforts to secure and renovate a ministry center to train new
RUF pastors and church planters continue to move forward. The team hopes
to soon have a permanent base for reaching and equipping Bogotá for Christ's
church and kingdom at the three leading universities and in the 20 localities of
the city.

<a id="ga52-p386"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=388 printed_page=386 -->

**Native America - Cherokee** —
Grace Community Church of Cherokee continues to reach out to their
community with the truth of the gospel. Recently, a young man surrendered
his life to Christ following the worship service. Additionally, a new young
couple has been attending worship, and there seems to be a growing
commitment from some who have been attending to be more dedicated to
regular worship. God continues to bless the Wednesday evening Bible study
through discussions about the sovereignty of God.

**Chile** —

The city of Viña del Mar continues to recover after wildfires ravaged the
region in early February. The devastation has been heartbreaking, but God
moved in the hearts of many donors and provided emergency funds for the
MTW team to help victims, especially those in or related to people in their
churches. Some needed temporary housing, others needed help salvaging what
was left of their property, and still others needed to replace their work
equipment to be able to once again generate family income. In one case,
Fernando, a blind man who runs a firewood business, was provided with a new
chainsaw, which he expertly uses!

—
**Monterrey, Mexico**
The Centro para la Plantación de Iglesias (CPI) in Monterrey had a busy and
fruitful summer. A program was held for interns from San Pablo Seminary.
Jorge Aleman and Charles Davidson also finished a church planter training
with Peninsular Presbytery in Merida, Yucatan. We rejoice that one of their
church planters was recently licensed to preach by his presbytery. CPI also
entered into agreements to collaborate with Paso del Norte Presbytery to plant
a church in Chihuahua (with planter Armando Cuevas) and with the Antioquia
Presbyterian Church to plant in Merida.

**G** **LOBAL** **M** **USLIM** **M** **INISTRIES**

War in the Middle East has shaken the Muslim world, where many of our field
workers serve and are planning to serve. In 2024, we had field placements
delayed, field workers evacuated, and some even denied re-entry from where
they have served for many years.

Though placing workers in the Muslim world is not easy, GMM continues to

<a id="ga52-p387"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=389 printed_page=387 -->

partner with PCA fellowships and presbyteries to raise up laborers for the
harvest. The Lord has been gracious in answering our prayers, as a record
number of applicants have come alongside our growing teams in these areas.
At the same time, several workers have retired or transitioned because they
fulfilled their time of service, suffered from health concerns, needed to care
for family, were called to new ministries, or were not able to sustain the needed
funding to keep them on the field. This type of turnover is difficult and greatly
affects the progress of the work.

Our field workers continue to keep church planting at the forefront of the work.
This past year, several new groups were formed, and leaders are being raised
up to help shepherd these small gatherings of worshippers. Relief efforts and
theological education, as well as Bible studies and social media, are key ways
that contacts are made, the gospel is shared, and the lost are saved.

There are more than enough opportunities to join God in what He is doing in
the Muslim world. In light of this, we attempt to position ourselves to be able
to mobilize, train, and support these teams serving in these difficult locations
among the unreached. This past year, we have made progress in developing
initial term field training and leadership training. We hope that these efforts
will produce healthy teams that can keep the mission of making disciples at
the front of what they are doing as well as care for one another amid
challenging circumstances.

It is our hope that through this priority on mobilization, leadership
development, and healthy teams, we will be able to bolster our existing teams
with quality candidates and continue to work and pray toward launching five
new works in North Africa, Middle East, Central Asia, and the Asian Crescent
to the end of establishing worshipping communities in the Muslim world in
the years to come.

_*Last names withheld for security reasons._

**R** **ECOMMENDATIONS**

The Committee on Mission to the World (CMTW) makes the following
recommendations to the Fifty-Second General Assembly of the Presbyterian
Church in America:

36. That the General Assembly urge churches to set aside the month of

November 2025 as a month of prayer for global missions, asking God to
send many more laborers into His harvest field. (MTW will offer a 30
Days of Prayer Calendar, which your church can download from
mtw.org/30dop in the fall as well as other resources);

37. That the General Assembly urge churches to set aside a portion of their

giving for the suffering peoples of the world; to that end, be it
recommended that a special offering for relief and mercy (MTW
Compassion offering) be taken during 2025 and distributed by MTW
(MTW offers bulletin inserts by mail, as well as a digital version that can
be downloaded at mtw.org/compassion);

38. That the General Assembly urge churches to set aside Sunday, November

2, 2025, as a day of prayer for the persecuted church worldwide;

39. Having performed an annual review of our coordinator, we give all praise,

honor, and glory to God and thank Him for Dr. Lloyd Kim and his
gracious, humble, and effective servant-leadership. CMTW unanimously
recommends to the General Assembly that Dr. Lloyd Kim be re-elected
coordinator of Mission to the World.

40. That the proposed budget of MTW, as presented through the

Administrative Committee, be approved;

41. That the General Assembly approve the minutes of the meetings of the

Permanent Committee on Mission to the World dated March 13-14, 2024
and September 18-19, 2024 without exception.

42. That **Overture 9** from James River Presbytery regarding, “Amend BCO

14-1 to Make AC Members Appointed by Committees and Agencies Nonvoting Advisory Members,” be answered in the **NEGATIVE.**

Grounds:

As the overture states, “the General Assembly’s
Administrative Committee exercises tremendous influence

over the PCA’s national operations…the preparation of
budgets for permanent committees and agencies; arranging

<a id="ga52-p394"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=396 printed_page=394 -->

for the General Assembly to meet each year…” These
functions directly affect committees and agencies. The
present system allows for committees and agencies to speak
and vote on matters that directly affect them. This overture
would disenfranchise committees and agencies.

The committees and agencies not only vote to approve their
representatives to the AC, but also these representatives have all
been duly elected by the General Assembly to carry out the duties
of the committees and agencies, including (for some) representing
the committees and agencies on the Administrative Committee.
Therefore, in our current practice, 100% of the voting members
serving on the AC committee have been elected by the General
Assembly. This overture is trying to solve a problem that does not
exist.

43. That **Overture 11** from South Texas Presbytery regarding, “To Study

Incorporating Bitcoin into Session, Presbytery, and Committee & Agency
Balance Sheets,” be answered in the **NEGATIVE.**

Grounds:

The overture is outside the purview of the General
Assembly. The study or not of a specific investment product
(Bitcoin) is best determined by each session, presbytery,
committee and agency, not the general assembly.
Secondly, this overture would require time and energy at
every level of the church that may be better used in carrying out
the mission of the church. These decisions should be left at the

most local level rather than dictated by the General Assembly.

Respectfully submitted,
TE Owen Lee, Chairman
Committee on Mission to the World

<a id="ga52-p395"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=397 printed_page=395 -->

Supplemental Report

**SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT OF THE**

**COMMITTEE ON MISSION TO THE WORLD**

The Committee on Mission to the World submits the following
Recommendations 9-10 as a supplemental report to the 52 [nd] General
Assembly.

**R** **ECOMMENDATIONS**

44. That **Overture 27** from Northwest Georgia Presbytery regarding, “Erect

Ad Interim Committee to Receive Suggestions for Permanent Committee
and Agency Operations, Resources, and Expenses,” be answered in the
**NEGATIVE** .

Grounds:

The ad interim committee proposed overture is not given clear
direction of expected outcomes or deliverable results, nor is it
given criteria on how solicited recommendations would be
evaluated or reported. According to _RAO_ 9-1 ad interim
committees may only be erected to “handle particular matters of
business”. The proposed overture fails to meet _RAO_ 9-1
requirements.

The ad interim committee proposal is redundant and
confusing. Lower courts already send qualified elders to serve on
our Permanent Committees and Agencies who have the
responsibility of evaluating and overseeing the operations,
resources, and expenses of the committees and agencies. The
committees and agencies also use external auditing firms to do
impartial and thorough yearly audits of operations and expenses.
c mt w has a subcommittee that does a thorough review of the
audit before it is approved at the committee level. Furthermore,
each committee and agency’s work are reported, reviewed and
approved by the CoC of each committee or agency, AC
Committee (in relation to the respective budgets), and the General
Assembly. Introducing another committee to do the same work of
our existing committees and agencies and communicated through

<a id="ga52-p396"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=398 printed_page=396 -->

different channels (website, ad interim committee report) would
be confusing and potentially lead to inconsistent decision making,
results, and actions on behalf of the denomination. In addition, this
overture could violate donor intent.

Finally, this overture, by its proposed means of collecting and
distributing “recommendations for improvement, optimization,
and greater transparency” creates a platform with the potential to
divide.

45. That **Overture 30** from Houston Metro Presbytery (“Amend _BCO_ 8-4, 20
1, 21-1, 21-12, and 23-1 re the Calling and Dissolution of TE Relationships
for Needful Works”) be **REFERRED** back to Houston Metro Presbytery
without prejudice.

Respectfully submitted,
TE Owen Lee, Chairman
Committee on Mission to the World

**MINUTES OF THE**

**NOMINATING COMMITTEE**

**March 22, 2025**

_See 52-35 (p. 66) for the report of the Assembly’s action_

The Nominating Committee of the General Assembly convened in Atlanta,
GA on Saturday, March 22, 2025. Chairman TE Matthew Adams called the
meeting to order at 8:30 a.m. and RE Joshua Torrey opened with prayer. The
chairman then led the Committee in a devotional from Isaiah 6:1-8.

Attendance was taken and a quorum was declared. The Chairman welcomed
the Committee and recognized three guests from the PCA Administrative
Committee Office, TE Bryan Chapell, Stated Clerk, Ms. Heidi Harrison,
Operations Manager, and Ms. Angela Nantz, Meeting Planner. Seventy-eight
committee members were in attendance as follows, and two additional
members submitted preliminary ballots.

**Members attending:**

**Presbytery** **Member** **Class**

Wisconsin Rev. Michael Vogel 2027

The committee received the subcommittee report on voting strategy.

**M/P** To suspend the rules to use the voting method of weighted votes for
committees and commission and raw votes for the agencies as recommended
by the subcommittee.

**M/S/P** To amend the operations manual of the Nominating Committee to
allow candidates to use letters of recommendation written by individuals
outside their presbyteries.

**M/S/P** To require nominations submissions to file biographical information
digitally.

<a id="ga52-p406"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=408 printed_page=406 -->

Preliminary vote tallies were discussed by the Committee. The Committee
approved a slate of nominees for each of the Standing Committees, Agencies,
and Commission to be presented to the General Assembly.

Nominations were entertained for Chairman and Secretary of the 2025-2026
Nominating Committee. The Committee elected TE Benjamin Lyon,
Tidewater Presbytery, to serve as Chairman and TE Tomothy LeCroy, New
York State Presbytery, as Secretary.

The Chairman announced that the next meeting of the Nominating Committee
will be at General Assembly in Chattanooga, TN, on Wednesday, June 25,
2025, at the close of business. The 2026 meeting will be held in Atlanta, GA,
on Saturday, March 28, 2026.

**M/S/P** To dismiss the previously appointed subcommittee to address the
weighted voting system with thanks.

**M/S/P** To form a subcommittee made up of RE Howie Donahoe, TE Fred
Greco, and RE Frank Cohee, to formulate the manual language changes
incorporating new voting method.

**M/S/P** The Committee adjourned at 12:30 pm, with prayer offered by TE
Porter Harlow.

Respectfully Submitted,
TE Matthew Dallas Adams, Chairman TE Michael VanDerLinden, Secretary

<a id="ga52-p408"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=410 printed_page=408 -->

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Robert Brunson:** _Central Georgia_ . BA, Communications, University of
Alabama, 1979. MDiv RTS 1986. Over his 37 years of ordained ministry, Bob
has worked as either a solo/senior Pastor (14 years) or Executive Pastor (18
years) including his current role as Executive Pastor at North Macon
Presbyterian Church. Extensive experience in budget, financial, and longrange planning as well as compensation and benefit plan development,
personnel and volunteer management, database and new ministry
development, and facility/construction planning. Four-time Presbytery
Moderator. Previous service on Ridge Haven Board (10 years) and on AC
Permanent Committee (12 years), several times as chairman.

**RE Todd Carlisle:** _Evangel._ BS, Samford University, 1988. J.D. Samford,
1991. RE at Briarwood Presbyterian Church for the last 25 years. He works as
an attorney specializing in corporate and merger & acquisition law. Served on
the Boards of Campus Outreach Birmingham and Briarwood Christian School
and served more than 10 years on the Board of United Way of Central
Alabama. Todd has previously served a multi-year term on the AC.

**RE Frank Cohee:** _Providence_ . BS, Cybersecurity, SNHU, 2025. RE at
Cornerstone Presbyterian in Huntsville, AL for last 11 years. Works as Chief
Security Officer and Director of Product Security for an Oil & Gas Mobility
Company specializing in cyber security operations, product development, and
secure technology solutions. He is a former Naval Intelligence Specialist.
Served as Presbytery Clerk and Moderator and is on its Admin Committee.
Member of GA Nominating Committee (and its chairman in 2018 and 2023).
Frank previously served on the GA Administrative Committee from 20192024 and currently serves on the AC Subcommittee for Technology.

**TE Brian Salter** : _Tennessee Valley_ . BS, Education, University of Georgia,
1997. MDiv RTS Orlando, 2006. On staff at Lookout Mountain Presbyterian
for the last 19 years. Senior Pastor for the last 7 years. Helped lead Lookout
Mountain and a Session of 34 men through a successful $18 million capital
campaign. He has served on various Presbytery committees including RUF,
Nominations and Presbytery's Leadership Team. Served on seven different GA
Committees of Commissioners as well as the Overtures Committee. Brian

recently coordinated efforts between AC and TVP to host GA in Chattanooga.

<a id="ga52-p409"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=411 printed_page=409 -->

**RE Rick Owens:** _North Texas_ . BS, University of Georgia, 1970. Dallas
Theological Seminary, 1984. Navy War College, 1989. University of
Michigan Executive Business School, 1992. Retired Navy fighter pilot, 19711995 (active & reserve). Executive Officer of a Carrier Group; retired as O-6
Captain (full Colonel). Retired as an American Airlines pilot (1978-2008),
having served as Chief Pilot/Base Manager and Fleet Captain Check Airman.
Led Owens-Douglas Ltd Financial Management from 2009-2019. RE at Park
Cities Presbyterian, Dallas for the last 17 years. Chaired PCPC Missions,
Personnel, and Vision Committees. Chairman of Host Committee for the 47th
GA in Dallas.

**A.** **Present Personnel**

_Teaching Elders:_ _Ruling Elders:_

**C.** **Nominations**

**Class of 2029**

TE David Gordon, Ascension RE Joshua Torrey, South Texas

**Alternates**

TE Randy Schlichting, Metro Atlanta RE Philip J. Temple, Calvary

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE T. David Gordon** : _Ascension_ . MAR, Westminster Theological Seminary,
1979. ThM, WTS, 1980. PhD, Union Theological Seminary, VA, 1984.
Taught 14 years at Gordon-Conwell Seminary in Boston (1984-1998),
including a course on Presbyterian Denominational Standards for 10 years.
Taught for 22 years at Grove City College (1999-2021). Previous moderator
of the PCA's (former) Northeast Presbytery for five years, chairing its Admin
and Candidates Committees for five years. Served on the GA Bills &
Overtures Committee in the 1990s, chairing it once. Current Moderator of
Ascension Presbytery. Served last year as TE Alternate on the CCB.

**RE Joshua Torrey** : _South Texas_ . BS, Electrical Engineering, University of
Texas at San Antonio. Has worked as a Computer Chip Engineer for 10 years
at Advanced Micro Devices and 7 at Apple. RE at Redeemer Presbyterian,
Austin, TX, where he and his family have been members for 11 years. Has
served on several Presbytery committees and is the current Moderator. Helped
rewrite Presbytery standing rules. served on ga committees of
Commissioners each year he's been at GA. re alternate on ga theological
Examining Committee in 2023. Served last year as the RE Alternate on the
CCB.

**TE Randy Schlichting** : _Metro Atlanta._ BBA, University of Houston. MA,
International Business, University of Texas-Dallas. MDiv Metro Atlanta
Seminary. DMin Birmingham Theological Seminary. Randy worked in
business for 15 years, mainly in hospital administration in the Middle East and
US. He's been in the PCA for 36 years, serving at Perimeter Church since 1997.
heist heir pastor of Shepherding. He's been MAP's Clerk since its formation
in 2012. He has attended the last 16 GAs, serving on many CoCs and as

<a id="ga52-p411"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=413 printed_page=411 -->

Parliamentarian in 2022 and 2024. He's also on the Board of the Association

of Reformed Theological Seminaries.

**RE Philip Temple** : _Calvary_ . BA, Education, Bob Jones University. MA,
Education, Clemson University. J.D. University of South Carolina. School
teacher and administrator for 6 years prior to law school. Practiced law for 40
years, including advising on church/state issues, policy formation, and risk
management. Now retired. Elder at Mitchell Road Presbyterian for 31 years.
Chair of Presbytery's CE Committee; member of Candidates and Shepherding
Committees. Commissioner to 15 GAs. Previously served four years as a
member of CCB in the class of 2016.

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Bradley Barnes** : _Southern New England_ . Undergraduate at Wake Forest
University. MDiv Gordon Conwell (2003). RUF Harvard (2004-2009).
Planted Christ the King Church in Newton MA (2009) and serving as Pastor
there to this day. Served on Covenant College Board of Trustees (2021-2025).
Grandson of Covenant College’s second president Dr. Marion Barnes.

**TE Alexander Brown** : _Savannah River_ . MDiv Free Church College (now
Edinburgh Theological Seminary). Pastor at Golden Isles Presbyterian
Church, St. Simons Island GA. Served as Moderator of Savannah River

<a id="ga52-p413"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=415 printed_page=413 -->

Presbytery (2024) and the Presbytery’s MTW and MNA Committees. At
General Assembly served on Review of Presbytery Records (2012-2014),
Overtures (2013), MTW Committee of Commissioners (2016), Covenant
College Committee of Commissioners (2015, 2017). One term on Covenant
College Board of Trustees (2021-2025). Husband to Sara, graduate of
Covenant College (2001).

**RE Robert Curtis** : _Southwest Florida_ . Vocation: Restaurateur. Ruling Elder
for 29 years. Redeemer Church Riverview FL. Five of his children have
attended Covenant College. Served nine years on Covenant College Board of
Trustees and on its Executive Committee.

**RE Mark Griggs** : _Tennessee Valley Presbytery_ . Ruling Elder at Grace +
Peace Church, Chattanooga, TN. Part of the core team at Grace + Peace’s
planting and a founding Ruling Elder. Served on Covenant College Board of
Trustees for 12 years. Currently Chairing the Student Development Committee
and serving on the Executive Committee.

**RE Drew Jelgerhuis** : _Great Lakes_ . Ruling Elder Christ Church, Grand Rapids
MI. Vocation: Engineer. Part of three church plants. Three of his children are
graduates of Covenant College. Served on term on Covenant College Board of
Trustees and served as chairman of the Advancement Committee.

**TE Dennis Louis** : _Tennessee Valley Presbytery_ . BA and M.A. Pensacola
Christian College. MDiv Reformed Theological Seminary – Jackson. Pastor
of Discipleship at McIlwain Presbyterian, Pensacola, FL (2013-2020). Senior
Pastor at Chattanooga Valley Presbyterian, Flintstone, GA (2021-current). In
Presbytery: Examinations Committee (Gulf Coast Presbytery), Candidates and
Licensure Committee (Tennessee Valley Presbytery).

**RE Marty Moore:** _Georgia Foothills_ . Retired Chief Financial Officer. Ruling
Elder at Chestnut Mountain Presbyterian Church, Flowery Branch Georgia.
Serves on their finance, human resources, and investment committees. Served
the Covenant College Board of Trustees (17 years). Roles on the Board include
Chairman of the Board, Chairman of Presidential Search, Treasurer of the
Board, Chairman of the Finance and Chairman of Audit Committees.

**RE Stephen Rowe** : _Hills and Plains_ . Vocation: University Professor and
Accountant. BS Covenant College (1999). M.S. Loyola University. PhD

<a id="ga52-p414"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=416 printed_page=414 -->

University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. Associate Professor of
Accounting at University of Arkansas and C.P.A. Previous faculty positions at
Tulane University and City University. Previously served church as deacon
and currently at as a Ruling Elder at Trinity Grace Church, Rogers AK. There
he has taught Sunday School, led small groups and served on pastoral search,
finance, benevolence and building committees. Serves on Hills and Plains
Administrative Committee.

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Tim LeCroy:** _New York State_ . BS, Chemical Engineering, NC State 2001;
MDiv CTS 2006; PhD Historical Theology, St. Louis University, 2012. Pastor
of New Life PCA, Ithaca, NY. Solo pastor of two churches, visiting instructor
at CTS, discipled children at Seneca Hills Bible Camp in Western PA the last
4 summers. Chairman of the DASA ad interim committee. PCA service: AC

Permanent Committee, (chaired the Planning and Management
subcommittee). Commissioner to 14 GAs, serving on many CoC’s. Served on
RPR, Nom Com, and Overtures. Presbytery moderator, vice moderator,
parliamentarian, chairman of Credentials, and served on Admin and RUF
committees.

**TE Jonathon Medlock:** _Northern California_ . BA, Oglethorpe University
1992; JD Samford University 1995; MDiv CTS 2005; Served the local church
as assistant and senior pastors for 15 years prior to serving as VP of People
and Culture for Geneva Benefits, where he serves pastors and their families in
the PCA. Prior he served on the Board of Directors for RBI for 10 years and
was chairman for 5. Current PhD candidate in Educational Studies at Trinity
Evangelical Divinity School.

**TE David Vosseller:** _Savannah River_ . BA Political Science, Bucknell
University 1991; MDiv CTS 1998; Senior Pastor of Lakemont Presbyterian

<a id="ga52-p416"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=418 printed_page=416 -->

Church, Augusta GA for 13 years, previously serving churches in youth and
ministry development. Training in youth ministry through P.A.C.T. Ministries,
and expositional preaching through Simeon Trust Workshops. Served on the
Christian Education Committee in Central GA Presbytery, Shepherding and
Candidates & Credentials Committee for Chicago Metro Presbytery, and
Candidates & Credentials of Savannah River Presbytery, currently serving on
the joint Committee on Campus Ministry. Served various CoCs at GA
including CDM and served two separate terms as Alternate on CDM.

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Hugh Barlett** : _Missouri_ . Bachelor’s – Wheaton; MDiv, DMin – Covenant
Theological Seminary (CTS). Senior Pastor – Chesterfield PCA (1992present). Service at CTS: Adjunct Professor (homiletics, ordination prep,
church renewal) for over 15 years; board member, first elected in 2015; board
chairman, 2023-24; member of capital campaign to expand facilities; member
of accreditation team. Presbytery Service Presbytery: Chairman, MNA
(Missouri). Previously – Chairman, Care of Churches (Heartlands Presbytery);
member, MNA, Candidates. Former campus minister at University of
Nebraska. Married to Judy for 42 years; three children and five grandchildren.

**RE Bill Bennett** : _Evangel_ . BA Furman (1991). School of Mortgage Banking
(1997). MBA Samford (1998). Senior VP, Cadence Bank, Birmingham, AL
(2011-present). Service at Covenant Theological Seminary (CTS): board
member, first elected in 2015. GA Service: Overtures Committee, MNA CoC.
Presbytery service: Church and Pastor Care, Admin, Credentials. Member of
Covenant PCA in Homewood, AL since 1992. Served as deacon and elder;
served on many committees, including Pastor Search Committee and Board of
Covenant Day School. Worked for AmSouth/Regions bank for 20 years before
joining Cadence Bank in 2011. Married with three children, one grandchild.

**TE Brian Cosby** : _Tennessee Valley_ . BA Samford; MDiv Beeson Divinity
School; PhD Australian College of Theology. Senior Pastor, Wayside

<a id="ga52-p418"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=420 printed_page=418 -->

Presbyterian Church. CTS Board member since 2022, serving on the
Advancement Committee. Adjunct Professor of Historical Theology at RTS
Atlanta and Reformation Bible College in Sanford, FL. Chaplain – US Air
Force Reserves. Author of 12 books, including _Giving Up Gimmicks:_
_Reclaiming Youth Ministry from an Entertainment Culture_ . Served two terms
on the GA’s Nominating Committee. Theological Examining Committee –
Tennessee Valley Presbytery.

**RE Scott Shidemantle** : _Ascension_ . MDiv Gordon-Conwell. Th.M. Princeton

Seminary. PhD TEDS (in NT, studying under DA Carson). Professor of
Biblical Studies and Bible Core Coordinator, Geneva College (1997-present).
Service at Covenant Theological Seminary (CTS): Board member since 2022,
member of Academic Affairs Committee, and CTS ambassador to Western
Pennsylvania. Other Presbytery, church service: Ascension Presbytery –
multiple terms as Moderator, multiple terms on Credentials Committee.
Faculty Representative to Geneva College Board. Chapel PCA of Beaver, PA

- Ruling Elder since 2008 and Clerk of Session. Married for 23 years with two
children.

**RE Lee Tyner** : _North Texas_ . Bachelor’s at University of Mississippi. J.D.
from UVA. General Counsel, TCU since 2018 (first to hold the position). RE
at Trinity PCA in Ft. Worth, TX. Previously served as RE at Christ PCA in
Oxford, MS. Other experience: TCU – University Leadership TeamButler
Snow firm; General Counsel at University of Mississippi (2003-2018). Served
with the SBC in Budapest, Hungary, for a year after college graduation.
Current board member – Reformed Youth Ministries Previous board member

of Palmer Home for Children. MS Joint Commission on Campus Work, 201114. Married with five children.

**RE Frank Wicks** : _Missouri_ .

Retired; former President of Applied Markets Business Unit for SigmaAldrich after 33 years with the company. CTS Board Member since 2005
(advisory board beginning in 2001); has served on Finance, Governance,
Trustee Development, and Executive Committees. Navigators, College
Ministry – five years at Oklahoma State, US Air Force Academy, and
University of Colorado. Helped start Good Shepherd PCA with Phil Douglass
(of CTS) in 1988 and became an inaugural ruling elder. Member, Chesterfield
PCA in Missouri. Married with 3 daughters and 12 grandchildren.

<a id="ga52-p419"></a>

**TE Michael Kelly:** _Pacific Northwest_ . BA Communication and Media Study,
St. Louis University; M.Div, Covenant Theological Seminary. Associate
Pastor, Trinity Church Seattle. Previously Lead Pastor of Green Lake
Presbyterian Church (now Trinity Church) (1995 - 2014). Executive Director
and Founder of the Northwest Church Planting Network (2000 - 2025).
Married for thirty-five years with three children and five grandchildren.

**TE Tim Rice:** _Southwest Florida_ . BA Rhetoric, Appalachian State University;
MDiv, RTS Orlando. Founding and Lead Pastor, Trinity Presbyterian Church,
Lakeland, Florida. Apprenticed fir additional church planters in greater
Lakeland area. RUF at Florida Souther College (1993 - 1996). Assessor at
PCA Assessment Center (2003 - 2025). Chartered Renew Polk, a greater
Lakeland church planning network, in 2009. Married for thirty-eight years
with four child and three grandchildren.

**RE Mark Kaiser:** _Wisconsin_ . BS, Chemical Engineering and MBA,
University of Wisconsin, Madison. CEO, President, and Owner of Lindquist
Machine Corporation (2000 - 2025). RE at Jacob’s Well, Green Bay,
Wisconsin. Member of multiple core group church plants in ARP and PCA.
Chair of Presbytery’s Mission/MNA Committee (2013) and involved in
developing the planting vision that resulted in the On Wisconsin Church
planting network. Previously NXTGEN Pastors Board member. Co-host Chair
for the 2027 GA in Milwaukee, WI. Married for forty-one years with four
children and nine grandchildren.

**RE William Taft:** _Central Indiana_ . B.S, History, Cedarville University; M.S.,
Historic Preservation, Ball State University. Senior Fellow at the Sagamore
Institute. Previously Managing Partner at Full Circle Housing Development;
Senior Vice President at Local Initiatives Support Corp; and President of
Southeast Neighborhood Development. RE at Redeemer Indianapolis. Led
core group that planted Redeemer and he’s served on other plants and mission

church commissions. Member of Presbytery’s Church Planting and Outreach

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Andrew Lewis:** _Calvary._ BA, Erksine College, MDiv Erskine Theological
Seminary _._ Has served in various leadership roles at Mitchell Road
Presbyterian Church in Greenville, SC since 1997, acting as lead teaching
pastor for since 2008. Has been involved with international mission trips for
decades and led Mitchell Road to focus on equipping leaders to plant churches
around the world (most recently in Athens, Greece). Currently working on his
PhD.

**RE Daryl Brister:** _Houston Metro._ President and Owner of Competency
Consultancy Solutions, 2014-present. Has served for 10 years on the
Permanent Committee on MTW. Has served for 20 years as area t christ
Church PCA in Katy, TX. Served on Houston Metro’s MTW Committee for
18 years, serving as chairman for 15 of those years. Currently serves on
Houston Metro’s Nominations Committee. Led numerous short-term mission

trips for Christ Church PCA.

**RE David Moore:** _Central Florida._ MBA, Rollins College-Crummer
Graduate School of Business. BS, Electrical Engineering, Auburn University.
30-year career with PFM Financial Advisors, where he went on to serve on the
Strategic Planning Committee, Board of Directors and Executive Committee.
Has served for 21 years as area t orange wood Church, serving as Treasurer,
school board member and Executive Director where he was responsible for
directing over $500,000 per year to various local and global missions. Served
as an alternate to the Permanent Committee on MTW in 2022 and 2024.

**TE Larry Trotter:** _South Florida._ PhD, Communication, Regent University,
2007. MDiv Westminster Theological Seminary, 1986. BS, Duke University,

<a id="ga52-p425"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=427 printed_page=425 -->

1983. Founding Pastor, Florida Gulf Coast Church, Pompano Beach, FL.
Founding Pastor, Iglesia Cristo Redentor, Guadalajara, Mexico, 1996-2016.
Pastor, Iglesia El Shaddai, Mexico City, 1990-1994. Served with MTWMexico from 1989-2016 as Country Director (2012-2016), Team
Leader/Church Planter, Guadalajara (1995-2012), Church Planter, Mexico
City (1989-1994). Founder and Instructor of Seminario Reformado,
Guadalajara (2007-2012). Currently Serves as Adjunct Professor of Practical
Theology, Knox Theological Seminary, (2018-present) and Online Adjunct
Professor of Practical Theology-Spanish, Westminster Theological Seminary
(2023-present).

**RE Charles Lokey:** _Northwest Georgia._ MSSc, International Relations,
Syracuse University. BS, Organizational Management, Covenant College.
Founder and President, Teaching Missions International, 2013-present. Serves
as a Board Member to Reformation Hope, a mission to Haiti, and Grenada
Institute for Theological Education. Serves as acting president of L’Académie
Théologique de la Réforme, a Bible college in Haiti. Committee Member of
Northwest Georgia Presbytery’s Missions Committee. Founded and served as
Executive Director of TechRomania, an organization created to provide a
business platform for missions in Southeast Europe from 2001-2004.

Westminster College. Practicing attorney since 1982. Pennsylvania and

Probate, Business, and Probate & Trust Law Sections. Geneva Benefits Group

University of Pittsburgh. Published, interviewed, and quoted in various
regional, professional, and Christian publications, including World Magazine.

Managing Director of Wachovia Securities and Senior Vice President of
Wachovia Bank, N.A., responsible for syndicated loan finance business and
currency risk management business.

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Robert Cunningham:** _Ohio Valley._ MDiv Covenant Theological
Seminary; PhD candidate, University of Leicester. Founder and Director of
Christ for Kentucky (2022-present); Senior Pastor of Tates Creek PC,
Lexington, KY (2011-2022). Led RUF staff training on human sexuality and
gender; speaker multiple occasions at RUF Summer Conference. Past
Moderator, OVP; current Shepherding Committee. Twice served on General
Assembly Overtures Committee. Led key legislative efforts in Kentucky in
favor of on-line pornography age verification and technology protections for
youth. Syndicated column in nine Kentucky newspapers; Every Square Inch
podcast with a listener base of over 350,000.

**RE Tyler Martin** : _Platte Valley_ . B.S Chemistry, University of NebraskaKearney, M.D. University of Nebraska, College of Medicine. RE at Redeemer
PC, Lincoln Nebraska. Currently CEO of 3 biotechnology companies: Great
Plains Biotechnology, Adjuvance Technologies and Aeolian Biotech. Former
RE Grace Silicon Valley, Palo Alto, CA; Leadership Development Committee
in Platte Valley. Previous service: Board of Trustees, Redeemer Theological
Seminary, Dallas, TX. School Board, including 6 years as Board President,
The King's Academy, Sunnyvale, CA.

**RE John Miller** : _Eastern Carolina_ . BS North Carolina State University. RE
Christ the King PC, Raleigh, NC. Current alternate on the RUF permanent
committee. Currently works in commercial real estate. RUF student member
at NC State; Lived in and then purchased a house for RUF students at NC
State, which continues to be a ministry resource for the RUF students there.

**TE Jack Howell** : _Tidewater_ . BA Vanderbilt University; MDiv Covenant
Theological Seminary. Organizing and Senior Pastor of Trinity PC, Norfolk,
VA. Three terms on RUF permanent committee; former chair of RUF
permanent committee; chairman of search committee to hire RUF Coordinator

<a id="ga52-p432"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=434 printed_page=432 -->

Will Huss. Founder and chairman for 20 years of Virginia's Joint Committee
for RUF, which started 8 RUF ministries. RUF student member at Vanderbilt.
Parent of RUF student members at UVa. Served several terms as moderator of

Tidewater Presbytery.

**RE Michael Martin** : _Blue Ridge_ . BA UVa; M.A. Regent. 20 years a sr eat
Trinity PC, Charlottesville, VA. CEO Crown Mortgage. Six years’ service on
RUF permanent committee. 15 years on Virginia Joint Committee for RUF.
Leadership Development Committee of Blue Ridge. Committee of
Commissioners for Geneva Benefits and Overtures Committee.

**D.** **Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Christopher Bechtel:** _Pacific Northwest._ PhD, Hebrew Bible, University
of Edinburgh, 2012. MDiv Covenant Theological Seminary, 2008. BA,
Covenant College, 2004. Senior Pastor, Evergreen Church, Salem, OR.
Visiting Professor of Old Testament, Covenant Theological Seminary.
Executive Board Member, Northwest Church Planting Network. Adjunct
Professor of Bible at Missio College (formerly named Reach Training
Institute), Salem, OR. Former Research Associate, Scottish Council on Human
Bioethics. Member of Pacific Northwest Presbytery Candidates and
Credentials Committee, 2014-present (Chairman of committee, 2019-present).

**RE Randy Scott:** _South Texas._ MBA, New Mexico State University. BS in
Computer Science, New Mexico State University. Retired, IBM. Has served
as a Ruling Elder since 2014 at Redeemer Presbyterian in Austin, TX (member
since 2002). Prior to Redeemer, was a member at University Presbyterian
Church in Las Cruces, NM, where he served as a Deacon. Has served since
2018 on South Texas Presbytery Credentials Committee. He and his wife of
36 years have 3 children.

**TE Bruce Baugus:** _Great Lakes._ PhD, Philosophical Theology, Calvin
Theological Seminary, 2009. M.Div, Southern Baptist Theological Seminary,
1999. BS, Penn State, 1996. Professor of Systematic Theology and
Apologetics and Director of ThM Program at Puritan Reformed Theological
Seminary. Associate Pastor, Christ Church PCA, Grand Rapids, MI. Served as
Professor of Philosophy and Theology from 2008-2022 at Reformed
Theological Seminary in Jackson, MS. Chair of Credentials Committee of
Great Lakes Presbytery. Served on Theological Examinations Committee in

<a id="ga52-p437"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=439 printed_page=437 -->

Mississippi Valley Presbytery from 2014-2024 (Chair of committee from
2019-2022). Served as Moderator of Mississippi Valley Presbytery, 2014.
Regularly teaches in Africa, Asia, and South America.

**RE Josh Spears:** _Hills and Plains._ MA, Philosophy, University of Oklahoma.
BA, Theology, Randall University. Serves as the current alternate for the
Theological Examining Committee. RE at City Presbyterian in Oklahoma City
for 7 years. Has taught Bible, theology and philosophy for over 25 years at
different private Christian schools as well as public universities around
Oklahoma City. Serves on Candidates and Credentials Committee of Hills and
Plains Presbytery.

**1) ADMINISTRATIVE COMMITTEE**

**Class of 2029**
**Nominating Committee Nominee** **Floor Nominee**
TE Robert Brunson, Central Georgia TE Ligon Duncan, MS Valley
RE Todd Carlisle, Evangel RE Melton Ledford Duncan, Calvary

**Alternates**

TE Brian Salter, Tennessee Valley TE Rob Pacienza, South Florida
RE Rick Owens, North Texas RE Rich Leino, James River

**Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Robert Brunson:** _Central Georgia_ . BA, Communications, University of
Alabama, 1979. M.Div. RTS 1986. Over his 37 years of ordained ministry,
Bob has worked as either a solo/senior Pastor (14 years) or Executive Pastor
(18 years) including his current role as Executive Pastor at North Macon
Presbyterian Church. Extensive experience in budget, financial, and longrange planning as well as compensation and benefit plan development,
personnel and volunteer management, database and new ministry
development, and facility/construction planning. Four time Presbytery
Moderator. Previous service on Ridge Haven Board (10 years) and on AC
Permanent Committee (12 years), several times as chairman.

**TE J. Ligon Duncan III:** _Mississippi Valley._ BA Furman; MDIV, MA CTS;
PHD Edinburgh. Professor of Systematic and Historical Theology, RTS

<a id="ga52-p441"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=443 printed_page=441 -->

Jackson, (1990-) Assistant Trinity Jackson (1990-1995), Interim Pastor, First
Yazoo (1993), Senior Minister First Jackson (1996-2013); Chancellor/CEO
Reformed Theological Seminary (2013-), Moderator Calvary Presbytery
(1996), GA Creation Study Committee (1998-2000), GA TEC Co Chairman
(2000-2001), Moderator Mississippi Valley Presbytery (2000-2001), PCAGA
Bills & Overtures Chairman (2002), Moderator 32nd PCA General Assembly
(2003), GA Study Committee on Federal Vision (2006-2007), Chairman MidSouth RUF (2011-2017), OPC Fraternal Delegate (2014), Convener Twin
Lakes Fellowship.

**RE Todd Carlisle:** _Evangel._ BS, Samford University, 1988. J.D. Samford,
1991. RE at Briarwood Presbyterian Church for the last 25 years. He works as
an attorney specializing in corporate and merger & acquisition law. Served on
the Boards of Campus Outreach Birmingham and Briarwood Christian School
and served more than 10 years on the Board of United Way of Central
Alabama. Todd has previously served a multi-year term on the AC.

**RE Melton L. Duncan:** _Calvary._ Attended Clemson, Ruling Elder Second
Presbyterian Church Greenville. He serves as SPC’s Church Administrator,
Chairman Building Program Committee. Stated Clerk (since 2016) & former
Moderator (2008, 2012, 2013) of Calvary Presbytery. Service on PCAGA
Committee of Commissioners: Overtures, Administrative, MTW, CE&P,
RUF, Thanks. Service on PCA Permanent Committees or Special Committees:
RUF, Nominating, RPR. Standing Judicial Commission (2019-2024). PCA
History Center Advisory member since 1992. Executive Council, Gospel
Reformation Network

**TE Brian Salter:** _Tennessee Valley._ BS, Education, University of Georgia,
1997. M.Div.RTS Orlando, 2006. On staff at Lookout Mountain Presbyterian
for the last 19 years. Senior Pastor for the last 7 years. Helped lead Lookout
Mountain and a Session of 34 men through a successful $18 million capital
campaign. He has served on various Presbytery committees including RUF,
Nominations and Presbytery's Leadership Team. Served on seven different GA
Committees of Commissioners as well as the Overtures Committee. Brian

recently coordinated efforts between AC and TVP to host GA in Chattanooga.

**TE Robert Pacienza:** _South Florida._ B.A., Samford University, 2003.
M.Div., Knox Theological Seminary, 2012. D.Min., Westminster Theological
Seminary, 23. Senior Pastor, Coral Ridge Presbyterian Church, Ft. Lauderdale,

<a id="ga52-p442"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=444 printed_page=442 -->

FL. CEO/President, Coral Ridge Ministries. Founder, Institute for Faith and
Culture. General Council, Gospel Reformation Network. Contributor, _World_
_Magazine_ & _The Washington Times_ . Board Member, National Religious
Broadcasters. Doctoral work focused upon equipping cultural-shaping
Christians. Served on presbytery education committee, and currently serving
on the Ministry and Church Relations Committee.

**RE Rick Owens:** _North Texas_ . BS, University of Georgia, 1970. Dallas
Theological Seminary, 1984. Navy War College, 1989. University of
Michigan Executive Business School, 1992. Retired Navy fighter pilot, 19711995 (active & reserve). Executive Officer of a Carrier Group; retired as O-6
Captain (full Colonel). Retired as an American Airlines pilot (1978-2008),
having served as Chief Pilot/Base Manager and Fleet Captain Check Airman.
Led Owens-Douglas Ltd Financial Management from 2009-2019. RE at Park
Cities Presbyterian, Dallas for the last 17 years. Chaired PCPC Missions,
Personnel, and Vision Committees. Chairman of Host Committee for the 47th
GA in Dallas.

**RE Rich Leino:** _James River._ B.S. Nuclear Engineering from RPI. M.S.
Electrical Engineering from the Naval Postgraduate School. M.S. Military
Studies from USMC Command and Staff. M.A. Christian Ministry from New
Geneva. Vice President of Growth at JST and SMX from 2011-2025, Marine
Corps Officer from 1990-2011 to include command during combat operations
and leadership for thousands of Marines leading complex enterprise
transformations. Nominating Committee (2012, 2013), Review of Presbytery
Records (2017-2018), Admin CoC (2011, 2022), Overtures (2012, 2013, 2016,
2017, 2019), MTW CoC (2020), Theological Examinations Committee (20192023, three years as Chair), current Alternate at the Administrative Committee
(2025).

**2) COMMITTEE ON CONSTITUTIONAL BUSINESS**

**Class of 2029**
**Nominating Committee Nominee** **Floor Nominee**
RE Joshua Torrey, South Texas RE Matt Fender, James River

**Alternates**

TE Randy Schlichting, Metro Atlanta TE Sean Morris, Tennessee Valley

<a id="ga52-p443"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=445 printed_page=443 -->

**Biographical Sketches:**

**RE Joshua Torrey:** _South Texas_ . BS, Electrical Engineering, University of
Texas at San Antonio. Has worked as a Computer Chip Engineer for 10 years
at Advanced Micro Devices and 7 at Apple. RE at Redeemer Presbyterian,
Austin, TX, where he and his family have been members for 11 years. Has
served on several Presbytery committees and is the current Moderator. Helped
rewrite Presbytery standing rules. served on ga committees of
Commissioners each year he's been at GA. re alternate on ga theological
Examining Committee in 2023. Served last year as the RE Alternate on the
CCB.

**RE Matt Fender:** _James River._ Matt is a Ruling Elder at All Saints Reformed
Presbyterian Church in Richmond, VA. He holds a JD from the University of
Virginia and a BA in Political Science from Virginia Tech. he is licensed to
practice law in Virginia, New York, and texas an dis a partner ina large law
firm. Matt is the outgoing chairman of the Committee on Constitutional
Business (“CCB”), to which he was first elected in 2021. His past GA service
includes serving on the Overtures Committee in 2018, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024,
and 2025 a swell a sr pr in 2023, 2024, and 2025.

**TE Randy Schlichting:** _Metro Atlanta._ BBA, University of Houston. MA,
International Business, University of Texas-Dallas. M.Div. Metro Atlanta
Seminary. D.Min. Birmingham Theological Seminary. Randy worked in
business for 15 years, mainly in hospital administration in the Middle East and
US. He's been in the PCA for 36 years, serving at Perimeter Church since 1997.
heist heir pastor of Shepherding. He's been MAP's Clerk since its formation
in 2012. He has attended the last 16 GAs, serving on many CoCs and as
Parliamentarian in 2022 and 2024. He's also on the Board of the Association

of Reformed Theological Seminaries.

**TE Sean Morris:** _Tennessee Valley._ Sean serves as an Associate Pastor at
Covenant Presbyterian Church in Oak Ridge, Tennessee. he is a graduate of
Grove City College (BA), RTS-Jackson (MDiv), University of Glasgow
(MTh), and Puritan Reformed Theological Seminary (PhD).While in Blue
Ridge Presbytery, Morris served two consecutive years as moderator of the
presbytery and served on the Leadership Development Committee. While in
TN Valley, Morris served two years on the PCA’s Review of Presbytery

<a id="ga52-p444"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=446 printed_page=444 -->

Records committee and currently serves as the chairman of the presbytery’s
Theological Examination Committee. Sean and his wife, Sarah, live with their
children in Oak Ridge, TN.

**TE James Baird:** Suncoast Florida. Pastor of Christian education at Covenant
Church of Naples. he i san alumnus of covenant College, Westminster

of Business and Technology. he served as a director at Westminster, Ligonier,
and Synthesis (the school originating on the campus of SpaceX). He and his

and Griffin.

**TE Joe Christman:** Chicago Metro. B. S. Business, University of Illinois at
Chicago, 2014. MDiv, Trinity Evangelical Divinity School, 2017. Ordained as
a Teaching Elder in 2017. Pastor of Redeemer Presbyterian Church, Lombard
IL. Has served the Chicago Metro Presbytery as Moderator, Vice Moderator,

Has served on several Committees of Commissioners (Overtures, Admin,

**4) COMMITTEE ON DISCIPLESHIP MINISTRIES**

**Class of 2030**
**Nominating Committee Nominee** **Floor Nominee**
TE Timothy LeCroy, New York TE George Grant, Nashville
TE Jonathan Medlock, N. California TE Steven Spinnenwebber, N Florida
Vacancy RE Mark M. Hogewood, Evangel

**Alternate**

Vacancy RE Stuart McAllister, GA Foothills
RE Kevin Mobley, Calvary

**Biographical Sketches:**

**TE Tim LeCroy:** _New York State._ BS, Chemical Engineering, NC State 2001;
MDiv CTS 2006; PhD Historical Theology, St. Louis University, 2012. Pastor
of New Life PCA, Ithaca, NY. Visiting Instructor of Historical Theology at
Covenant Seminary. Discipled children at Seneca Hills Bible Camp in Western
PA the last 4 summers. Chairman of the DASA ad interim committee. PCA

service: AC Permanent Committee, (chaired the Planning and Management
subcommittee). Commissioner to 14 GAs, serving on many CoCs. Served on
RPR, Nom Com, and Overtures. Presbytery moderator, vice moderator,
parliamentarian, chairman of Credentials, and served on Admin and RUF
committees.

**TE George Grant:** _Nashville_ . B.A. Political Science, Univ. of Houston; M.A.,
D.Litt., Ph.D., Whitefield Theological Seminary; D.Hum. Humanities,
Belhaven College; D.Min. Cand. Theology, Knox Theological Seminary;
Pastor Emeritus, Parish Presbyterian Church, Franklin, TN; _prev._ Moderator,
Nashville Presbytery; Dir., King’s Meadow Study Center; Founder, Franklin
Classical School & Bannockburn College; General Council, Gospel
Reformation Network; married to Karen, with three children and many
grandchildren. Dr. Grant is a prolific author, having penned dozens of books
and hundreds of articles on pastoral theology, education, and more. He is
active in PCA church-planting efforts regionally and internationally (esp. in
the Muslim world).

**TE Jonathon Medlock:** _Northern California_ . BA, Oglethorpe University
1992; JD Samford University 1995; M.Div. CTS 2005; Served the local church

<a id="ga52-p446"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=448 printed_page=446 -->

as assistant and senior pastors for 15 years prior to serving as VP of People
and Culture for Geneva Benefits, where he serves pastors and their families in
the PCA. Prior he served on the Board of Directors for RBI for 10 years and
was chairman for 5. Current PhD candidate in Educational Studies at Trinity
Evangelical Divinity School.

**TE Stephen Spinnenweber:** _North Florida._ Senior Minister of Westminster
Presbyterian Church in Jacksonville, FL (M.Div, GPTS). Presbytery Service:
Moderator (2023, 2024), Candidates and Credentials Committee Member
(2023-), Chairman (2025-), Chairman of Under Care/Interns Committee
(2020-2023). General Assembly Service: Overtures (2021, 2023, 2025). RUF
CoC (2024). Admin CoC (2022). Nominating Committee (2023). Vocational
Experience: Published author. Short form writings can be found at Ref21,
Heidelblog, Gospel Reformation Network, and Ligonier magazine. Hosts
podcasts on the Westminster Standards. Speaker at the inaugural Millennial
Forum hosted before 2024 GA. Speaker at forthcoming MNA Millennial
Summit (Oct, 2025).

**RE Mark Hogewood:** _Evangel._ member of covenant pc in Birmingham, AL.
He is a graduate of Wake Forest and Vanderbilt Law School and is currently
an attorney in Birmingham. RE Hogewood has been a Bible study leader and
an active volunteer in the children’s ministry of Covenant PC. He regularly
teaches the communicants class. He is a former member of the Vestavia Board

of Education, as well as a former board member of Restoration Academy, a
Christian school for predominantly lower income families of Fairfield, AL.

**RE Stuart McAllister:** _Georgia Foothills._ Member of Restoration PCA in
Dacula, GA (Georgia Foothills Presbytery). He is married and has two adult
children who are also married. Since becoming a Christian in his home country
of Scotland in 1977, RE McAllister has traveled the globe, first smuggling
Bibles and Christian resources behind the Iron Curtain. RE McAllister’s

experience in adult discipleship ministry and apologetics will make him a
helpful resource to CDM.

**RE Kevin M. Mobley:** _Calvary._ BS Life; Biblical Counseling Certificate
CCEF, Health Advisory Board North Greenville University. SPC Greenville
Administration Committee Chairman since 2017, A Time to Build Committee,
Sunday School Teacher, Trustee. Calvary Presbytery Administration

<a id="ga52-p447"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=449 printed_page=447 -->

Committee Chairman, Moderator Calvary Presbytery 2024. Dr. Mobley has
been married for 25 years, and they have been blessed with twelve children.

**Appendix A**

**AMENDMENT TO NOMINATING COMMITTEE**

**MANUAL OF OPERATIONS**

**SUBCOMMITTEE OF**

**TE FRED GRECO, RE FRANK COHEE,**

**AND RE HOWIE DONAHOE**

**June 9, 2025**

Part II. The Nominating Process, Section B, paragraph 3, NC Preliminary
Vote Forms is amended as follows (insertions indicated by underlining and
deletions by strikethrough):

NC Preliminary Vote Forms. A form will be designed each year by
the Office of the PCA Stated Clerk, in consultation with the officers
of the NC, on which to tally the preliminary votes of the members of
the NC. This preliminary vote tally will show only the raw numbers
of votes per place (e.g. 1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.). This preliminary vote tally
will be used to set the initial motion for each committee and agency at
the in-person meeting according to highest total raw votes for
candidates in preliminary voting.

**RATIONALE**

This amendment will establish clear criteria for setting the initial motion for
the slate of candidates for each committee and agency. Further, the initial slate
will be in accordance with the total number of raw votes received by each
potential nominee.

Here's an example. Let's say there were 4 TE slots for the XYZ Board Class
of 2030. And the vote distribution from the April preliminary ballots filed by
Nominating Committee members came as shown below (names listed
alphabetically, as they appear in the Rainbow Book). Each of the 80
Committee members voted, each casting 4 votes for the XYZ Class of 2030
for a total of 320 raw votes.

1 [st] place 2 [nd] place 3 [rd] place 4 [th] place Total
Thomas Brooks 5 5 10 10 30

John Calvin 35 30 10 15 90
Stephen Charnock 0 5 5 10 20

With the preliminary ballot totals shown above, the main, initial motion the
Nominating Committee would consider would be: "To nominate TEs Calvin,
Hodge, Edwards and Knox to the XYZ Class of 2030." Then, anyone could
move to substitute Charnock for Knox, and present argument for why that
should be done. Etc.
