**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-08: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 40-3; _BCO_ 13-9.b) — Results
of review of records of church sessions not stated.

**Response:** Results of the review of church records
were provided to Presbytery but mistakenly were not included
in the minutes. This will be corrected and the Presbytery
promises to be more careful in the future.
**2024-01: 28 Jan, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.
**Response:** The candidate was in fact examined in
PCA history; however, Presbytery mistakenly omitted
recording it in the minutes. Presbytery regrets the error and
promises to be more careful in the future. The minutes will be
corrected accordingly.
**2024-02: 28 Jan, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-5) — te included on roll of
Presbytery without explanation.
**Response:** The TE was hired by the church to serve
in a non-pastoral role on the staff. The reason Presbytery
received him "without call" was to enable him to be active ni

Presbytery, with the desire for a pastoral call ni the future. The
Presbytery minutes wil be amended to include the reason for
receiving him "without call." The Presbytery promises to be
more careful in the future.

**2024-03: 28 Jan, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes
of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes, and these
must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** Thecommission minutes were presented
and approved by the Presbytery and included with the

<a id="ga52-p528"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=530 printed_page=528 -->

Presbytery minutes as an attachment. However, they were
mistakenly omitted from the minutes submitted to RPR. The
commission minutes are attached. Presbytery promises to be
more careful in the future.

**2024-04: May 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of sixmonths membership for candidate.
**Response:** Leazer and Skillingstad have both met the
6-month membership requirement; however, Presbytery
concurs it mistakely omitted recording it in theminutes.
Presbytery promises to be more attentive in the future. The
minutes will be corrected accordingly
**2024-05: 28 Jan, 2023** (PP 6) — No record that members of
the provisional Session were called by the congregation.
**Response:** Presbytery confirms that the provisional
(temporary) session was not called by the congregation. The
church had particularized on October 4, 2020, with a session
comprised of a pastor and two Ruling Elders. As of the
January 28, 2023 Presbytery meeting, both of the Ruling
Elders had resigned and left the church. Since there were no
Ruling Elders, Presbytery’s judgment was that the church
reverted back to mission status. Therefore, per _BCO_ 5-3,
Presbytery appointed a temporary session.
Presbytery accepts RPR’s judgment and promises to
be more careful in the future. For corrective action, the
Presbytery will advise the church membership regarding a
congregational meeting and proceed accordingly.
**2024-06: 28 Jan, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
congregation concurred with dissolution of pastoral relation.
**Response:** The congregational meeting minutes
include concurrence that the congregation approved the
dissolution of the pastoral relation. The Presbytery minutes
have been amended to include the concurrence of the

congregation. The Presbytery promises to be more careful in
the future.

**2024-07: 28 Jan, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that Session
concurred with dissolution of pastoral relation.
**Response:** The session minutes provided
concurrence with the resignation and dissolution of the
pastoral relationship. The minutes have been amended to

<a id="ga52-p529"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=531 printed_page=529 -->

correct this omission.The Presbytery promises to be more
careful in the future.

**2024-09: May 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
congregation concurred with dissolution of pastoral relation.
**Response:** The minutes of the congregational
meeting reflected concurrence of the pastoral dissolution.The
minutes have been amended to correct this omission. The

Presbytery promises to be more careful in the future.
**2024-10: Sep 12, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes
of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes, and these
must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** The minutes of the commission were
approved by the Presbytery. However, they were mistakenly
not included in the report to the RPR.The minutes of the
commission are attached to this report. The Presbytery
promises to be more careful in the future.

_on Central Georgia Presbytery because one of the debated exceptions raised_

_Commission in a case from another Presbytery._

11. That the Minutes of **Chesapeake Presbytery:** _53-6-3_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **None.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **Jan 20, 2024; Sep 10,**

**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 19-7; _BCO_ 19-8; _BCO_ 19-9;
19-10) — Internships are established for men at the July,
September, and November meetings, but there is no record of
the specifics of the internships or of the required exams, and
no evidence that the required record was made. Further, in 3
of the cases there is no record in the minutes or the Directory
that the men were candidates.
**2025-02: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 19-12) — No reports on interns
at each stated meeting.
**2025-03: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 8-7; _BCO_ 13-2) — No annual
reports for TEs without call and missing reports for TEs who
are serving out of bounds.
**2025-04: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 40) — Of the 36 churches in
Presbytery, 11 have no mention of any review of their minutes
and 10 have evidence of reviews of prior year's minutes, but
not of 2023 minutes. Further, there is no record that
Presbytery required responses to exceptions of substance.
**2025-05: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-5; _BCO_ 46-6; _BCO_ 46-7)
— TEs are transferred to other PCA presbyteries with no
record (in one case) of a call or other reason for the transfer,
and no evidence that the action is pending the action of the
other court, that a certificate of dismission was issued, or,
from subsequent minutes, that the man transferred at the
September meeting was received by the other presbytery.
**2025-06: Mar 12, 2024** ( _BCO_ 46-6; _BCO_ 46-7) — A TE is
received from another PCA Presbytery with the understanding

<a id="ga52-p531"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=533 printed_page=531 -->

that the other Presbytery will issue a certificate of transfer.
There is no record in subsequent minutes that the transfer
certificate was ever received, and the TE was installed without
reference to the certificate having been received.
**2025-07: Mar 12, 2024** ( _BCO_ 32; _BCO_ 31-2; _BCO_ 34; _BCO_
38-1; _RONR_ (12th ed) 48:14, 35:13) — Presbytery votes to
"strike from the minutes of its 86th Meeting of the Presbytery

[which would have been over 3 years prior] where it
erroneously records that TE [Name omitted] was suspended
from the sacraments." Presbytery then votes to instruct the
Stated Clerk to "communicate the judgment of CPJC" to the
TE, which judgment was the censure of deposition for the sin
of failing to manage his household well. There is no record
of the 2/3 vote needed to amend previous minutes. Further,
there is no record of any formal process, of a case without
process, or of any report as to how this matter came about. It
is not clear if this is a new censure or if it is imposing what
Presbytery intended to impose at the 86th meeting. Either is
constitutionally dubious at this point.
**2025-08: Mar 12, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-5; _BCO_ 15; _BCO_ 21-6; _BCO_
21-9) — Two installation commission reports are attached to
the minutes. The first does not show attendance, the order of
service, or most of the required elements of _BCO_ 21-5. The
other omits all the elements of the first paragraph of 21-5.
Further, the second report sometimes refers to the TE as being
installed as an Associate Pastor and sometimes as an Assistant

Pastor.

**2025-09: May 14, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-2; _BCO_ 22-5; _BCO_ 22-6)
— A TE from some other body (it is not specified whether he
is from another PCA Presbytery) is examined for licensure to
serve as stated supply at a church. There is no record the
Session made a request for stated supply or that the other body
consented to have the TE serve within the bounds of

Chesapeake Presbytery.
**2025-10: May 14, 2024** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that the
congregation was cited to appear before Presbytery in a matter
of the dissolution of a pastoral call.
**2025-11: Nov 9, 2024** ( _BCO_ 18-6; _BCO_ 19-12) — There are
what appear to be annual reports for some candidates and

<a id="ga52-p532"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=534 printed_page=532 -->

interns, but many of the names do not match the names in the
Directory and vice versa, and it is clear that there were not
annual reports for all candidates and interns. Further, these is
no record of reports from seminary instructors for candidates
still in seminary.
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-01: Mar 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2.f; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Presbytery approved a candidate for licensure whom they
judged held a view that was hostile to the system or striking
at the vitals.

**Response:** We appreciate the RPR's diligence and
recognize Presbytery's action was irregular, though we
believed it to be appropriate in this case. We would offer the
following rationale as to why we made this decision: 1) The
candidate was already serving as a pastoral intern at one of
our churches and was seeking to fulfill all of the formal
internship requirements of _BCO_ 19-7ff, which includes the
testing of his preaching and teaching gifts. In order for the
intern to do this adequately, we believed licensure would be
necessary. 2) At our Presbytery meeting in March, members
of the Session where the candidate was serving as a pastoral
intern made clear that they agreed with Presbytery with
respect to the assessment of the candidate's particular view on
remarriage, and that they would be working with the candidate
to encourage him to reconsider his view in light of Scripture
and the standards and that they would ensure he not teach or
preach this view to the congregation. 3) The candidate seemed
humble and teachable and so we believed giving some
additional time to reconsider the view while working closely
with the Session of the church where he was serving and
fulfilling his internship requirements would be the kindest and
most reasonable approach. 4) At the end of the examination
on the floor of Presbytery, it was made clear to the candidate
by the chair of the Leadership Development Committee that
unless the candidate's view changed to be in accord with our
system of doctrine, he would not be able to be ordained in our
Presbytery. 5) As noted in the minutes, we also instructed the

<a id="ga52-p533"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=535 printed_page=533 -->

candidate that he "not teach, preach, or counsel his exception
to the standards."
**Rationale:** _BCO_ 19-2.f is explicit that “The court
may grant an exception to any difference of doctrine ONLY
if in the court’s judgment the applicant’s declared difference
is not out of accord with any fundamental of our system of
doctrine because the difference is neither hostile to the system
nor strikes at the vitals of religion.” (emphasis added) This
language does not give Presbytery the authority to grant an
exception that is hostile to the system of doctrine or strikes at
the vitals, even if Presbytery believes there are special
circumstances.

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2022-02: Aug 18, 2021** ( _BCO_ 21-4.f; 40-2) — Presbytery
granted a doctrinal exception that may be hostile to our system
of doctrine. More information or clarity is needed on the
exception, wherein the candidate expresses his belief that
images of Christ may be suitable “for purposes of instruction
and (with great care) **public worship** ” (emphasis added).
**Response:** [2023] The candidate was referring to
depictions of Jesus in such instances as instructional material
and Christmas pageants; not to iconography, crucifixes, or
other images that are venerated in other religious traditions.
Hence, we judged his view to be not hostile to our system of
doctrine.
**Rationale:** The response does not explain how use
of images of Christ might be appropriately used in public
worship.
**Response:** The individual has since revised his stated
difference to remove the words "and (with great care) public
worship."

<a id="ga52-p535"></a>

**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-11: Aug 16, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) —
Minutes of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes,
and these must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** Presbytery acknowledges the error and
has included the minutes with this response. Upon review of
the minutes, we noted the commission to ordain and install
Michael Fenimore did have two teaching elders, but one was
from another presbytery. It was not clear to the commission
from The Book of Church Order that a teaching elder from
another Presbytery could not property be counted in the
quorum of a commission, and Presbytery did not catch the
error until after the installation service. Presbytery will be sure
to include minutes in our record in the future and to make sure

that all commission members counted in the quorum are in
this Presbytery.
**Rationale:** Presbytery needs to ratify the actions of
the incomplete commission.

<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=540 printed_page=538 -->

13. That the Minutes of **Covenant Presbytery:** _59-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024; Feb 6, 2024;**
**Oct 1, 2024; Nov 4, 2024 (ES); Nov 19, 2024 (ES).**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **May 21, 2024.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance: **None.**
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-03: Oct 3, 2023** ( _BCO_ 34-10) — No record of 2/3 vote
to divest TE.

**Response:** Covenant Presbytery respectfully
disagrees that it erred in this citation. RPR alleges there was
no record of 2/3 vote to divest a TE. However, the cited
portion of the minutes by RPR, Covenant Presbytery minutes
159-11.8, begins “The motion was seconded and unanimously
passed (emphasis added), to divest [named TE] from the
office of TE in accordance with _BCO_ 34-10.” A unanimous

vote surpasses a 2/3 threshold and was rightly noted in the
minutes.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-01: Feb 7, 2023** ( _BCO_ 38-3) — No record that upon an
attempt of a PCA TE to transfer to the PC(USA) that
Presbytery judged whether or not the PC(USA) is failing to
maintain the Word and Sacraments in their fundamental

integrity.
**Response:** Covenant Presbytery respectfully
disagrees that it erred in this citation. _BCO_ 38-3.a and _BCO_
38.3.b are taken in concert with one another. _BCO_ 38.3.a says,
in part,”if at the time of the attempt to withdraw he is in good
standing, the irregularity shall be recorded, and his new
membership acknowledged.” _BCO_ 38.3.b says in part, “when
a member or minister of the Presbyterian Church in America
shall attempt to withdraw from the communion of this branch
of the visible Church by affiliating with a body judged by the
court of original jurisdiction as failing to maintain the Word
and Sacraments in their fundamental integrity…”. The
acknowledgement of the irregularity (noted in the minutes as
“dismissed to” [note not “transferred”]” and his new

<a id="ga52-p539"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=541 printed_page=539 -->

membership recorded in the minutes fulfills the requirement
of _BCO_ 38-3.a. Committee members may disagree with the
conclusion o fa pres byte r yon a certain matter, but that is not
a reason fora citation of substance.

**Rationale:** A determination must be made whether

the new ecclesiastical body is a branch of the visible church,
permitting irregular removal ( _BCO_ 38-3.a.), or whether the
new ecclesiastical body is a body failing to maintain the Word
and Sacraments in their fundamental integrity in order to warn
the TE of the danger of such attempted affiliation ( _BCO_ 383.b.).
**2024-02: May 16, 2023** ( _BCO_ 43-8) — A complaint may not
be dismissed, and the Presbytery “shall hear the complaint,”
unless there is evidence that the complainant currently does
not have standing or has withdrawn or abandoned his
complaint.
**Response:** Covenant Presbytery respectfully
disagrees that it erred in this citation. RPR notes “A complaint
may not be dismissed, and the Presbytery “shall hear the
complaint,” unless there is evidence that the complainant
currently does not have standing or has withdrawn or
abandoned his complaint.” The citation references Covenant
Presbytery minutes 158-11.2. Covenant Presbytery minutes
158-11.2 explicitly state that the Complainant “no longer has
standing to prosecute this Complaint” and notes that the
Complainant and family had joined another church outside of
the PCA. RPR may take issue with the language of
“dismissed”, but that would be notation of form not substance.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
actions ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). The complainant was still a member
of the PCA with standing to prosecute a complaint when the
complaint was dismissed without a hearing, and Presbytery
has not rescinded the unconstitutional denial of the complaint.
Once the unconstitutional action is rescinded,
acknowledgement of judicial mootness would be appropriate.

**a.** Be approved without exception: **Oct 25–26, 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **None.**

<a id="ga52-p540"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=542 printed_page=540 -->

**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record of annual
report of TE laboring out of bounds.
**2025-02: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-2) — No record of annual
report of TEs without calls. .
**2025-03: Feb 23, 2024** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record of 4/5
congregational vote for associate pastor to succeed former

**2025-04: May 31, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-11; _RAO_ 16-3.e.7.) —

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-03: Apr 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of
endorsement by candidate’s Session.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception: at
the time Mr. [Name Omitted] came under care, he was not a
member of any PCA church (and therefore did not receive a
letter of endorsement from a PCA session). Since that date, he
has become a member of [Church Name Omitted] Toronto
(PCA). Presbytery will obtain a letter of recommendation
from Mr. [Name Omitted]’s Session and commits to be more
careful in future.
**2024-05: Feb 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — Extraordinary clause
“invoked” to waive six-month membership of a candidate
without a vote by presbytery.
**Response:** Presbytery is unsure how to respond to the
exception. The wording appears to be identical to exception
2024-4, which references page 7 of the records. However,
2024-5 references page 17 of the records, and page 17 does
not mention a candidate coming under care, or Presbytery
invoking an extraordinary clause.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2023-03: Jun 3, 2022** ( _BCO_ 18) — Potential candidate not a
member of PCA church; presbytery voted to “waive”
requirement, but _BCO_ 18 makes no such provision for
waiving this requirement.

<a id="ga52-p541"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=543 printed_page=541 -->

**Response:** We agree with the exception. Our
intention was to invoke the extraordinary circumstance
permitted in _BCO_ 18-2: an applicant for care shall be a
member of the congregation for at least six-months ... “except
in those cases deemed extraordinary by the Presbytery.” The
Presbytery judged this to be an extraordinary case for the
following reasons: 1) Mr. [Name Omitted] is pastoring his
own church full time, making it difficult to attend or become
a member of ap ca church, 2) Mr. [Name Omitted] had been
undergoing supervision and training by the pastors of [Church
Name Omitted]?}, and 3) Mr. [Name Omitted] desires to
bring his church into the PCA. We will correct the minutes as
follows: “MSC to waive the 6-month membership
requirement by invoking the extraordinary clause of _BCO_ 182 for the following reasons: 1) Mr. [Name Omitted] is
pastoring his own church full time, making it difficult to
attend or become a member of ap ca church, 2) Mr. [Name
Omitted] had been undergoing supervision and training by the
pastors of [Church Name Omitted], and 3) Mr. [Name
Omitted] desires to bring his church into the PCA.”
**Rationale:** The situation presented still does not
resolve the issue that there is no provision in _BCO_ 18 for
approving a candidate that i snot a member oft hep ca (i.e.,
“the Church” in _BCO_ 18-1). The “extraordinary clause” is not
applicable to cases of membership in the Church in full
communion.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
resolves to correct its action by removing Mr. [Name Omitted]
from the roll of candidates under care because Mr. [Name
Omitted] is not a member of any church in the PCA.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
action or record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1) Presbytery has not recorded
removal of candidate from the roll of candidates.

**2023-04: Jun 3, 2022** ( _BCO_ 8-7; 20-1) — No record that the
Presbytery is assured that an out-of-bounds TE will have full
freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our Church;
pres byte ry approved a call not from a church, Presbytery, or
General Assembly without making “a record of the reasons
why it considers the work to be a valid Christian ministry.

<a id="ga52-p542"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=544 printed_page=542 -->

**Response:** We agree with the exception. The
Presbytery judged that a professorship of ministry at

[University Name Omitted] is a valid call to gospel ministry,
considering that he will be equipping and teaching students
for ministry in the church. We will correct the minutes as
follows: “MSC that the Presbytery accept the terms of call for
TE [Name Omitted] to serve as Assistant Professor of
Ministry at [University Name Omitted] because a
professorship of ministry at [University Name Omitted] is a
valid call to gospel ministry, considering that he will be
equipping and teaching students for ministry in the church
( _BCO_ 8-7, 20-1).”
**Rationale:** Presbytery's response fulfills the
requirements of _BCO_ 20-1, as Presbytery judged this to be a
valid call. However, Presbytery's response fails to fulfill the
requirements of _BCO_ 8-7, as the response does not indicate
that it received assurance that an out-of-bounds TE will have

full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our Church.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
resolves to correct its action by obtaining a letter from

[University Name Omitted] stating that TE [Name Omitted]
will have full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of

our Church ( _BCO_ 8-7).
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
action or record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not recorded
receipt of the letter assuring full freedom.
**2024-01: Feb 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of
endorsement by candidate’s Session.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
resolves to correct its action by obtaining a letter of
endorsement from the Session overseeing Mr. [Name
Omitted].
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
action or record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not recorded
receipt of letter of endorsement from Session.
**2024-02: Feb 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1) — No record of quorum
for meeting of a commission.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
resolves to correct its action by empowering and directing the

<a id="ga52-p543"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=545 printed_page=543 -->

C&C Committee to meet as a commission again, with
quorum, in order to examine Rev. [Name Omitted] for
transfer.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
action or record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not recorded
action of either Presbytery or a commission (with quorum),
examined or ratified transfer examination of TE.

**2024-04: Feb 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — Extraordinary clause
“invoked” to waive six-month membership of a candidate
without a vote by presbytery.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception: at
the time Mr. [Name Omitted] came under care, he was not a
member of any PCA church. Since that date, he has become a
member of [Church Name Omitted] (PCA). Presbytery will
obtain a letter of recommendation from Mr. [Name Omitted]’s
Session (per exception 2024-1) and commits to be more
careful in future.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
action or record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1).
**2024-06: Jun 2, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.c) — Extraordinary clause
“invoked” to waive theological and exegetical papers without
a vote by presbytery.
**Response:** Presbytery respectfully disagrees with the
exception. The checklist for reception of a minister from
another denomination (041-A) does not reference theological
or exegetical papers. Therefore, we believe these papers are
not required based on the PCA Administrative Committee
guidelines and our precedent of receiving ordained ministers
from other denominations.
**Rationale:** _BCO_ 13-6; reception of ordained
ministers from other denominations requires all the trials for
ordination requirements of _BCO_ 21-4.c(2), and (3), except in
the limited use of the extraordinary clause ( _BCO_ 21-4.i.), by
3/4 majority vote of Presbytery. Presbytery has not recorded
appropriate supermajority action of Presbytery vote to waive
the theological and exegetical papers.

<a id="ga52-p544"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=546 printed_page=544 -->

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**

**2023-02: Feb 12, 2022** ( _BCO_ 19-1) — All specific
requirements of licensure exam not recorded.
**Response:** We regret the failure to report the
fulfillment of this requirement. Mr. [Name Omitted] did
indeed preach a sermon approved by the elders at [Church
Name Omitted], [Name Omitted] in fulfillment of the
requirements for his licensure. We will be more diligent about
recording these things in the future. In addition, the minutes
of Feb. 12, 2022 will be amended to reflect that a sermon had
been preached

<a id="ga52-p545"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=547 printed_page=545 -->

**Rationale:** Licentiate sermons must be preached
before presbytery or before a committee of presbytery ( _BCO_
19-2.d) and approved by presbytery action, not session action.
**Response:** Please forgive the lack of clarity in the
previous response. The Session of [Church Name Omitted]
acted as a committee of Presbytery. As such they heard,
reviewed, and recommended to the Presbytery the approval of
Mr. [Name Omitted]'s sermon. The minutes will be amended
to specify this.
**2024-03: General 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-10.a) — No record in
minutes of approval of response to GA for 2022 citation.
**Response:** We respectfully disagree with this
exception. The following motion appears in our 2/11 /23
minutes under the report of the Stated Clerk (p. 34 ): "MSC to
approve the responses to RPR regarding exceptions of
substance to the 2022 minutes of presbytery, and to take the
actions stated in the responses."
**2024-01: Sep 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 38-3) — No record that
presbytery made determinations required by _BCO_ 38-3.
**Response:** We regret the oversight. TE [Name
Omitted] was removed from the rolls according to _BCO_ 383a. The minutes will be amended to reflect this, and care will
be taken in the future to specify the precise action taken.
**2024-02: Sep 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 38-3) — No record that
presbytery made determination required by _BCO_ 38-3.
**Response:** We regret the oversight. TE [Name
Omitted] was removed from the rolls according to _BCO_ 383a. The minutes will be amended to reflect this, and care will
be taken in the future to specify the precise action taken.

17. That the Minutes of **Evangel Presbytery:** _63-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **Feb 13, 2024; Aug 13, 2024;**
**Nov 12, 2024; Nov 12, 2024 (ES).**

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-01: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
Congregation or Session concurred with dissolutions of
pastoral relations.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
will amend our practice to include this in the future. Our
Church and Pastor Care Committee confirmed church/session

concurrence with dissolution but did not report that to the
presbytery. The presbytery approved the following motion at
its November 2024 meeting, “That Presbytery acknowledges
the following churches/sessions concurred with the
dissolution of pastoral relations. The Presbytery failed to
include these in previous minutes. [List of TE's and Church's
Names Omitted]
**2024-02: May 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes
of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes, and these
must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees and will include those
minutes.

**2024-03: Nov 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.b) — No record of
requiring licentiate to indicate whether he has changed his
previous views.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with this exception and
will amend our practice to include it in the future. The
presbytery committee confirmed with the licentiates that their
views had not changed in the relatively short time between
their licensure exams and their ordination exams, but the
presbytery neglected to include them in the minutes. The
presbytery approved the following motion at its November
2024 meeting, “That Presbytery note in the minutes that

[Name Omitted]’ views did not change between his licensure
at the 177th Stated Meeting and his ordination at the 179th
Stated Meeting. Further, the minutes note that [Name
Omitted]’s views did not change between his licensure at the
178th Stated Meeting and his ordination at the 179th Stated
Meeting.”

<a id="ga52-p547"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=549 printed_page=547 -->

why the resignation of a TE should be accepted.

requires the citing of the church to appear by its

169-11], wherein requests were made for Presbytery to
dissolve the pastoral calls of TE [Name Omitted] (Pastor), are

In regard to the exception on judging stated differences on p.

<a id="ga52-p550"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=552 printed_page=550 -->

136, it is not clear whether this applies to the licensure
examinations of TE [Name Omitted], TE [Name Omitted], or
Mr. [Name Omitted]: 1. TE [Name Omitted]’s numerous
differences were listed [p. 137-140] and the Examining
Committee, acting as a Commission under our Standing
Rules, found that for “the special needs of the Church and for
purposes of this Licensure Exam only, these differences were
deemed more than semantic, but not out of accord with any
fundamental.” [p. 136] 2. TE [Name Omitted] stated “no
differences” with the Westminster Standards. [p. 135] 3. Mr.

[Name Omitted] stated “no differences” with the Westminster
Standards. [p. 136]
**2024-06: May 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 24-1; _BCO_ 12-1) — Presbytery
approved REs to “augment” church sessions apart from
constitutional election.

**Response:** Presbytery disagrees with these
exceptions. in regard to appointing re [Name Omitted] to
augment the Session of the [Church Name Omitted],
Presbytery would point out that the appointment was made
“pending the consent of the [Church Name Omitted]
congregation.” [p. 89] in regard to appointing re [Name
Omitted] and TE [Name Omitted] to augment the Session of
the [Church Name Omitted], Presbytery would point out the
appointments were made “pending the consent of the [Church
Name Omitted] congregation.” TE [Name Omitted] was not
approved to be Moderator of the Session by Presbytery, as
requested, as this is an action for the Session, not Presbytery.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-05: Jan 10, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-2; _BCO_ 34-10) — Process
of _BCO_ 13-2 and 34-10 not followed in cases of TEs without

call for three or more years.
**Response:** Presbytery disagrees with this exception.
In order to comply with _BCO_ 13-2 and 34-10, Presbytery
adopted the following Standing Rule: 2.8. The Stated Clerk
shall report at each Stated Meeting the names of any Teaching
Elders who have three consecutive un-excused absences from

Stated Meetings and any Teaching Elders who have been

<a id="ga52-p551"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=553 printed_page=551 -->

without call to a particular work for 3 years (BCO13-2). 2.8.1
The Stated Clerk shall recommend the action, if any,
Presbytery should take. This Report was made for each of the
three (3) Stated Meetings in 2023 [p. 54, 95, & 135], noting
that all Teaching Elders, who are without call, regardless of
whether the three (3) year period has passed, are currently
filing our pulpits on a regular basis and meeting the needs of
our small congregations. Of our 39 churches, 17 do not have
full-time pastors. There is only 1 Teaching Elder who is past
the three (3) period, and he is filling one of these pulpits
almost every Lord’s Day. Accordingly, no recommendation
was made to proceed under _BCO_ 34-10 and there were no
motions from the floor to take any action.
**Rationale:** _BCO_ 13-2 states that _BCO_ 34-10 shall be

followed f orate retained o na presbytery’s rolls for more
than three years without call. Furthermore, _BCO_ 34-10 states
that the clerk shall deliver written notice to a TE without call

for more than three years that Presbytery will consider his
status at the next stated meeting. Neither a lack of
recommendation from the clerk nor the absence of a motion

from the floor constitutes compliance with _BCO_ 13-2 and 3410; Presbytery must correct its action by following _BCO_ 13-2
and 34-10.

exception of one man, who is the subject of the process of

divestiture, per _BCO_ 13-2 and 34-10. GCP will endeavor in

RPR.

brought under care of GCP (Sean Miller & Landon Jostes).
We failed to record in our minutes that they had each been
members in good standing of their PCA congregations for at
least 6 moth rmonths. And we had failed to record that their

Sessions recommended them to be under care. GCP is taking
action at our February 2024 meeting to amend the minutes to
note that thes me had met the 6-month requirement and had
received recommendation of thir Sessions.these men had met

the 6-month requirement and had received recommendation
of their Sessions.

for May 23 called meeting.

23, 2023 meeting to June 6, 2023 at the request of the Session
which had made the initial request. GCP had included the
rational in the communication to the presbytery. But we had
not included any note of rational in the minutes of the June
6th meeting. At our February 2025 meeting, GCP is taking
action to amend the minutes of June 6, 2023 to include rational
for the date change.

the minutes of our stated May 9, 2023 meeting the report of

to amend the May 2023 minutes to include the commission
report.

labor out of bounds have provided reports, as indicated in our
October 2024 minutes (TE [Name Omitted] was incorrectly
listed as working out of bounds). We will endeavor in the
future to continue to ensure that all Teaching Elders working
out of bounds report annually.

records and will endeavor to include these reports in future
minutes.

record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review [or summarized the actions taken during
session and recorded them in its minutes]. The actions of a
commission are the actions of Presbytery, thus subject to the
general review and control of General Assembly “for the
preservation both of truth and duty” ( _BCO_ 40; PP II.3).

added to the 2022 minutes and submitted for review.

**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024; Feb 3, 2024;**

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**

<a id="ga52-p556"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=558 printed_page=556 -->

**2024-08: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-6; _BCO_ 19-12) — No
record of annual reports of candidates nor of reports on interns
at each stated meeting.
**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception and will be more diligent in requiring candidates
and interns to submit their reports according to the guidelines
of the _BCO_ . This should be reflected in our Fall Stated

Meeting.
**2024-09: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7; _BCO_ 13-2) — No record
of annual reports for one of the men serving out of bounds or
for two of the three men without call.

**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception and will be more diligent in requiring TEs serving
out of bounds and TEs without call to submit their reports
according to the guidelines of the _BCO_ . This should be
reflected in our Fall Stated Meeting.
**2024-01: Feb 4, 2023** ( _BCO_ 6-2; _BCO_ 57) — Presbytery
accepts a response from a session that had “received members
while imposing conditions on their membership.” While
session admitted their error and promised not to repeat it in
the future, there is no evidence that they removed the
“conditions” from the members in question.
**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception. The Session in question has removed the condition
for membership by passing the following motion to Amend
Something Previously Adopted (RONR [12th ed.] 48:15):
Motion to amend the action adopted at the July 22, 2021
Stated Meeting of the Session of [Church Name Omitted]
relating to the membership of the [names omitted] which
stated, “Motion to approve [names omitted] for membership
in [Church Name Omitted] with the condition that they would
not be allowed to teach. [Names omitted] each gave a credible
profession of faith in Christ during their interview. [Names
omitted] assented to the vows of church membership as
outlined in _BCO_ 57-5. [Names omitted] have been baptized.

[Names omitted] will be received by reaffirmation of faith.”
The amended motion will now read, "Motion to approve

[names omitted] for membership in [Church Name Omitted].

[Names omitted] each gave a credible profession of faith in

<a id="ga52-p557"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=559 printed_page=557 -->

Christ during their interview. [Names omitted] assented to the
vows of church membership as outlined in _BCO_ 57-5. [Names
omitted] have been baptized. [Names omitted] will be
received by reaffirmation of faith."
**2024-02: Apr 28–29, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.g) — Presbytery
granted a doctrinal exception that appears to be out of accord
with the fundamentals of our doctrinal standards. The

examinee’s stated different was: "“2. _WLC_ Q. 109. What are
the sins forbidden in the second commandment? A. The sins

forbidden in the second commandment are, all devising,
counselling, commanding, using, and anywise approving, any
religious worship not instituted by God himself; tolerating a
false religion; the making any representation of God, of all or
of any of the three persons, either inwardly in our mind, or
outwardly in any kind of image or likeness of any creature
whatsoever... My exception here is in regards to the phrase “of
any of the three persons...” With reference to Jesus Christ, no
image should be made depicting His divinity. However He has
appeared in the flesh, presenting to us His image. To image
the humanity of Christ I do not believe is forbidden in the
second commandment. Thus by application, I have no
problem with the making of films depicting the life of Christ.
God has imaged Himself in the Son. Secondly, the phrase “or
outwardly any kind of image or likeness of any creature
whatsoever...” I take to be specifically prohibited for the
purpose of worship. To disconnect it from that qualifier makes
the commandment itself reduce to absurdity because it would
prohibit all paintings, art, photography or anything else that
represents any creature of God’s creation.”
As it stands, this statement seems to say we can make
“images” that picture the incarnate second person of the
Trinity as opposed to merely representing his humanity. If that
is the position it would seem to strike at the vitals of religion.
**Response:** RPR's objection to the stated exception is
unclear. We discern RPR is troubled by TE [Name Omitted]'s
statement that "no image should be made depicting [Jesus's]
divinity ... [but] [t]o image the humanity of Christ I do not
believe is forbidden in the second commandment … [because]
God has imaged himself in the Son." Upon review and further

<a id="ga52-p558"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=560 printed_page=558 -->

consideration, Heartland Presbytery is convinced that this
articulation o fan exception to _WLC_ 109 does in fact strike at
the vitals of religion. If this was RPR's intended objection, we
agree with it. To articulate the exception in this way without
further clarification would deny the hypostatic union of the
two natures of Jesus Christ (fully God and fully man) united
inseparably in the one true person of Jesus, the incarnate Son
of God and second person of the Trinity.
For a stated exception to _WLC_ 109 not to strike at the
vitals of religion it must acknowledge that imaging the second
person of the Trinity can only imperfectly and incompletely
reflect the two natures of Jesus united in his one person. And
such an image can never purport to reflect only one of those
natures (fully man) lest it become a false Christ and fall into
the heresy of Nestorianism and a denial of the hypostatic
union.

In order to provide clarity on this issue we required
TE [Name Omitted] to engage with RPR’s concern, as best as
we understand it, and appear before Presbytery to state his
difference to _WLC_ 109, if any, as he currently holds it. His
stated different is:

First, let me clearly state that I firmly believe, contra
both Nestorianism and Eutychianism, the historic orthodox
view that Christ is one person in two natures (since the
incarnation), and remains so forever (inseparably and
indivisibly united). I agree with Calvin who states, “…we
affirm his divinity so joined and united with his humanity that
each retains its distinctive nature unimpaired, and yet these
two natures constitute one Christ.” [Calvin, Institutes,
2.14.1.]. Thus, with Calvin I affirm that though there is no
division in the unity of the person of Christ in regards to His
person, there is “distinction.” (For example, consider the
historic teaching of the “communicatio idiomatum”
(communication of properties).
Second, I wish to acknowledge that my statements
made in my transfer exam could have been clearer. i in noway
believe it is possible to make a representation of Christ that
truly images the fullness of the person of Christ. Similarly, I
in no way mean imply that we can or should ever make images

<a id="ga52-p559"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=561 printed_page=559 -->

to somehow even capture the full human nature of Christ.
What I meant to refer to in my original comment on my
exception is the human physical body of Christ. Thus, I wrote,
“My exception here is in regards to the phrase ‘of any of the
three persons...’ With reference to Jesus Christ, no image
should be made depicting His divinity. However, He has
appeared in the flesh, presenting to us His image. To image
the humanity of Christ I do not believe is forbidden in the
second commandment.” (emphasis added). My last sentence
is unclear as I use both the terms “flesh” and “humanity” in a
way that may be understood to equate them. I should have
said, “He has appeared in the flesh, presenting to us His
image. To image Christ in the flesh I do not believe is
forbidden in the second commandment.”

i unpack i ta bit more hereby way of analogy. Though
i ti san imperfect analogy (since there is no perfect analogy of
the hypostatic union), all humans are comprised of body and
soul, and no picture of a person (drawing, photograph, stick
figure, etc.) can possibly capture their human nature; it at best
can capture imperfectly that which is visible (i.e. their
materially visible “tent”). Similarly, we cannot and should not
make images of the human or divine natures of Christ.
However, I do not believe that it is sinful to make or imagine
in our minds images of Christ dwelling in the flesh on earth
during his earthly ministry. I do not believe this position in
any way destroys or divides the unity of the one Person of
Christ in two natures.

Third, _WLC_ 109 forbids, “making any representation
of God, of all or of any of the three persons, either inwardly
in our mind, or outwardly in any kind of image or likeness of
any creature whatsoever…” The key concept that I take
exception to here is the prohibition to making any kind of
image “inwardly in our mind.” Consider our reading any of
the many descriptions of Christ in the gospels. How can we
not picture those events in our minds? How do we not picture
the hand of Christ scooping up clay to make salve for the blind
man’s eyes? How do we not picture the nails and sword
piercing Christ’s hands and side? How do we not picture
Christ walking with the men on the road to Emmaus or on the

<a id="ga52-p560"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=562 printed_page=560 -->

water with Peter? On the contrary, I believe we are meant to
picture those events (as deficient as our images may be).
Therefore, though we are not to make images of God,
for teaching purposes I believe it is not sinful to make images
of Jesus’ physical form (flesh). i do not believe that tod o so
somehow divides the indivisible union of the two natures in

Christ in one person.
Heartland Presbytery finds this stated difference to be
more than semantic, but not out of accord with any
fundamental of our system of doctrine ( _RAO_ 16-3.e.6.c).
**2024-03: Apr 28–29, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1) — No record of why
pres byte ry considered a call from a non-PCA source t ob ea
valid Christian ministry.
**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception. HP omitted the specific finding in question. The
minutes of the April 28-29, 2023 meeting state, “The call is
out-of-bounds of Heartland Presbytery, but the minister will
be engaged in preaching and teaching the Word with full
freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our Church, in
accordance with _BCO_ 8-7.” HP will amend the minutes by
adding the following statement, “Since the call involves the
preaching and teaching of the Word, Presbytery finds the call
to be a valid Christian ministry ( _BCO_ 20-1).”
**2024-04: Aug 26, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1; _BCO_ 21-1) — A man is
examined for ordination without Presbytery having the call in
its hands.

**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception. At the called meeting of August 26, 2023, HP
proceeded to commence the exam although the call was not in
our hands. Upon finding during the Called Meeting that the
call had not yet been delivered to HP, HP delayed the
ordination action to the September 2023 Stated Meeting, at
which time the call had been received by HP.
**2024-05: Aug 26, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2; _BCO_ 18-7) — No record
o fa man being a candidate in this oran y other pres byte ry prior
to his being examined for “transfer of license and ordination.”.
**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception. The record did not mention that the candidate for
licensure and ordination exams in HP was first a candidate of

<a id="ga52-p561"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=563 printed_page=561 -->

New Jersey Presbytery. Upon the candidate’s request and with
the cooperation via email of the clerks of New Jersey
Presbytery and HP, HP received a candidate’s dismission via
email and portal record from New Jersey Presbytery. HP will
update the record of the August 26, 2023 Called Meeting to
include, “[Name omitted] has been a candidate under the care
of New Jersey Presbytery and has been released for transfer
to the Heartland Presbytery. Record of the certificate of
dismission has been received electronically.”
**2024-06: Sep 29–30, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.c(4)) — Presbytery
establishes a 6 member committee to hear licensure and

ordination sermons for the next 3 years. For ordination this
would require a ¾ vote.
**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception. The vote to establish the committee was more than
3/4 majority, but the result of the vote was not specifically
recorded. HP will update the record of the September 29-30,
2023 Stated Meeting to include, “The vote was more than the
¾ majority required ( _BCO_ 21-4.c(4)).”
**2024-07: Oct 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-7) — no record o fa
certificate of dismission for a candidate coming from another
PCA presbytery.
**Response:** The Heartland Presbytery agrees with this
exception. We failed to record in our minutes that a certificate
of dismission was received. The transfer was conducted in the

Presbytery Portal. HP will update the record of the October
28, 2023 Called Meeting with the following line: “The Stated
Clerk of Heartland Presbytery has received an electronic
certificate of dismission for the candidate from the South

Coast Presbytery.”

**2022-01: May 11, 2021** ( _BCO_ 38-3) — No determination of
a judgment b ypres byte ry as to the withdrawal o fa minister
from the PCA to another body.
**Response:** The Heritage Presbytery moved/seconded
and approved the motion [p.1] to accept the resignation of
then TE [name omitted], at the May 11, 2021 124th Stated
Meeting). This action was predicated on the email we received
from the TE documented on page 18 of the approved Minutes
from the May 11, 2021, 124th Stated Meeting, resigning from
the Heritage Presbytery and the PCA. This report was
approved at the 131st Heritage Presbytery Meeting on January
28, 2023.
**Rationale:** Presbytery needs to judge the matter
under _BCO_ 38-2 or the appropriate section of _BCO_ 38-3.
**Response:** [2023] [Name Omitted] was divested
under 38-2, removed as irregularity under provision of 38-3
(a) because he affiliated with another branch of the visible
church the presbytery recognized as maintaining the word and
sacraments in their fundamental integrity.
**Rationale:** Presbytery needs to judge the matter
under _BCO_ 38-2 or the appropriate section of _BCO_ 38-3. The
Presbytery still needs to judge the matter in either provision.
If he is divested, he no longer falls under _BCO_ 38-3.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
has corrected its record. [Name Omitted] was removed from
the roll under 38-3.a, as he affiliated with another branch of
the visible church that the presbytery recognized as
maintaining the word and sacraments in their fundamental
integrity.

Committee and accepted by the Heritage Presbytery.

action upon committee reviews of 2022.

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-02: Apr 15, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.6) —
Specific arrangements of call not shown to be approved.
**Response:** The JRP acknowledges and agrees with
the Assembly's taken exception of substance. The terms of
call were presented at the meeting, discussed, and approved,
but our meeting minutes failed to capture this approval.
**2024-03: Apr 15, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6, 21-4) — No record of
requiring statement of differences with our Standards for
reception from another denomination.
**Response:** The JRP acknowledges and agrees with
the Assembly's taken exception of substance, and expresses
its commitment to comply with all of the _BCO_ 's requirements
for recording the requirement that a minister transferring from

<a id="ga52-p571"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=573 printed_page=571 -->

another denomination state differences to our Standards. This

is again a failure of our minutes to capture what occurred at
the meeting. The candidate was asked to state his differences
with our Standards, but had no differences. This should have
been captured in the minutes but was not.
**2024-04: Apr 15, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.d) — Incomplete record
of the trial parts omitted for reception from another
denomination.

**Response:** The JRP acknowledges and agrees with
the Assembly's taken exception of substance and expresses its
commitment to comply with the _BCO_ 's requirements for
recording what trial parts were omitted when the
extraordinary provision of _BCO_ 21-4(d) is invoked.
**2024-05: Apr 15, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6; _BCO_ 21-4.i) —
Incomplete record of extraordinary circumstances of the
church or proven extraordinary gifts of the man when
invoking the extraordinary provisions of _BCO_ 21-4 for
reception from another denomination.
**Response:** The JRP acknowledges and agrees with
the Assembly's exception that the JRP did not record the
reasons it believed that the TE was a man of "proven
extraordinary gifts" and expresses its commitment to properly
record its reasons for invoking the extraordinary provisions of
_BCO_ 21-4.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-01: Jan 21, 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-3.c.6) — No record of the
names of ruling elders in attendance.
**Response:** As stated in response to a similar
exception last year, the JRP disagrees with this exception of
substance. While names of attending ruling elders were not
included in the minutes, the record shows on the listed pages
that churches were either considered present or absent (noted
by the "P" and the "U" next to the names of each church) by
the presence of commissioned ruling elders. _BCO_ 13-1 makes
it clear that a Presbytery is comprised of TEs and her
churches, not the specific ruling elders. We consider our
record sufficient to satisfy _BCO_ 13-4. The JRP has included

<a id="ga52-p572"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=574 printed_page=572 -->

the sign-in sheets in the 2024 minutes for the 2024 stated
meetings in response to this exception.
**Rationale:** _RAO_ 16-3.c.6 Requires that “The names
of those present at the meeting” be recorded. This information
is required for various purposes, including determining which
ruling elders have the right of complaint, and which members
have the right of access to executive session minutes.

32. That the Minutes of **Korean Capital Presbytery:** _62-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **None.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **None.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 40) — There are no reviews of
the minutes of Sessions in 2024. This is at least the second

year in a row for this exception.
**2025-02: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 19-6) — No record of reports
on interns at each stated meeting.
**2025-03: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 8-7; _BCO_ 8-6; _BCO_ 13-2) —
There are no annual reports for over half the TEs serving out
of bounds (including missionaries with non-PCA calls), there
are almost no reports from TEs without call, and there are no
annual reports for about half the men under care.
**2025-04: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 18-2; _BCO_ 18-3; _BCO_ 19-7)
— No record of sessional endorsements or six-months

membership for ones coming under care; no record of charges
to candidates; no record of the nature of internships that are
established.
**2025-05: Apr 3, 2023** ( _WLC_ 109) — Presbytery ruled the
following stated difference put forth by a candidate for
licensure to be more than semantic, but not "out of accord with
any fundamental of our system of doctrine." "I'd like to
request an exception to _WLC_ 109 where it speaks against
making a representation of God. I personally believe that the
second commandment refers to not making images of "idols"
(cf. the NLT translation) rather than not making images of any
persons of the Godhead." If the candidate views the making
of images to any of the persons of the Godhead as an
acceptable practice, it would seem that this is "out of accord
with any fundamental of our system of doctrine because the

<a id="ga52-p573"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=575 printed_page=573 -->

difference is either hostile to the system or strikes at the vitals
of religion" ( _BCO_ 19-2.b.3.f). The candidate's position seems
to run directly contrary to Deuteronomy 4:15-17, and it
appears to undercut the regulative principle of worship as set
forth in _WCF_ XXI-1; _WLC_ 109 and _WSC_ 51.
**2025-06: Apr 8, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-5; _BCO_ 21-6) — During the
installation of an Assistant Pastor the questions are posed to
the Congregation rather than the Session. Further, there is no
evidence of who opened or closed the Commission meeting in
prayer, who preached the sermon, or the text of the sermon.
**2025-07: Apr 8, 2024** ( _BCO_ 22-2; _BCO_ 22-3; _BCO_ 21-5;
_BCO_ 21-6) — A TE's call is changed from Assistant to
Associate Pastor, but there is no evidence he is then installed
on the basis of the new call where the vows are with the

Congregation rather than the Session.
**2025-08: Oct 7, 2024** ( _BCO_ 25-11, para 3) — No record that
the required 30-day notice was given for the Congregational
meeting on a church's withdrawal.
**2025-09: Oct 7, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-6; _BCO_ 21-1) — No record
of examination or approval of call for a TE transferring into
Presbytery from another PCA Presbytery.
**2025-10: Oct 7, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-1) — Presbytery may not
amend its Standing Rules to **mandate** that TE members of
Presbytery pay an annual $50 fee, nor may Presbytery charge
a mandatory fee for examinations. This would be tantamount
to a local session mandating that all church members pay a
fee to be examined for membership or to be members in good
standing.
**2025-11: Oct 7, 2024** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record of
Congregational concurrence in dissolution of calls.
**2025-12: Oct 7, 2024** ( _BCO_ 46-6; _BCO_ 21-1) — A TE is
transferred to another PCA Presbytery. No record of the call
or other reason that is the basis of the transfer, and no record
that a certificate of dismission was to be issued.

**2025-13: Dec 15, 2024** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record that
Presbytery is assured that an out-of-bounds TE will have full
freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our Church.
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**

<a id="ga52-p574"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=576 printed_page=574 -->

**2024-01: Apr 3, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-10; Preliminary Principle
II.3) — No record of transfer or dismissal of members upon
dissolving a church.
**Response:** We regret this oversight. Most of the
dissolution process was handled by the session commission
and the presbytery stated clerk was notified after the fact. We
will do better with caring and shepherding our sessions in the
future.

**2024-04: Jun 4, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-12) — Notice for called
meeting not in order (10-day notice and verbatim meeting call
not recored in minutes).
**Response:** We regret this oversight. The notice was
sent out in advance of 10-days before the meeting. The
minutes was revised to include the text of the original notice
and is attached to this response.
**2024-05: Jul 20, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-12) — Notice for called
meeting not in order (10-day notice and verbatim meeting call
not recored in minutes).
**Response:** We regret this oversight. The notice was
sent out in advance of 10-days before the meeting. The
minutes was revised to include the text of the original notice
and is attached to this response.
**2024-06: Jul 20, 2023** ( _BCO_ 31-2; _BCO_ 32-2; 32-3) — No
record of a _BCO_ 31-2 investigation that raised a “strong
presumption of guilt” or a written charge made out against the
accused TE before the Presbytery appointed a prosecutor to
prepare the indicment and designated a commission to
conduct the trial.

**Response:** We regret not being clear on the plan of
subsequent actions in the called meeting minutes. The
temporary judicial commission met after the called presbytery
meeting and determined the times, places, and circumstances
for the accused to make his defense against the indictment.
The summary of that judicial commission's work was attached
to the 63rd stated meeting minutes, along with their report in
the appendix, which reports all the relevant times, places and
circumstances.

<a id="ga52-p575"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=577 printed_page=575 -->

**2024-07: Jul 20, 2023** ( _BCO_ 32-5) — No record that
Presbytery specified “the times, places and circumstances” for
accused to make his defense” against the indictment.
**Response:** We regret not being clear on the plan of
subsequent actions in the called meeting minutes. The
temporary judicial commission met after the called presbytery
meeting and determined the times, places, and circumstances
for the accused to make his defense against the indictment.
The summary of that judicial commission's work was attached
to the 63rd stated meeting minutes, along with their report in
the appendix, which reports all the relevant times, places and
circumstances.

**2024-09: Oct 2, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-5; _BCO_ 20-1) — No record
of call to a definite work f orate transferring from another
denomination.

**Response:** We regret the oversight. The clerk read
and reported on the nature and details of the
ministry("[Church Name Omitted]"), and the presbytery
approved. The minutes was revised to include that action.
**2024-10: Dec 31, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-12) — Notice for called
meeting not in order (10-day notice and verbatim meeting call
not recored in minutes).
**Response:** We regret this oversight. The notice was
sent out in advance of 10-days before the meeting. The
minutes was revised to include the text of the original notice
and is attached to this response.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-03: Apr 3, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; _BCO_ 40-1) —
Incomplete record of review of records of church Sessions.
**Response:** We regret this oversight. Many of our
church sessions have been lacking response to the call of the
stated clerk to submit their minutes in the past. We are
restructuring the process of our session minutes review
committee so that the review is done before the presbytery and
trying to better remind the sessions about and enforce the
session minutes submission requirements.

<a id="ga52-p576"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=578 printed_page=576 -->

**Rationale:** Presbytery continued the error by failing
in 2024 to review the minutes of any session.
**2024-08: Oct 2, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; _BCO_ 40-1) — Results of
review of records of church Sessions not stated.

**Response:** We regret the oversight. The session
minutes review committee failed to report on their work. We
will remind the committee to be more diligent in reporting
their review work in the future.
**Rationale:** Presbytery continued the error by failing
in 2024 to review the minutes of any session.

33. That the Minutes of **Korean Central Presbytery:** _61-0-1_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **None.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: Apr 9–10, 2024** ( _BCO_ 38-2) — No record that
minister came forward and state lack of call to ministry at
prior meeting or that full deliberation of presbytery occurred.
**2025-02: Apr 9–10, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-6; _BCO_ 21-4) —
Incomplete record of exam for minister seeking admission
from another denomination.

**2025-03: Apr 9–10, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-6; _BCO_ 21-4) —
Incomplete record of exam for minister seeking admission
from another denomination.

**2025-04: Oct 8–9, 2024** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
congregation was cited to appear and show cause re severance
of cal loft e [Name Omitted]; TE [Name Omitted]; TE

[Name Omitted].
**2025-05: Oct 8–9, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record for ordination exam.
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-01: Oct 12-13, 2021; Apr 5-6, 2022** ( _BCO_ 24-1, 40-4,
11-4; _BCO_ Preface II-6) — Minutes contain several
references to men having passed ‘the examination for ruling
elders.” This appears to be the presbytery exercising oversight
over who may be elected as a ruling elder.
**Response:** Korean Central Presbytery (hereafter
“KCP”) acknowledges CRPR’s concern about the possibility

<a id="ga52-p577"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=579 printed_page=577 -->

of the Presbytery exercising undue oversight over local
congregations. But we also respectfully note that the churches
in our Presbytery elect their own ruling elder candidates. KCP
examines these nominees only at the request of these church
Sessions who deem themselves unable to administer these

examinations on their own. The churches then install the RE

nominees after they successfully complete their examinations
by presbytery. While this seems to be a common practice
among some Korean-language Presbyteries in the PCA, KCP
acknowledges it is not ideal. We hope to move in a direction
where our local church Sessions can examine their own
nominees for Ruling Elders per _BCO_ 12-5.b and _BCO_ 24-1.
**Rationale:** Presbytery did not respond according to
_RAO_ 16-10.b. Presbytery can either agree with the exception
or respectfully disagree with the exception.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
acknowledges our error. We have corrected our actions by
discontinuing our practice of administering examinations to
RE candidates.

**2023-02: Oct 12-13, 2021; Apr 5-6, 2022** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_
16-3.e.5) — Stated differences not recorded in the
minister’s/candidates own words.

**Response:** Korean Central Presbytery agrees with the
exception. At our October 2021 Stated Meeting, an ordination
candidate, [Name Omitted], noted the following stated
difference:

_I have a different view of the “Sabbath” than WFC Chapter_
_21-8. I affirm with the Westminster statement that man needs_
_to “observe a holy rest” on the Sabbath or Lord’s day. This_
_means that man is to be separate from work, so that the_
_Sabbath day looks different from other days. I affirm that man_
_is not good at resting, there are many activities that one may_
_consider resting that in fact is “profaning the day by idleness”_
_(WLC 119). I believe that the specific definition of “holy rest”_
_being that one should rest from “their works, words, and_
_thoughts about their worldly employments and recreations”_
_goes too far in its prohibitions. The statement “spend the_
_whole time in public and private exercises of worship” fails_
_to allow for creational rest and refreshment that can include_

<a id="ga52-p578"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=580 printed_page=578 -->

_such recreational activities as running, walking, and other_
_God-glorifying, family nurturing actives as WLC 121 affirms._
_To do Sabbath worship and duties “all the day” and “the_
_whole time” would seem at tension with the great significance_
_Scripture also teaches on rest._
KCP adopted its Candidates and Credentials Committee’s
recommendation to judge this stated difference to be “more
than semantic but ‘not out of accord with any fundamental
system of our doctrine.’”
At our April 2022 Stated Meeting, a licensure candidate,

[Name Omitted], noted the following stated difference from
_WCF_ 21-8, _WLC_ 117 and 119, and _WSC_ 60-61:
_I have a scruple concerning the forbidding of worldly_
_recreations on the Lord’s day. I believe that recreation, such_
_as the playing of sports or games can be a lawful form of rest_
_on the Lord’s day._
KCP adopted its Candidates and Credentials Committee’s
recommendation to judge this stated difference to be “more
than semantic but ‘not out of accord with any fundamental
system of our doctrine.’”
We apologize for not including these stated differences in our
minutes, and we promise to be more careful in the future.
**Rationale:** Presbytery apologized and promises to be
more careful in the future, but needs to amend the record and
indicate as such in responses ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1).
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
once again acknowledges our error. We will correct our
Minutes for the relevant Stated Meetings, and we promise to
be more careful in the future.

**2024-01: Oct 11, 2022** ( _BCO_ 24-1; 40-4; 11-4; Preface II-6)
— Minutes contain several references to men being examined
for the office of ruling elder. This appears to be the presbytery
exercising oversight over the election of ruling elders.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. We have corrected our actions by
discontinuing our practice of administering examinations to
ruling elder candidates.
**2024-02: Oct 11, 2022** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.

<a id="ga52-p579"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=581 printed_page=579 -->

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. We neglected to record in our
minutes that the ordination candidate, [Name Omitted],
fulfilled all of the requirements listed in _BCO_ 21-4 (bachelor’s
degree, seminary degree, experiential religion, personal
character, family management, Greek and Hebrew, Bible
content, theology, PCA history, preached sermon, completed
internship, and call arrangement). We will correct the
Minutes, and we promise to be more careful in the future.
**2024-03:** **Oct** **11,** **2022** ( _BCO_ 38-2; 38-3) —
Demission/withdrawal requires more information according
to _BCO_ 38-2; 38-3.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. The TE who was granted demission
from office ([Name Omitted]) had made the request to the
Presbytery at a previous Stated Meeting because he indicated
that he no longer sensed a call to the ministry. The TE who
withdrew his membership ([Name Omitted]) had
communicated his plans to transfer to another denomination
to fulfill his new pastoral call. Both ministers were in good
standing with the Presbytery when their requests were
granted. We will amend the minutes from this Stated Meeting,
and we promise to be more careful in the future.
**2024-04: Oct 11, 2022** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — Incomplete
examination for candidates for the gospel ministry.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. All three candidates had fulfilled the
required 6-month membership at their local churches before
their approval to come under the care of the Presbytery as
candidates for the Gospel Ministry. We will amend the
minutes from this Stated Meeting, and we promise to be more
careful in the future.

**2024-05: Oct 11, 2023** ( _BCO_ 24-1; 40-4; 11-4; Preface II-6)
— Minutes contain several references to men being examined
for the office of ruling elder. This appears to be the presbytery
exercising oversight over the election of ruling elders.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. We have corrected our actions by

<a id="ga52-p580"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=582 printed_page=580 -->

discontinuing our practice of administering examinations to
ruling elder candidates.
**2024-06: Apr 18, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6; 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of examination for transfer from outside
the PCA.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. We neglected to record in our
minutes that the transfer candidate, [Name Omitted], fulfilled
all of the requirements listed in _BCO_ 21-4 (bachelor’s degree,
seminary degree, experiential religion, personal character,
family management, Greek and Hebrew, Bible content,
theology, PCA history, preached sermon, completed
internship, and call arrangement). We will correct the minutes
from this Stated Meeting, and we promise to be more careful
in the future.
**2024-07: Oct 11, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6) — Incomplete exam for
transfer from another presbytery within the PCA.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. We neglected to record that the
transfer candidate, [Name Omitted], was examined
thoroughly with regard to his Christian experience along with
his knowledge and views of theology, the sacraments, and
church government in accordance with _BCO_ 13-6. We will
correct the minutes from this Stated Meeting, and we promise
to be more careful in the future.

**2024-08: Oct 11, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6; 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of examination for transfer from outside
the PCA. Portions of the exam may be omitted by a ¾ vote of
presbytery.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. We neglected to record that the
transfer candidate, [Name Omitted], fulfilled all of the
requirements listed in _BCO_ 21-4, including Christian
experience, theology, sacraments, and church government.
We will correct the minutes from this Stated Meeting, and we
promise to be more careful in the future.
**2024-10: Jan 10, 2023** _(BCO_ 40-1; 13-11) — Minutes of
Called Meeting 1/10/2023 missing/not received by RPR.

<a id="ga52-p581"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=583 printed_page=581 -->

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
apologizes for our error. We have submitted the Minutes for
this January 2023 Called Meeting for review.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2022-01: Apr 13–14, 2021** ( _BCO_ 13-11, 31-2, 32-2, 32-3, 324, 32-5, 36-1, 36-5, 37-3) — There is insufficient record in the
minutes of actions taken by the Presbytery (as well as
documents in support of those actions) pursuant to their
handling of an ecclesiastical trial against TE (Name Omitted),
such as, but not limited to the allegations against him, the
recommendations from an ad interim committee appointed to
consider these allegations, the indictment of the prosecutor,
the imposition and removal of censure, and several additional
motions related to this situation. These missing items need to
be either recreated or collected and submitted to the 50th

General Assembly.
**Response:** Korean Central Presbytery agrees with
this exception and humbly apologizes for our oversight in this
matter. We are submitting the following documents to the
50th General Assembly in response to this request by CRPR:
(1) the original allegations against the TE as submitted to the
Presbytery; (2) the findings and recommendations of the adinterim committee as summarized in their report as received
by the Presbytery; and (3) the minutes from this stated
meeting, which summarizes our actions on the committee’s
recommendations. In addition, some of the actions from this
April 2021 were the subject of a complaint that was
adjudicated by the Standing Judicial Commission in June
2022

**Rationale:** SJC Case 2021-11). While the SJC
sustained the complaint against some of Presbytery’s
procedural errors in this matter, it also ruled that many of these
defects were no longer correctable, including the absence of
an indictment from the prosecutor.
**Response:** Though Presbytery acknowledges their
error and has sent some documents, the report from the
interim committee seems to be missing their respective and

<a id="ga52-p582"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=584 printed_page=582 -->

referenced appendices (see: _RAO_ 16-3.e.8), in addition to a
letter of confession from the confessing TE.
**Rationale:** Appendices have been received;
confession letter has not been received.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
acknowledges our error. The TE’s confession letter has been
submitted as requested.
**Rationale:** Presbytery needs to correct its minutes.

**d.** **That the following responses shall be submitted to the**
**following GA as no responses were received in 2025:**

<a id="ga52-p583"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=585 printed_page=583 -->

**2023-01: General 2022** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; 40-1) — No record of
review of records of church Sessions.

**Response:** We respectfully disagree with this
exception. The following motion appears in our 2/11 /23
minutes under the report of the Stated Clerk (p. 34 ): "MSC to
approve the responses to RPR regarding exceptions of
substance to the 2022 minutes of presbytery, and to take the
actions stated in the responses."
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
action. No review of Presbytery records have been recorded
for the current or past year.
**2023-02: General 2022** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record of annual
report(s) of TE(s) laboring out of bounds.
**Response:** Some of OB ministers submitted their
ministry report with excuse absence request, and KEP kept the
report in the docket of stated meeting. KEP apologizes for
submitting minutes without the docket material. The stated
clerk will make sure to include OB ministers’ report when
submitting the meeting minutes.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record or its actions. No OB ministers’ reports have been
included for the current or prior year.
**2024-08: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6; _BCO_ 21-5) — A minister
admitted from another denomination was not asked the

questions put to candidates at their ordination.
**2024-09: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; _BCO_ 40-1) — No
record of review of records of church Sessions.

**2024-10: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record of annual
report(s) of TE(s) laboring out of bounds.
**2024-11: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-6) — Annual reports of
candidates not included.
**2024-01: Sep 19, 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7; _BCO_ 20-1) — Presbytery
approved an out-of-bounds call without assurance that the TE
will have full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of

our church (8-7), without record that the TE is engaged in
preaching and teaching the Word (8-7), and without stating
reasons why it considers the out-of-bounds work t ob ea valid
Christian ministry (20-1).

<a id="ga52-p584"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=586 printed_page=584 -->

**2024-02: Sep 12, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2.f) — No record of
requiring statement of differences with our Standards.
**2024-03: Sep 12, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2) — All specific
requirements of licensure exam not recorded.
**2024-04: Mar 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2) — All specific
requirements of licensure exam not recorded.
**2024-05: Mar 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6) — Incomplete record of
examination of TE transferring into Presbytery.
**2024-06: Mar 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6; _BCO_ 21-4) —
Incomplete record of exam for minister seeking admission
from another denomination.

**2024-07: Mar 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _BCO_ 13-6) — No record
of requiring statements of differences with our Standards.
**2024-12: Mar 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) —
Minutes of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes,
and these must be presented for review next year.
