**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-03: May 3, 2022; Nov 1, 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 163.e.4) — Minutes of commission not entered in Presbytery
minutes, and these must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. The Minutes of
the Commission were received by the Presbytery, included in
the Docket for the Presbytery’s, but were not included in the
Minutes as required. They are attached to the Minutes from
the Presbytery meeting [Nov 7, 2023] that this response to
RPR is approved
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). [The response says that the minutes
of the commission are “attached” to the end of the 7

November 2023 minutes, but they are not.]
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. A clerical error
resulted in the required documents to not be submitted for

<a id="ga52-p691"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=693 printed_page=691 -->

review by RPR in response to the initial Exception. The
Presbytery will include them with these Minutes for Review.
**2023-04: May 3, 2022** ( _BCO_ 34-7; 30-3) — No record of trial
or case without process prior to suspending member from

sacraments.

**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. On the basis of
a signed confession and the desire to protect the peace and
purity of the Church, we suspended the now deposed member
from the Sacraments. it was a misapplication of _BCO_ 34-7
that led us to that action, as we also instituted a definite
suspension from ministry at the same time. A later Judicial
Commission led to a final judgment of the former TE being
deposed from ministry and indefinite suspension from the
Sacraments.
**Rationale:** Report from Judicial Commission not
entered into the record.

**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. A clerical error
resulted in the required documents to not be submitted for
review by RPR in response to the initial Exception. The
Presbytery will include them with these Minutes for Review.
**2023-05: Nov 1, 2022** ( _BCO_ 19-2; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — All
specific requirements of licensure exam not recorded.
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. Should this
Candidate come forward for Ordination, as anticipated, we
will examine him in these areas and those required for
Ordination.
**Rationale:** Presbytery’s response does not correct
the error, as it is not clear that the Presbytery has actually
conducted the licensure exam.

**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. The Licentiate
had been examined as to his Christian experience and inward
call to Gospel ministry, which was not noted properly in the
Minutes. We will endeavor to specify all specific
requirements. This serves to correct the record.

<a id="ga52-p692"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=694 printed_page=692 -->

**2023-06: Nov 1, 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-3) — No record of full
statement of the case and the judgment rendered by Judicial
Commission.

**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. The full
statement of the case and the judgment rendered by Judicial
Commission were properly before the Court, but not properly
recorded in the Minutes. These will be appended to the
Minutes in which this response is approved to correct the
previous failure to include that statement in the Minutes.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1)
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. A clerical error
resulted in the required documents to not be submitted for
review by RPR in response to the initial Exception. The
Presbytery will include them with these Minutes for Review.
**2024-01: Feb 7, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-11) — Minutes of Executive
Session not included, and these must be submitted next year.
**Response:** The Presbytery disagrees with this
Exception - the entirety of the Exectutive Session Minutes are
present and were ony for the purpose of Approving the
Executive Minutes from the Previous Presbytery meeting
(55th Stated Meeting), which took place in November of
2022. This is exactly what the Minutes state and no other
actions were taken during that particular Executive Session.
**2024-02: Aug 1, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes
of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes, and these
must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. It has not been
our practice to include these Minutes when a Standing
Committee has been empowered to act as a Commission and
the Presbytery is informed of the action, thus creating a
second set of Minutes that address the action taken.

Furthermore, the Presbytery receives the Pastoral Care
Committee’s Minutes within Docketed materials.

<a id="ga52-p693"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=695 printed_page=693 -->

**2024-03: Aug 1, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-11; _RAO_ 16-3.e.7) —
Minutes of Executive Session not included, and these must be
submitted next year.
**Response:** The Presbytery disagrees with this
exception. As noted on page 33, the Executive Session
Minutes of August 1st were attached seperately and found on
page 59, clearly marked as such. The Clerk does note,
however, that there may have been confusion due to an
incorrect Section heading and will submit again.
**2024-04: Nov 7, 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-3.c.8) — No record that
previous minutes were approved.
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with this exception
and promises to be more careful in the future. The previous
minutes were, in fact, approved by the Presbytery, but notated
incorrectly. This serves to correct the record.

**2025-22: Sep 23–24, 2024** ( _BCO_ 46-6; 13-7) — No record
regarding the reception of a certificate of dismission from
transferring presbytery (May be accomplished through Clerks
portal).
**2025-23: Sep 23–24, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Presbytery improperly classified a view regarding marriage
with Roman Catholics as "more than semantic but not out of

accord with any fundamental of our system of doctrine" rather
than “merely semantic.”.
**2025-24: Sep 23–24, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Presbytery failed to judge candidate’s stated differences.
**2025-25: Sep 23–24, 2024** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
congregation was cited to appear before Presbytery.
**2025-26: Nov 12, 2024** ( _BCO_ 46-6; 13-7) — No record
regarding the reception of a certificate of dismission from
transferring presbytery (May be accomplished through Clerks
portal).
**2025-27: Nov 12, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-6) — Incomplete record of
examination of TE transferring into Presbytery. [Should be
Christian experience rather than testimony and call to
ministry, and views examined must be listed: theology, the
sacraments, and church government.].
**2025-28: Nov 12, 2024** (; _BCO_ 46-6; 13-7) — No record
regarding the reception of a certificate of dismission from
transferring presbytery (May be accomplished through Clerks
portal).
**2025-29: Nov 12, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-4.f) — Presbytery appears
to have incorrectly judged that a candidate’s stated difference
is not out of accord with any fundamental of our system of
doctrine because the difference is neither hostile to the system
nor strikes at the vitals of religion. “4) _WLC_ #177 (maybe
_WSC_ #97). I believe in paedocommunion (young child
communion). Our baptized children are ‘not only for the
solemn admission of the party baptized into the visible
Church, but also to be unto him a sign and seal of the covenant
of grace, of his ingrafting into Christ, of regeneration, of
remission of sins, and of his giving up unto God, through
Jesus Christ, to walk in newness of life’ ( _WCF_ 28.1). I believe
they should likewise be welcome at the Lord’s Table as part

<a id="ga52-p696"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=698 printed_page=696 -->

of this family when they are welcomed to the kitchen table as
part of their families (i.e., not as suckling infants but as young
children). As described in the _WCF_ 31.4, ‘suspension from the
sacrament of the Lord’s Supper for a season’ is for those who
are being disciplined, and I do not believe it is biblical to treat,
our covenant children the same way as 89 ‘obstinate
offenders.’ ( _WCF_ 31.3) I agree with Dr. Robert S. Rayburn’s
position in the Minority Report to the Ad-Interim Committee
to Study the Question of Paedocommunion (16th GA).
However, and this is probably the most important aspect of
this, i will not nor have i ever practiced anything contrary
to that which is permitted in the Westminster Standards and
the _BCO_, as God’s Word and my ordination vows would
require me to submit in practice to the judgment of the church
in this matter.” ["I believe in paedocommunion."].
**2025-30: Nov 12, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-4.f) — Presbytery appears
to have incorrectly judged that a candidate’s stated difference
is not out of accord with any fundamental of our system of
doctrine because the difference is neither hostile to the system
nor strikes at the vitals of religion. TE seems to imply that
making images of God isn’t forbidden by the second
commandment. “2) Larger Catechism 109. Where the
Confession states ‘The sins forbidden in the second

commandment are…the making any representation of God, or
of any of the three persons, either inwardly in our mind, or
outwardly in any kind of image or likeness of any creature
whatsoever,’ I believe that Scripture teaches in the second
commandment (Exodus 20:4) that it is forbidden to make
idols or images to bow down and worship, but not that it is
forbidden to make any representation of God at all, either
inwardly or outwardly. Though Christian tradition has
generally found it impious to create images of God the Father,
it has not generally thought the same with regard to Christ, the
Son of God, or the Holy Spirit. More specifically, the
Scriptures themselves speak of the form of Jesus (e.g. Isaiah
53, John 20:24- 29, etc.) and of the Holy Spirit in the form of
a creature (Matthew 3:16). Thus I believe that the confession
goes beyond the prohibitions of Scripture in this regard.”

<a id="ga52-p697"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=699 printed_page=697 -->

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2022-02: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-12; _RAO_ 16-3.c.1) —
Notice and purpose and those calling the called meeting not
recorded.

**Response:** The previous stated clerk forgot to attach
the call to the minutes. The following is the call: “Dear
Brothers, Please see the attached docket and documents for
the called Presbytery meeting on May 21, 2021 in Las Cruces.
This meeting addresses TE [Name Omitted] being called as
pastor to [Church Name Omitted], [Name Omitted] being
called as intern by [Church Name Omitted] and changes in the
affiliation of [Church Name Omitted] and [Name Omitted].

[Church Name Omitted] has decided to withdraw from the
PCA and join the Bible Presbyterian Church. While I am sad
to see them leave and will miss seeing leaders from [Church
Name Omitted] at Presbytery, I honor their decision. At this
time, TE [Name Omitted] wishes to remain as Honorably
Retired in our Presbytery. The call letter to [Name Omitted]
will be sent to you when I receive it. Yours in Christ, [Name
Omitted], Temporary Clerk”
**Rationale:** No reference as to whether PRG has

corrected its minutes. PRG must resubmit corrected minutes

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected with the
call letter attached (Attachment 1)
**2022-04: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b) — No record of review
of records of church Sessions.

**Response:** Noted in the minutes that I do have record
that Presbytery reviewed the minutes of the sessions
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery approved
review of session minutes (April 25-26, 2022).
**Response:** We have corrected the minutes as the
2021 Session Minutes were reviewd and approved at the April
2022 Stated Meeting. The April 2022 minutes now reflect
such a change (Attachment 2).
**2023-03: Sep 26–27, 2022** ( _BCO_ 13-5, 23-2) — Presbytery
adopted a policy requiring honorably retired ministers to
transfer their membership upon relocation to another
Presbytery. [ _BCO_ 13-5 specifically excludes honorably

<a id="ga52-p698"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=700 printed_page=698 -->

retired ministers from the expectation that they must become
members of the Presbytery in the area they reside.].
**Response:** In correspondence with the Stated Clerk’s
office, we adopted a policy that stated it is up to the will of the
Presbytery body to determine if a candidate should remain in
our Presbytery, even if honorably retired. In this case, we had
a retired minister who lived 2,000 miles away, making
repeated requests of Presbytery even though he had no
involvement in our body. We are willing to modify our
language or rescind the policy if it violates the will of RPR.
**Rationale:** _BCO_ 13-5 does not apply to an honorably
retired TE.

**Response:** At our January 2025 Stated Meeting, we
will rescind this change from our standing rules. Once the
motion carries, I will attach the January 2025 minutes to
reflect the change.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
actions ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Minutes not attached for review.
**2024-02: Jan 23–24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.a.) — Incomplete
record of ordination exam.

**Response:** Amended the minutes on 1/23/23 to:
“MSC to approve his internship at [Church Name Omitted]
in Olde Providence in Charlotte given the satisfactory
testimonials.”
**2024-03: Jan 23–24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.c.(1)(g)) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.
**Response:** Amended the 1/23/23 minutes:
Church History and History of the PCA: questioned were
asked by TE [Name Omitted] and by the floor. MSC to arrest
this portion of the exam.
**2024-04: Jan 23–24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.c.(1)(g)) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.
**Response:** Amended the 1/23/23 minutes:
Church History and History of the PCA: questioned were
asked by TE [Name Omitted] and by the floor. MSC to arrest
this portion of the exam.
**2024-05: Jan 23–24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.c.(1)(g)) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.
**Response:** Amended the 1/23/23 minutes:

<a id="ga52-p699"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=701 printed_page=699 -->

Church History and History of the PCA: questioned were
asked by TE [Name Omitted] and by the floor. MSC to arrest
this portion of the exam.
**2024-06: Apr 24–25, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4) — Incomplete record
of ordination exam.

**Response:** Amended the 4/24/23 minutes:
MSC to approve his internship at [Church Name Omitted] in
Las Cruces, NM given satisfactory testimonials from TE

[Name Omitted] as to the completion of his internship.
**2024-07: Apr 24–25, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-2) — Non-members of
the Presbytery listed among the quorum for the ordination
commission.

**Response:** Struck both TE [Name Omitted] and TE

[Name Omitted] from the minutes as commissioner members
of the commission.

**2024-08: Apr 24–25, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-6) — Ordination
questions to the congregation wrongly asked of a
congregation of which the TEs are not ministers.
**Response:** I acknolwedge the error. Since the
ordination has already taken place, we have no way to rectify
this error. We will, in the future, be more careful.
**2024-09: May 21, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-12) — No record of
required 10-day notice for scheduling a called meeting.
**Response:** The minutes did not include the call letter,
which I have attached to the minutes now. The call letter

originally went out on May 09, 2023 (Attachment 3)
**2024-10: May 21, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1, 7, 8; _BCO_ 21-1) —
Called meeting is out of order.
**Response:** We acknowledge the error. Given the time
gap, we have no avenue to correct the action. There also have
been no complaints from the Presbytery nor the congregation
regarding this matter.
**2024-11: Sep 25–26, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-7) — Required items for
dismissal of a candidate not recorded.

**Response:** Amended the minutes:
Andy Herman no longer under care ( _BCO_ 18-7). Mr. [Name
Omitted] was under care of the Rio Grande Presbytery and a
member at [Church Name Omitted]. Mr. [Name Omitted] has
since changed his theological convictions regarding

<a id="ga52-p700"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=702 printed_page=700 -->

Presbyterianism and has joined a Baptist church. The Session
at [Church Name Omitted] unanimously agreed to release Mr.

[Name Omitted] from being under care.
**2024-12: Sep 25–26, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1, 7, 8; _BCO_ 21-1) —
Presbytery is not allowed to ordain a minister prior to taking
action on an in order call from a congregation.
**Response:** We acknowledge the error and the
minutes from [Church Name Omitted] congregational
meeting are attached that show that the both TE [Name
Omitted] and TE [Name Omitted]’s calls were dissolved and
reissued (Attachment 4).
**2024-13: Sep 25–26, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record
presbytery cited church to appear at presbytery to agree or
challenge TE’s resignation.
**Response:** We acknoledge the error that we did not
cite the church to appear at Presbytery to agree or challenge
TE [Name Omitted]’s resignation. Given that this resignation
was over a year ago, we do not see a way to correct the action.
I have attached the minutes from [Church Name Omitted]
where they voted in favor to dissolve the pastoral relatinoship
with TE [Name Omitted].
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**

Standards as more than semantic but not striking at the vitals

Meeting.”

stated difference in the man’s own words.

Minutes from the Acheson Commission and the

Commission's Full Statement of the Case will be attached to
this Response to Exceptions for RPR review at the 2025 52nd
General Assembly. (Attachments: Acheson Commission
Report, and Acheson Full Statement)
**2024-04: Apr 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 34-10) — No record of advance
notice to minister or of 2/3 vote to divest him of his office

without censure.

**Response:** Rocky Mountain Presbytery agrees that
there is no record o fa 2/3 vote being taken. RMP apologizes
for the error and promises to be more careful in the future.
Rocky Mountain Presbytery disagrees that there is no
record of advance notice as required by _BCO_ 34-10. Minutes
from the 2022 Fall Stated Meeting on Oct. 6, 2022 record
approval by presbytery to send letters regarding TE [Name

<a id="ga52-p703"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=705 printed_page=703 -->

Omitted] and others. Here is a copy of those minutes: “Moved
and Carried that Presbytery send letters to the following: TEs

[Names Omitted] informing them that at the following
Presbytery meeting the question of divesting them of their
office without censure ( _BCO_ 34-10) will be voted on.” A 2/3
vote to approve the divestiture of TE [Name Omitted]
occurred at the April 25, 2024 Stated Meeting and is recorded
in those minutes.

**2024-06: Oct 5, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
Congregation or Session concurred with dissolutions of
pastoral relations.
**Response:** Rocky Mountain Presbytery agrees with
this exception and corrects its record (if possible), corrects its
actions (if possible) and promises to be more careful in the
future.

TE [Name Omitted]’s situation is described in the
previous response, but there was no record of the Session
approving the dissolution of pastoral relations. TE [Name
Omitted]’s change of call was to Honorably Retired and the
dissolution of the pastoral relationship with the Session of

[Church Name Omitted] was not received or noted.

**2021-02: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; _BCO_ 40-1) —
Incomplete record of review of records of church Sessions.
(Received and reviewed 7 churches. At Spring Stated
Meeting, Session record of churches north of Denver are
reviewed. (SR1.072) No report of reviewed records at Fall
Stated Meeting. At Fall Stated Meeting, Session records of
churches in and south of Denver are reviewed. (SR 1.072)
(Please refer to the Clerk’s Report for responses previously
submitted that the General Assembly found unsatisfactory,
along with their rationale.).
**Response:** Rocky Mountain Presbytery agrees with
the exception and will correct our record (if possible), will
correct our action (if possible), and promises to be more
careful in the future.

<a id="ga52-p704"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=706 printed_page=704 -->

**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). The Presbytery should act to include
appropriate documentation in its record.
**Response:** Rocky Mountain Presbytery respectfully
asks for clarification to what year these exceptions took place
so corrections can be made appropriately.
**Rationale:** This 2022 General exception regards the
RMP minutes of 2021 (7 of 35 churches reviewed, pp. 42, 70).
**Response:** Rocky Mountain Presbytery agrees with
this exception and corrects its record (if possible), corrects its
actions (if possible) and promises to be more careful in the
future.In reviewing Presbytery minutes for 2021, we found
that of the 35 churches in the directory for that year 12
churches submitted minutes for review, 20 churches did not
submit records for review, and 3 churches have since
dissolved. RMP will request 2021 records for review from the
20 churches not previously reviewed.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted or
noted that the 2021 session minutes have been received and

reviewed.

**2024-03: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-7) — No record that
candidate was notified of the reasons that he was removed

from the roll.

**Response:** Rocky Mountain Presbytery agrees with
this exception and corrects its record (if possible), corrects its
actions (if possible) and promises to be more careful in the
future.

RMP believes the candidates were notified of the reasons for

removal from the roll of Candidates being Under Care of
presbytery, but no record was recorded in the minutes that the
presbytery was properly informed. RMP promises to be more
careful in the future.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery needs to amend their
minutes and note why candidate was removed

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-01: Jan 28–29, 2022** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Stated differences not recorded in the candidate’s own words.

**Response:** The incoming applicant for licensure, an
ordained PCA TE from another Presbytery, was seeking to
serve as a stated supply fora particular church within the
Presbytery and was therefore examined for licensure (per
_BCO_ 19-1, 19-2). He did not submit stated differences.
However, in the course of his licensure examination, both in
the committee and on the floor, touching upon his views in
theology, it became clear in the judgment of the Presbytery
that his view of the acceptability of liturgical dance in worship
did not conform to the teaching of _WCF_ 21. In response to
extra-confessional views orally expressed by the applicant on
the floor, the Presbytery took action to restrict his teaching
concerning the elements acceptable in worship, so that it
conforms to _WCF_ 21.3-5 as long as he’s engaged in the
ministry of the word within the bounds of our Presbytery.
While the Presbytery did examine and probe the applicant’s
views beyond written materials provided, and while the

<a id="ga52-p707"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=709 printed_page=707 -->

Presbytery did take measures to seek to guard the confessional
integrity of the teaching within her bounds, we do
acknowledge our failure to record any differences orally
expressed in the applicant’s own words. We will be more
diligent to document any differences discovered during
exams, and we will be careful to record such matters more
thoroughly.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
actions ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
candidate’s stated differences in his own words for review.

**Response:** I was introduced to liturgical dance at

[Church Name Omitted], Birmingham, Alabama, in a preGeneral Assembly evening worship service in the 1980s.
Liturgical dance in the art form of ballet is the use of the Godcreated body to express the beauty, majesty, grace and
holiness of God. On the basis of 4 passages in Scripture that
either describe or encourage worshipful dance, I believe that
when truly used as a doxological form, liturgical dance falls
within the regulative principle of worship.
**2024-01: General 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-10.a) — No response to the
Assembly concerning the disposition of the exception of
substance to which the response to the 50th GA was found to
be unsatisfactory.
**Response:** At our 129th stated meeting on 1/26/2024,
we approved responses to five exceptions of substance cited
by the 50th GA, to be submitted to the RPR committee, but
inadvertently omitted in our responses the other exception to
which our previous response had been found unsatisfactory.
At our next stated meeting (130th) on 4/25/2024, we
approved the new response to this missing exception of
substance, where our original response to the 50th GA had
been found to be unsatisfactory. We subsequently submitted
this new response separately to the RPR committee, which
was well after the committee deadline (3/15/2024), for which
we sincerely apologize.
This response was taken up on the floor of the RPR
committee meeting, along with responses to other exceptions
that had already been submitted, and all of them except for
one were recommended to be found satisfactory, which is the

<a id="ga52-p708"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=710 printed_page=708 -->

action the 51st GA took (please see 51st GA minutes, pages
645-7).
We will be careful in the future to submit our responses in a
timely manner so as to avoid any confusion that our late
submission might have caused last year.
**2024-02: General 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-3.c.6) — The minutes do
not list the names of teaching and ruling elders who are
present at any given meeting. The latter is particularly
important should there be a complaint, protest, etc.
**Response:** The Presbytery acknowledges its failure
to provide a list specifying the TEs and REs present at the
128th Stated Meeting. We will amend the minutes to include
a full roll, and will endeavor to include the roll in all future
minutes.

**2024-13: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7; _BCO_ 13-2) — No record
of annual reports from men without call or from some men
serving out of bounds. Further, the reports that are received
come only to a committee with no record that they are shared
with Presbytery, even in summary form.
**Response:** The Presbytery has customarily received
oral reports from such TEs during the time set 8apart at the
beginning of each Stated Meeting for Church Reports and
Prayers of Intercessions. We will review and shore up our
recording practices to receive and clearly document written
annual reports in future minutes.
**2024-14: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-6) — No record of annual
reports from most candidates, and the one report that is
received appears to have been shared only with a committee.
There is no record of Presbytery having heard the report.
**Response:** The Presbytery receives regular annual
reports from candidates. An oral summary of a report from
Mr. [name omitted] (both written and given in person to the
Committee) was summarized on the floor the Presbytery from
the C&C Committee at the 128th Meeting (Sept 28, 2023),
though we failed to note it in the minutes. We will amend our
minutes and record the reception of such reports in the future.
Other candidates under care in 2023 were either removed

from being under care upon their request during the year (see
127.26), or examined for ordination during the year (see

<a id="ga52-p709"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=711 printed_page=709 -->

127.27). We will be sure to document more clearly the
reception and sharing of such annual reports going forward.
**2024-03: Jan 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-7; _BCO_ 19-10) —
Presbytery establishes an internship for a man but there is no
record of how that internship will fulfill _BCO_ 19-7, para 3, or
that the steps in _BCO_ 19-10 were taken.
**Response:** The Presbytery acknowledges that it did
not adequately fulfill all the formal steps for establishing Mr.

[name omitted] as an intern of the Presbytery according to
_BCO_ 19-7 and 19-10. The nature of Mr. [name omitted]’s
internship, and how it would fulfill the _BCO_ 19-7 (para 3),
was discussed at the Committee level and widely known
among the body, but we failed to articulate it clearly on the
floor of Presbytery. The prayer and address of _BCO_ 19-10 was
properly undertaken at Presbytery, but not properly recorded
in the minutes. We will correct these omissions, and we will
endeavor in the future to both carefully attend to the
requirements for establishing interns and the recording of the
process in our minutes.
**2024-04: Jan 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4.g) — Presbytery granted a
doctrinal exception that appears to be out of accord with the
fundamentals of our doctrinal standards. This statement seems

to say we can make “images” that picture the incarnate second
person of the Trinity, even “placing them in a worship space,”
so long as they are not made an object of worship.
**Response:** At a prior stated meeting of the Presbytery
(September 22, 2022), the transfer applicant submitted the
following stated difference with _WLC_ 109, which the
Presbytery ruled as “not out of accord with any fundamental
of our system of doctrine” [per _RAO_ 16-3.e.5.c]
_WLC_ 109 – I do not believe the making of any
representation of the Son of God is prohibited by Scripture as
the Divines state. Specifically, I do not believe Scripture
prohibits representations of Jesus for educational or artistic
purposes. The incarnation raises questions about the
representation of the Son of God which cannot adequately be
answered simply by an appeal to passages like Deuteronomy
4:15-19 (since in the case of Jesus the point would be
precisely that God did appear in the “likeness of sinful flesh,”

<a id="ga52-p710"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=712 printed_page=710 -->

to quote Paul). Moreover, the second commandment has
reference to the narrow issue of worshipping by means of any
image of any created thing, not simply the making of images
per se. On several occasions God actually commands the
making of images, often to be placed in the very space in
which worship occurs (Ex 25:18, 31-33; 28:33; 1 Kgs 7:29; 2
Chr 4:3-4). Thus, the prohibition in view is not the making of
images, or even placing them in a worship space, but more
narrowly the use of any image as the object of worship.
The applicant submitted his exception, as pertaining
only to the prohibition of the making images of created things,
citing several instances in the Scriptures where images of
created things were placed in the temple. Since then, he, now
as a member of this Presbytery, acknowledged the poor
wording in his original stated difference that led to confusion
and clarified that his stated difference does not extend to the

image of Jesus being “placed in a worship space”.
He submitted the following clarification:
“I don’t have any objection to images of CREATED
things in the worship space for decorative purposes or to
create a worship aesthetic as was done in both tabernacle and
temple, and most church buildings I would think. I think RPR
misunderstood me to be saying that I thought it was therefore
okay to put an image of JESUS in the worship space. I actually
don't think that’s wise as it could encourage the use of an
image of Jesus as an object of worship or veneration, and it
distracts from the central place of the Word in worship ... I
don’t believe images of Jesus should be placed in the worship
space, and that my stated difference had to do merely with
representations of Jesus for artistic or educational purposes.”
At the 132nd stated meeting on January 24th, 2025,
the Presbytery judged this to be more than semantic, but not
out of accord with any fundamental of our system of doctrine.
**2024-05: Jan 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-1) — A commission, acting
as the session of a mission church, votes to call a man as their
pastor. They then offer him employment as directory of
ministry until he can complete his ordination examination.
There is no evidence that Presbytery ever granted the man
permission to move onto the field prior to his examination.

<a id="ga52-p711"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=713 printed_page=711 -->

**Response:** According to the June 29, 2022, minutes
of the Oversight Commission, which was appointed by the
Presbytery, the commission did not vote to call Mr. [name
omitted] as the church’s pastor but to simply approve the
future call package in order to present it to the upcoming
Presbytery. Mr. [name omitted] was fulfilling his internship at
the church as a licentiate preparing for ordination. The
commission also offered Mr. [name omitted] employment that
is consistent with the internship that he was already fulfilling
at the church toward his ordination, which they believed
would not require the Presbytery's permission.
**2024-06: Jan 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 46-6) — No record regarding
the reception of a certificate of dismission from transferring
presbytery.
**Response:** We acknowledge our failure to document
the certificate of dismission from the transferring presbytery.
We will amend our minutes accordingly, and will endeavor to
clearly record the reception of future transfer
communications.
**2024-08: Apr 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19) — Improper approval of
internship requirement.
**Response:** We acknowledge our inadequate
documentation of the fulfilling of internship requirements by
the candidate. We will provide fuller documentation in our
minutes of satisfactory meeting of specific requirements in
our future record keeping.
**2024-10: Apr 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1) — Presbytery receives an
oral report from a commission, but no report of the
commission is included in the minutes.

**Response:** The commission reports received at this
meeting were provided in the attachments to our minutes.
**2024-11: Sep 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of six month
church membership for candidate or that his application was
filed one month in advance.

**Response:** The Presbytery acknowledges its failure
to clearly document the satisfactory meeting of requirements
in receiving a candidate under care. Mr. [name omitted] was
indeed a member of [Church Name Omitted] for more than
six-months at the time of his reception under care.

<a id="ga52-p712"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=714 printed_page=712 -->

Additionally, the candidate’s application was received the
month before (August) the September Presbytery meeting.
We will amend our minutes to clarify the satisfactory meeting
of these requirements.
**2024-12: Sep 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-7) — No record of proper
process for candidate being received from another presbytery.
**Response:** The Presbytery acknowledges that it did
not express in the minutes its intention to communicate with
Philadelphia Presbytery that Mr. [name omitted] be received
by Siouxlands upon receipt of certificate of dismission from
them. The communication (and reception o fa certificate of
dismission) did take place. We will amend our minutes
accordingly, and will endeavor to make the process of such
future transfers clear in our minutes.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-07: Apr 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-7) — Apparent improper
removal of candidates from the roll.

**Response:** The Presbytery removed the candidates
from the roll of candidates at their request per _BCO_ 18-7, but
we acknowledge our failure to provide documentation that the
sufficient reasons were reported to the Presbytery to act on the
Committee’s recommendation for removal.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted record
of the candidate’s request to be removed from the roll.
**2024-09: Apr 27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — Presbytery transferred
a TE with no record of first dissolving his call or citing his
church to appear.
**Response:** Since the request for dissolution of a
pastoral relation was an uncontested one, it was handled by
the Church and Ministerial Welfare Committee of the
Presbytery, acting as a commission (per our By-law 1.054.e.1)
during its meeting on 4/5/23, when both the TE and the church
representative appeared to present their case, following a
unanimous, uncontested congregational meeting of the church
on 3/26/23 accepting the TE’s resignation. The record of the

<a id="ga52-p713"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=715 printed_page=713 -->

commission’s action was provided as an attachment to our
minutes.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes of the congregational meeting at which the TE’s
resignation was accepted

**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2021-02: Jan 25, 2020** ( _BCO_ 19-2.a) — No exam/statement
of Christian experience and inward call.
**Response:** Our ordination team has historically
received a statement of Christian experience and inward call
when candidates come under care in the presbytery. In the
future, we will make sure this is added to licensure candidates
as well.
**Rationale:** When GA takes exception to a
Presbytery's minutes for failure to record or take a required
action, and Presbytery agrees with the exception, a
satisfactory response should indicate that they have taken the
required action, if possible, and properly recorded the same in
their minutes, amending and resubmitting relevant portions of
past minutes if necessary.
**Response:** The candidate came under care of the
Chicago Metro Presbytery in 2018, during his examination the
committee received and reviewed the material from the

<a id="ga52-p714"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=716 printed_page=714 -->

Chicago Metro Presbytery. We apologize for this error, and
have amended our minutes to reflect this portion of the exam.
**2021-03: Jan 25, 2020** ( _BCO_ 13-6; _BCO_ 21-4) — Incomplete
transfer exam (no theology, Bible content or Sacraments).
**Response:** Our ordination team recognizes and
agrees with this error and will remedy it in future transfer

exams.
**Rationale:** When GA takes exception to a
Presbytery's minutes for failure to record or take a required
action, and Presbytery agrees with the exception, a
satisfactory response should indicate that they have taken the
required action, if possible, and properly recorded the same in
their minutes, amending and resubmitting relevant portions of
past minutes if necessary.
**Response:** We recognize this error, and have
amended our minutes to reflect the transfer exam to include

the topic of the Sacraments, which was not listed.
**2021-04: Jul 23, 2019** ( _BCO_ 13-6) — Incomplete record of
transfer exam (no record of questions on views of sacraments
or church government).
**Response:** Our ordination team recognizes and
agrees with this oversight and will work to conduct a complete
transfer exam in the future.
**Rationale:** When GA takes exception to a
Presbytery's minutes for failure to record or take a required
action, and Presbytery agrees with the exception, a
satisfactory response should indicate that they have taken the
required action, if possible, and properly recorded the same in
their minutes, amending and resubmitting relevant portions of
past minutes if necessary.
**Response:** We recognize this error, and apologize
that due to the length of time for this special meeting, the
minutes cannot be located. We will due better in the future to

improve this process.
**2021-05: Jan 25, 2020** ( _BCO_ 19-7) — No indication of Mr.

[name omitted] having completed his presbytery internship
prior to ordination.
**Response:** Our ordination team agrees with this
oversight and will work to insure it is completed in the future.

<a id="ga52-p715"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=717 printed_page=715 -->

**Rationale:** When GA takes exception to a
Presbytery's minutes for failure to record or take a required
action, and Presbytery agrees with the exception, a
satisfactory response should indicate that they have taken the
required action, if possible, and properly recorded the same in
their minutes, amending and resubmitting relevant portions of
past minutes if necessary.
**Response:** We recognize this error, and it has been
amended in our minutes to reflect the approval of the
candidates internship in his previous presbytery.
**2021-06: Jan 25, 2020** ( _BCO_ 21-4) — No record that
theological thesis or exegetical papers submitted.
**Response:** Our ordination team agrees with this
oversight and will work to insure it is completed in the future.
**Rationale:** When GA takes exception to a
Presbytery's minutes for failure to record or take a required
action, and Presbytery agrees with the exception, a
satisfactory response should indicate that they have taken the
required action, if possible, and properly recorded the same in
their minutes, amending and resubmitting relevant portions of
past minutes if necessary.
**Response:** We recognize this error, and it has been
amended in our minutes to reflect the approval of the
candidates exegetical papers.
**2022-03: Apr 24, 2021** ( _BCO_ 34-4) — A minister was found
contumacious, but Presbytery did not make “record… of the
fact and of the charges under which he was arraigned.”.
**Response:** We apologize for this error, the record of
charges will be included in the future. This meeting had two
depositions, and this pastor was also deposed at a later
meeting. We will ensure this is carried out properly in the
future.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
required records for review
**Response:** We recognize this error, and that _BCO_ 344 does stipulate the proper recording of charges under which
he was arraigned, we have located the original charges, and

<a id="ga52-p716"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=718 printed_page=716 -->

they are included here as an attachment, and have been added
to the minutes.
**2023-01: Jan 25, 2022; Apr 26, 2022; Sep 27, 2022** ( _BCO_
21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — Stated differences not recorded in the
minister’s/candidate’s own words.

**Response:** The Presbytery apologizes for not
recording the stated differences of minister’s/candidates in
their own words in the minutes of the meeting for 1/25/22,
4/26/22, and 9/27/22. We will adjust our process to include all
stated differences with the Confessional Standards
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
candidates’ stated differences in their own words for review.

**Response:** We acknowledge this error, our process
has changed to include all exceptions in our minutes, and
these missing exceptions in the candidates own words have
been added to amend our minutes. Here are the presbytery
approved exceptions for the meetings listed.

**Jan 25, 2022- TE [Name Omitted]-**
_WCF_ 21.2 _WCF_ 21.8, _WLC_ 117, _WSC_ 60- I do not
believe that Scripture requires. Sabbath rest from “words and
thoughts about their worldly employments and recreations.” I
believe that God made the Sabbath for man (Mk. 2:27) to
teach us to consecrate our earthly work, vocations, and
enjoyments to him (1 Cor. 10:31). Scripture seems to teach
that rest is the ultimate end of the Sabbath and that man, being
made in the image of God, is to remember our salvation in
him and to enjoy the benefits of our redemption in him
through acts of mercy, fellowship with the body, and
enjoyment of his creation on the Lord’s day.
_WLC_ 109- In forbidding the “making any
representation of God, of all or any of the three persons, either
inwardly in our mind, or outwardly in any kind of image or
likeness of any creature whatsoever,” i believe the l cis
taking the second commandment too literally, overlooking the
intent of the command, which is to love and fear God above
all else. In our reading of Scripture, the prologue of John,
Colossians 1, or Revelation 19, for example, it seems

<a id="ga52-p717"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=719 printed_page=717 -->

impossible not to imagine Christ in our mind. I believe that
the 2nd commandment teaches us not to wrongly ascribe
worship to something that is not God, or in the case of the
Golden calf in Ex. 32-34, to wrongly ascribe worship to a
graven image, even if that image is attempting to capture the
triune God’s nature. Also, the confession would seem to
forbid the making of any symbol ascribed to God worthy of
worship, such as a cross. I do not believe this is the intent of
the 2nd commandment. That being said, i do believe it is
wrong to attempt to contain the first and third members of the
Trinity with imagery since they are invisible in substance.
_WCF_ 21.5- I take singing of psalms to mean the
hymnody of the Psalter and non-Scriptural spiritual songs and
hymns as the Apostle Paul means in Eph. 5:19. CHAR (10)
_WCF_ 24.6- I believe there may be otherwise and apt reasons
that align with the teachings of Scripture for a marriage to end.
In the cases of abuse, i believe this t ob ea violation of the 1st,
5th, and 6th commandments. Particularly, in the case of
repeated abuse, it may be un-biblical for the marriage to
continue, especially after church discipline has been carried
out and the abuser remains un-repentant. In this case, the
abusing spouse has neglected their duty to their family and
according to Eph. 5-6 and violated their marriage covenant. In
this case, I believe divorce is permissible. There may also be
cases where trust is broken repeatedly by violating the 8th,
9th, and 10th commandments within the marriage and family
that the same principles above are necessary. It also seems
appropriate to note that if one spouse apostatizes from the
faith, divorce may be permissible according to 1 Cor. 7. In
these cases, divorce may be advised to a member by the
session or presbytery.
**April 26, 2022- TE [Name Omitted]-**
It is with gratitude and conviction that I affirm my
subscription to the Westminster Confession of Faith and
Catechisms as containing the system of doctrine taught in the
Holy Scriptures.
In the interest of full candor, I make you aware of the
following:

<a id="ga52-p718"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=720 printed_page=718 -->

1. _WCF_ 21.8 insists that that one’s actions on the

Sabbath “are taken up, the whole time, in the public and
private exercises of his worship.” It seems the Standards say
more about worship and duties and less about rest than do the
Scriptures. Public and private worship must be maintained on
the Lord’s Day as well as other duties of religion, yet to do
them “all the day” and “the whole time” would seem in
tension with the emphasis Scripture places on rest. While
avoiding the idolatry of our commercial culture, which never
pauses for rest or reflection, I believe restorative and recreative activities are not forbidden on the Lord’s Day and can
be understood as fulfilling the requirement of “rest.”
Furthermore, with the finished work of Jesus Christ manifest
in the resurrection from the dead, the New Covenant
community gathers for worship on the first day of the week
now known as “the Lord’s Day” (Rev 1:10). Though not in
tension with the Old Testament Sabbath, the New Covenant
terminology seems more appropriate.
2. _WLC_ 109 forbids the “making any representation
of God...either inwardly in our mind, or outwardly....” Images
of the Triune God which are intentionally or unintentionally
produced or kept as a means of aiding worship (especially in
the sanctuary where such worship is ordinarily conducted) are
disallowed. The second commandment forbids fashioning any
image which believers might bow down to or offer any form
or part of worship. At the same time, in forbidding all mental
representations of God in all contexts and for all purposes,
seems to require what is impossible.
3. I hold that God has chosen to relate with his people
through one overarching covenant of grace, which remains
constant throughout history and that, as the people of God,
Israel and the Church share in the same promises. At the same
time, I hold that on the last day, at the one return of Jesus
Christ, both those found alive along with the redeemed dead
will be raised to life, yet at a later time the unredeemed dead
with be raised for judgment. Because of statements in
Revelation 20:4-5, I understand that _WLC_ 87-88, which
speaks of the “general resurrection of the dead, both of the just
and the unjust,” may be temporally separate events. Though

<a id="ga52-p719"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=721 printed_page=719 -->

much of the language in Revelation is symbolic, the
description of two resurrections in Revelation 20 lead met oa
“historic premillennial” position. Citation J. Gresham
Machen (“Premillennialism,” The Presbyterian Guardian 3/2

[24 October 1936], 45) argues:
“Subscription to the Westminster Standards in the
Presbyterian Church of America [e.g., OPC] is not to every
word in those Standards, but only to the system of doctrine
which the Standards contain. The real question, then, is
whether a person who holds the Premillennial view can hold
that system. Can a person who holds the Premillennial view
be a true Calvinist; can he, in other words, hold truly to the
Calvinistic or Reformed system of doctrine which is set forth
in the Westminster Standards? We think that he can; and for
that reason we think that Premillennialists as well as those

who hold the opposing view may become ministers or elders
or deacons in The Presbyterian Church of America. We think
that a man who holds that the return of Christ and the final

judgment take place not in one act, as the Westminster
Standards contemplate them as doing, but in two acts with a
thousand-year reign of Christ upon the earth in between, yet
may honestly say that he holds the system of doctrine that the
Standards contain.”
**Sept 27, 2022- TE [Name Omitted]-**
I take one exception to the Westminster Confession
of Faith on the issue of recreation on the Sabbath. _WCF_

Section 21.8 reads:

This Sabbath is then kept holy unto the Lord, when men, after
a due preparing of their hearts, and ordering of their common
affairs beforehand, do not only observe an holy rest all the day
from their own works, words, and thoughts, about their
worldly employments and **recreations; but also are taken**
**up the whole time in the public and private exercises of his**
**worship, and in the duties of necessity and mercy.**
I believe that the intent of the Scriptures regarding the
Sabbath is not to prevent recreational bodily activity that
might further our rest on Sabbath and enjoyment of the Lord.
The Confession grounds its reasoning for disallowing
recreation on a misreading of the words “doing your pleasure”

<a id="ga52-p720"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=722 printed_page=720 -->

found in Isaiah 58:13. “Doing your pleasure” in the context of
Isaiah is referencing keeping one’s business open or not
letting their fields rest. It has to do with commercial activity
and not recreational activity. Thus, while our time in public
and private worship ought to orient the way we spend our time
on the Sabbath, I see nothing in the Scriptures which would
prevent a christian from lawfully taking a walk, hike, or
engaging in any other refreshing recreational activity,
especially when in the company of fellow saints.

been amended in our minutes.

required record for review.

Commission minutes dated 2022-09- Admin Commission

Minutes.

the meeting is complete. We will make the language more

**2021-01: Jan 26, 2019; May 4, 2019; Sep 24, 2019** ( _BCO_ 151; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes of commission not entered in
Presbytery minutes, and these must be presented for review
next year.
**Response:** These minutes were received by our
administrative commission and reported to the presbytery, but
we failed to enter them into the minutes. We will revise our

processes to include them in the future.
**Rationale:** When GA takes exception to a
Presbytery's minutes for failure to record or take a required
action, and Presbytery agrees with the exception, a
satisfactory response should indicate that they have taken the
required action, if possible, and properly recorded the same in
their minutes, amending and resubmitting relevant portions of
past minutes if necessary.
**Response:** We recognize this error, however due to
the length of time the Administration Commission minutes
from 2019 cannot be located. We've amended our process, and
all Administration Commission minutes are located in the

correct location, and will be included with the minutes of the
stated meetings, and submitted to General Assembly for this
upcoming year 2025.
**Rationale:** Presbytery has not provided minutes for
review. Presbytery needs to do its best to recreate the missing
minutes and produce them per _BCO_ 40-4.
**2022-01: General 2021** ( _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Commission
minutes not provided.
**Response:** Commission minutes will be provided in
the future.

<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=726 printed_page=724 -->

**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review.

**Response:** We are unable to produce the commission
minutes requested from 2019, and we recognize this error and
apologize.
**Rationale:** Presbytery has not provided minutes for
review. Presbytery needs to do its best to recreate the missing
minutes and produce them per _BCO_ 40-4.
**2024-06: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _BCO_ 10-5; 13-11; _RAO_
16-3; RONR 9:30-36) — The Administration Commission
minutes do not conform to the requirements of the _BCO_ and
_RAO_ . Further, the Administration Commission takes actions
(approving and dissolving calls, establishing commissions,
etc) via “electronic correspondence.” This does not constitute
a deliberative assembly. Further, there is no evidence that
SCP’s ByLaws allow electronic meetings, that quorums were
present when those decisions were made, or that such actions
were ever appropriately ratified.
**Response:** We apologize for this oversight, and while
the bylaws do not outline how electronic meeting are to take
place, this has been our practice from the beginning of the
Admin Commission. We will work to bring the
Administration Commission Minutes into the format required
by the _BCO_ and _RAO_ .
**Rationale:** No corrective action has been taken also

as a commission this need to bead eli be rat ive process and not
just emails.
**2024-05: Jan 24–25, 2023** ( _BCO_ 31-2) — Presbytery treats a
_BCO_ 31-2 report as a motion and defeats the motion to begin
an investigation. _BCO_ 31-2 requires at least some
investigation and that Presbytery demand satisfactory
explanations from the one against whom the report is made.
There is no record that this happened.
**Response:** The motion was made by a TE, and
defeated, the TE subsequently demitted from office, and has
left the PCA, no explination was deemed necessary by the
Presbytery, the report was slanderous in nature.

<a id="ga52-p725"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=727 printed_page=725 -->

**Rationale:** _BCO_ 31-2 calls for some degree of
investigation and a ruling that there iso ri snot a strong
presumption of guilt. Minutes need to reflect this decision and
not simply be silent.

Incomplete record of examination for minister seeking
admission from another denomination.

however, this was a transfer from the KAPC, a NAPARC
denomination, the exam was thorough, and covered all areas
of our theology, we apologize for the lack of detail in the
minutes, and while we can amend the minutes, and have, the
TE is already ordained and serving in good standing in our
presbytery.
**Rationale:** The record needs to reflect this was

handled in accordance to _BCO_ 13-6 and the record needs to

be corrected.

**2024-12: Jul 17, 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-3.e.7; _BCO_ 13-11) —

submitted next year.

Note to Clerk.

**Rationale:** Minutes of executive sessions are

required to be provided for review.

not attached to the minutes.

**Response:** Our process has been improved to include
the commission reports.
**Rationale:** Reports needed to be provided for the
year in question in addition to procedure moving forward.
**2024-14: Sep 26–27, 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — Presbytery, on
motion, dissolves a man’s call to an out-of-bounds work
without the concurrence of the man or the calling body, or
even evidence of communication with the calling body, and
with no evidence of formal process.

ongoing matter that is yet to be resolved.

<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=728 printed_page=726 -->

exceptions, we apologize for this oversight, and have
amended our minutes.

determining exceptions to standards. The record must reflect

74. That the Minutes of **South Florida Presbytery:** _59-0-0_

candidates and not included.
**2025-02: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 19-12) — Annual report of
interns not included.
**2025-03: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 19-12) — Reports on every

at each stated meeting not included.
**2025-04: General 2024** ( _BCO_ 20-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.6) —
