**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2022-07: General 2021** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b, 40-1) — No record of
review of Session minutes.

**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with the exception
and regrets the error. The Presbytery has created an ad-hoc
committee to review Session minutes. We will be more

diligent to handle this issue correctly in the future.
**Rationale:** No record of review of any Session
records from the period cited.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets this mistake in recordkeeping. Presbytery completed
its review of Session Minutes at its November 12, 2024 stated
meeting, record of which is here submitted. Presbytery
commits to doing this each moving forward.
**2023-04: May 10, 2022** ( _BCO_ 24-4.f.) — Presbytery
implicitly granted a doctrinal exception that needs further
clarity for proper review. Mr. [name omitted] appears to
advocate for paedo-communion ( _WLC_ 177).

<a id="ga52-p729"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=731 printed_page=729 -->

**Response:** Presbytery regrets this lack of clarity in
full recordkeeping. It is correct that Mr. [Name Omitted] gave
a stated difference with the Westminster Standards in line

with what is called “paedocommunion” (though Mr. [Name
Omitted] did not use this term). The Presbytery questioned
him thoroughly about this view, especially as it touched other
doctrines in our confessional system and “Federal Vision
theology.” The Presbytery was satisfied with his answers,
especially his commitment not to promote this view or
practice it. Since the Presbytery has allowed for this exception
in the past, it was deemed acceptable still. We will strive to
record the full extent of the Presbytery’s examination of its
candidates’ stated differences with the Standards.
**Rationale:** Presbytery needs to demonstrate how his
views are not hostile to our system of doctrine.
**Response:** Presbytery judged this particular
exception from this particular candidate as a difference more
than semantic but not hostile to our system or striking at the
vitals of religion for a number of reasons. The candidate
affirmed that his view came from within the PCA, especially
from reading the minority report of Robert Rayburn, who
remained a minister in good standing in the denomination. His
difference was not influenced by disagreement with other
fundamentals of our system—e.g., justification by faith alone,
the efficacious reception of the sacraments as dependent upon
personal faith, and the obligation of individual selfexamination. Further, the candidate promised to submit to the
_BCO_ requirements and not practice his view. He expressed his
desire to maintain peace and unity and would not cause
dissension on this topic. Presbytery argued in accordance with
the denomination’s prior allowance of this exception in this
manner (not practicing or pushing it). On a similar issue RPR
previously referred to _BCO_ 34-5, which allows for such
differences if they are not “industriously spread” and “are not
likely to do much injury” (43rd General Assembly, pp. 422–
23). Presbytery believes such pertains in this case.
**2024-15: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; 40-1) — No record of
review of records of church Sessions.

<a id="ga52-p730"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=732 printed_page=730 -->

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets this mistake in recordkeeping. Presbytery completed
its review of Session Minutes at its November 12, 2024 stated
meeting, record of which is here submitted. Presbytery
commits to doing this each moving forward.
**2024-01: Feb 8, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of
endorsement by candidate’s Session.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets this mistake in recordkeeping. The Session
endorsements of these candidates are on file, though
regretfully not recorded in the original minutes. Presbytery
has corrected the record by a motion to Amend Something
Previously Adopted and has made a marginal notation in those
minutes pointing to this response (attached). We will be more
diligent to record this in future minutes.
**2024-02: Feb 8, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of six-months
membership for candidate.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets this mistake in recordkeeping. Each of these
candidates have been members of a PCA church for six
months in keeping with _BCO_ 18-2, though regretfully it was
not recorded. Presbytery has corrected the record by a motion
to Amend Something Previously Adopted and has made a
marginal notation in those minutes pointing to this response
(attached). We will be more diligent to record the six-month
memberships of candidates in future minutes.
**2024-03: Nov 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of
endorsement by candidate’s Session.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets this mistake in recordkeeping. The Session
endorsement of this candidate is on file, though regretfully it
was not recorded. Presbytery has corrected the record by a
motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted and has
made a marginal notation in those minutes pointing to this
response (attached). We will be more diligent to record
candidates' session endorsements in future minutes.

**2024-04: Nov 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.

<a id="ga52-p731"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=733 printed_page=731 -->

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with this exception and
regrets the error. The "examination for Church History" in the
record did include a distinct exam on the history of the PCA,
though it was not specifically recorded as such. Presbytery has
corrected the record by a motion to Amend Something
Previously Adopted and has made a marginal notation in those
minutes pointing to this response (attached). We will be more
diligent to record each of the requirements for examination in
future minutes.

**2024-05: Feb 8, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes
of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes, and these
must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. Presbytery has corrected the record by a
motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted to append
the commission minutes to the February 8, 2023 stated
meeting (attached) with a marginal note pointing to this
response. We will be more diligent to include commission
minutes in the future.

**2024-06: Feb 8, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes
of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes, and these
must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. Presbytery has corrected the record by a
motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted to append
the commission minutes to the February 8, 2023 stated
meeting (attached) with a marginal note pointing to this
response. We will be more diligent to include commission
minutes in the future.

**2024-07: Feb 8, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes
of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes, and these
must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees that an error was made
here, but it was a typo, not missing commission minutes.
There was no commission to ordain Juan Jose Cotto; he was
ordained on the floor of presbytery at the 2/8/2022 stated
meeting. Presbytery has corrected the record by a motion to
Amend Something Previously Adopted with a marginal

<a id="ga52-p732"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=734 printed_page=732 -->

notion pointing to this response. Presbytery apologizes for this
error in record keeping.
**2024-08: May 13, 2023** ( _BCO_ 38-1) — Full confession for a
38-2 case without process not recorded in the minutes.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. Presbytery has corrected the record by a
motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted to append
the full confession statement to the May 13, 2023 stated
meeting (attached) with a marginal note pointing to this
response. We will be more diligent to include full confessions
in 38-2 cases in the future.
**2024-09: Aug 8, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-6) — Incomplete record of
examination of TE transferring into Presbytery. No exam of
views touching on theology, the Sacraments, or church
government.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. The minutes record that TE [Name Omitted]
was examined on his Christian experience and his call to
preach the Gospel. Though unfortunately not recorded in the
original minutes, he was also examined on his views in
theology, Sacraments, and church government by the
Credentialing Committee, who then recommended him to
Presbytery. The TE also answered questions on the floor of
Presbytery, but this was not recorded in the minutes.
Presbytery has corrected the record by a motion to Amend
Something Previously Adopted and has made a marginal
notation in those minutes pointing to this response (attached).
The Presbytery apologizes for this oversight in recordkeeping,
and we will be more diligent to record all examination
requirements in the future.
**2024-10: Aug 8, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — All
specific requirements of licensure exam not recorded. No
record of written sermon.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. Each element of the licensure exam was
conducted by the Credentialing Committee, including the
submission of a written sermon, but each element was
unfortunately not recorded in the minutes. Presbytery has
corrected the record by a motion to Amend Something

<a id="ga52-p733"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=735 printed_page=733 -->

Previously Adopted and has made a marginal notation in those
minutes pointing to this response. Presbytery will be more
diligent to record all examination requirements in the future.
**2024-11: Nov 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam, specifically:
educational requirements, knowledges of Greek and Hebrew
languages, and exegetical papers. Additionally, no record of
completion and approval of an internship.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. Each element of the ordination exam was
conducted by the Credentialing Committee (including
educational requirements, knowledge of biblical languages,
and exegetical papers), but each element was unfortunately
not recorded in the minutes. (The completion of the
candidates' internships was approved on page 1, lines 33 and
35 of the minutes.) Presbytery has corrected the record by a
motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted and has
made a marginal notation in those minutes pointing to this
response. Presbytery will be more diligent to record all
elements of examination requirements in the future.
**2024-12: Nov 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.6) —
Specific arrangements of call not shown to have been
approved.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. The specific arrangements of the two
candidates' calls were reviewed and approved, but it was
unfortunately not recorded as such. Presbytery has corrected
the record by a motion to Amend Something Previously
Adopted and has made a marginal notation in those minutes
pointing to this response. Presbytery will be more diligent to
record that specific arrangements of minister calls are
approved in the future.
**2024-13: Nov 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4) — Incomplete record of
ordination exam, specifically: theological and exegetical

papers.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. Each element of the ordination exam was
conducted by the Credentialing Committee (including
theological and exegetical papers), but each element was not

<a id="ga52-p734"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=736 printed_page=734 -->

recorded in the minutes. Presbytery has corrected the record
by a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted and has
made a marginal notation in those minutes pointing to this
response. Presbytery will be more diligent to record all
elements of examination requirements in the future.
**2024-14: Nov 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — All
specific requirements of licensure exam not recorded. No
written sermon.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
regrets the error. Each element of the licensure exam was
conducted by the Credentialing Committee, including the
submission of a written sermon, but each element was
unfortunately not recorded in the minutes. Presbytery has
corrected the record by a motion to Amend Something
Previously Adopted and has made a marginal notation in those
minutes pointing to this response. Presbytery will be more
diligent to record all elements of examination requirements in
the future.

75. That the Minutes of **South Texas Presbytery:** _64-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **03 Jul, 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **General 2024.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: Jan 26–27, 2024** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.6) —
Stated differences not recorded in the candidate’s own words.

**2025-02: Apr 26–27, 2024** ( _BCO_ 15-2; _RAO_ 14-2) —
Presbytery gave commission-like power to a single individual
to appoint representatives to a Committee of Commissioners.
Representatives to Committees of Commissioners must be
elected by Presbytery or a commission thereof.
**2025-03: Aug 9–10, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-2) — no record oft e
laboring out of baounds with concurrence of Presbytery
within whose bounds he labors.
**2025-04: Oct 25–26, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-6; 19-2.f; 21-4.g; _RAO_
16-3.e.6) — A candidate’s difference with the Standards’
teaching on creation must be stated in the minutes of
Presbytery in the candidate’s own words.
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**

<a id="ga52-p735"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=737 printed_page=735 -->

**2022-01: Apr 23–24, 2021** ( _BCO_ 20-1) — No record of call
to a definite work.

**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets its omission. As reported by the
Candidates Committee, there was a call to a definite work,
however the Clerk neglected to record it. The Presbytery will
ensure recitation of the calls be included in future minutes.
**Rationale:** The minutes need to include appropriate
correction

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that the calls of (a) TE [Name Omitted] to labor out of
bounds under a call in [Church Name Omitted] of Ashburton,
New Zealand, and (b) TE [Name Omitted] to labor out of
bounds as President of Covenant Seminary were received and
reviewed by the Ministerial Relations Committee of the
Presbytery and, being considered valid Christian ministries,
presented to the Presbytery, found in good order, deemed for
the good of the Church under Section 46-6 of the Book of
Church Order, and placed in their hands, who found them
acceptable, and the Stated Clerk has been authorized to make
a marginal notation in the minutes indicating this correction.
**2022-02: Apr 23-24, 2021; Aug 13-14, 2021** ( _BCO_ 21-4;
_RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — All specific requirements of ordination
exam not recorded.

**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets its error. All examination requirements
of _BCO_ 21-4 were met, however, the Clerk failed to record
each detail of examination. The Presbytery will be more
diligent in its record keeping for trials of examination in its
minutes.

**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that all requirements of the ordination examination of
Adam Lopez to the office of Teaching Elder under Section 214 of the Book of Church Order were satisfied, being (I)
fulfilment of the educational requirements of subsections (a)
and (b), including knowledge of the Greek and Hebrew
languages, (II) preaching of a sermon before the Candidates

<a id="ga52-p736"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=738 printed_page=736 -->

and Credentials Committee of the Presbytery, (III)
preparation of exegetical and theological papers, (IV)
satisfaction of examinations of his Christian experience and
in his knowledge of the English Bible, the Book of Church
Order and the government and discipline of the church,
theology, the Sacraments, and Church history and the history
of the Presbyterian Church in America, and (V) an inquiry
into his views on the Confession of Faith and Catechisms and

grants of exceptions to his stated differences, and that all
requirements of the ordination examination of [Name
Omitted] to the office of Teaching Elder under Section 21-4
of the Book of Church Order were satisfied, being (i)
fulfillment of the educational requirements of subsections (a)
and (b), including knowledge of the Greek and Hebrew
languages, (ii) preaching of a sermon before the Candidates
and Credentials Committee of the Presbytery, (iii) preparation
of exegetical and theological papers, (iv) satisfaction of
examinations of his Christian experience and in his
knowledge of the English Bible, the Book of Church Order
and the government and discipline of the church, theology, the
Sacraments, and Church history and the history of the
Presbyterian Church in America, and (v) an inquiry Into his
views on the Confession of Faith and Catechisms and grants
of exceptions to his stated differences, and the Stated Clerk
has been authorized to make a marginal notation in the
minutes indicating these corrections.
**2023-01: General 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — No
minutes of any committee empowered as commissions
submitted for GA review.

**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets this omission. the Stated Clerk has
called for all Commission reports to be turned in to him to be
included in the minutes. Commission minutes will be included

with the 2023 minutes.
**Rationale:** Minutes of past commissions need to be
properly attached to the appropriate minutes and submitted for
review.

**Response:** Not withstanding the response of the
Presbytery to the 50th General Assembly, some, but not all,

<a id="ga52-p737"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=739 printed_page=737 -->

commission reports from 2022 have been received. To the
extent they have been received the minutes have
beencorrected andthe Stated Clerk authorized to make a

marginal notation in the minutes indicating such corrections.
The minutes wil bemaintained intherecords of the Presbytery.
To the extent commission minutes are not available, no
corrections can be made. Beginning in 2024, minutes of
committees actingas commissions, which in some cases are
authorized by the Standing Rules adopted by the full
Presbytery, and of commissionsseparately appointed by the
Presbytery are being includedwiththeminutes.
**2023-03: Jan 28–29, 2022** ( _BCO_ 40-2.1) — Minutes state that
the presbytery voted to receive the commission report to
ordain and install [name omitted], but referenced Attachment
speaks only of a report to Commission to Ordain/Install a Mr.

[name omitted]. Minutes make no mention of Mr. [name
omitted].
**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to reflect
that the "Report of the Commission ofthe South Texas
Presbytery to ordain and install [Name Omitted] as assistant
pastor of [Church Name Omitted] in Borne [sic.]" that was
referenced in the Report of the Stated Clerk should have been
the Report of the Commission to ordain/install Mr. [Name
Omitted] as Pastor of [Church Name Omitted] (PCA) that is
found as Attachment D to the minutes, and the Stated Clerk
hasbeen authorized to make a marginal notation ni the minutes
indicating this correction.
**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that the "Report of the Commission ofthe South Texas
Presbytery to ordain and install [Name Omitted] as assistant
pastor of [Church Name Omitted] in Borne [sic.]" that was
referenced in the Report of the Stated Clerk should have been
the Report of the Commission to ordain/install Mr. [Name
Omitted] MartoranoMendoza as Pastor of [Church Name
Omitted] (PCA) that is found as Attachment D to the minutes,
and the Stated Clerk hasbeen authorized to make a marginal
notation ni the minutes indicating this correction.

<a id="ga52-p738"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=740 printed_page=738 -->

**2023-04: Apr 29-30, 2022; Oct 28-29, 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-1) —
It appears committees are empowered to act as if they were
commissions.

**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets the oversite. RE [Name Omitted]’s
report included only one Committee item which needed
Presbytery approval, and because there was no quorum, a
motion and second was required. Our minutes were unclear
on that point. Our Standing Rules empowers the Ministerial
Relations Committee to act as a commission to dissolve the

pastoral relations of a minister in cases “where the minister
and congregation formally concur in such dissolution and
when pastoral problems are not involved.” The Chairman of
the committee should have asked for Presbytery ratification of
the committee/commission’s actions. As for the report of the
Administrative Committee on the complaint against TE

[Name Omitted], the Standing Rules grant the Committee
authority for initial investigation to determine if the complaint
was in order. This is what happened. The Presbytery regrets
the ambiguity of its minutes. in the case oft e [Name
Omitted]’s theology and exegetical papers, the Stated Clerk
failed to memorialize the motion of the Candidates and

Credentials Committee’s motion to approve those actions.
The Presbytery regrets this oversite and will endeavor to be
more complete in future records keeping.
**Rationale:** There is no record that the relevant

minutes have been corrected.

**Response:** In the case of the April meeting, the
Nominations Committee was not acting as a commission. The
reference to "two items of business" that may have given thie
improssion included one item that required a presbytery vote,
given the committee lacked a quorum, and the presbytery
voted. The second item was an announcement. The Presbytery
therefore does not feel a correction to the minutes is needed.

At the April meeting, the Ministerial Relations Committee
under the Presbytery Standing Rules adopted by the full
Presbytery "has the authority to act as a commission... in
dissolving pastoral relationships... where the minister and
congregation formally concur in such dissolution and ...

<a id="ga52-p739"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=741 printed_page=739 -->

pastoral problems are not involved." The committee was
acting in this capacity, the minutes have been corrected to
reflect this, and the Stated Clerk has been authorized to make
a marginal notation in the minutes indicating this correction.
The questions around the Administrative Committee
actions at the October meeting are addressed in the response
to Exception 76-2023-13.
**2023-05: Apr 29–30, 2022** ( _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — Minutes do not
indicate which areas transfer candidates were examined in.

**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets the oversite. All transfer candidates are
examined in their views covered in _BCO_ 13-6 both by the
Candidates Committee and on the floor of Presbytery. The
Presbytery regrets that our minutes did not include those areas
of examination and will endeavor to include them in future

minutes.

**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that all requirements of the transfer examinations of
Teaching Elders [Names Omitted]under Section 13-6 of the
Book of Church Order were satisfied, including satisfaction
before theCandidates and Credentials Committee and on the

floor of Presbytery of examinationsof their views on theology,
the Sacraments, and church government, and the Stated Clerk
hasbeen authorized to make a marginal notation in the minutes
indicating thesecorrections.
**2023-06: Apr 23, 2022; Aug 20, 2022** ( _BCO_ 13-6) —
Incomplete record of examination of TE transferring into
Presbytery.
**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets its oversite. Transferring TEs are
examined in their views of theology in accordance with _BCO_
13-6 both in committee and on the floor of presbytery. This
examination occurred by the minutes failed to memorialize
the event. The Presbytery will endeavor to be more diligent in
recording topics of views examination in its minutes.
**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

<a id="ga52-p740"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=742 printed_page=740 -->

**Response:** No meeting of the Presbytery occurred on
April 23, 2022. If the cited meeting is the one that took place
on April 30, 2022, Teaching Elders [Names Omitted]were
examined under Section 13-8 of the Book of Church Order

before the Candidates and Credentials Committee and on the

floor of Presbytery on their views on theology, the
Sacraments, and church government, and the examinations
were sustained. The Stated Clerk has been authorized by the
Presbytery to make a marginal notation in the minutes
indicating these corrections. If the citation is to the meeting
on August 23, 2021, the minutes reflect that Teaching Elder

[Name Omitted], transferring from Nashville Presbytery, was
examined on theology and the examination was sustained.
No meeting of the Presbytery occurred on August 20, 2022.
If the cited meeting is the one that took place on August 13,
2022, Teaching Elder [Name Omitted] was examined under
Section 13-6 of the Book of Church Order before the

Candidates and Credentials Committee and on the floor of

Presbytery on his views on theology, the Sacraments, end the
examination was sustained.

**2023-07: Apr 29–30, 2022** ( _BCO_ 13-1) — Minutes indicate
honorably retired te is a member o fa church.
**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to reflect
that Honorably Retired Teaching Elder [Name Omitted] is not
"a member at [Church Name Omitted] [Church in] [Name
Omitted]" but attends that church, and the Stated Clerk has
been authorized to make a marginal notation in the
minutesindicating this correction.
**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that Honorably Retired Teaching Elder [Name
Omitted] is not "a member at [Church Name Omitted]

[Church in] [Name Omitted]" but attends that church, and the
Stated Clerk has been authorized to make a marginal notation
in the minutesindicating this correction.
**2023-08: Apr 29–30, 2022** ( _BCO_ 8-7; 20-1) — No record of
the reasons why Presbytery considers an out-of-bounds work
t ob ea valid christian ministry. No record that the Presbytery

<a id="ga52-p741"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=743 printed_page=741 -->

has full assurance that the TE will have full freedom to

maintain and teach the doctrine of our Church.

**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets its oversite. TE additionally reported to
our MTW and Candidates committees that he would have

freedom to teach the doctrines of the Confession of Faith. His

work is with the [Church Name Omitted]. The [Church Name
Omitted] is over 100 years old. He is the new senior pastor of
the church, following two other PCA pastors in that role. The
Presbytery will be more diligent to record these details in
future minutes

**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that the Presbytery, based on inquiries by the
Ministerial Relations, Mission to the World,and Candidates
and Credentials Committees, found that Teaching Elder

[Name Omitted], in laboring out of bounds assenior pastor at
the [Church Name Omitted], Columbia, where he would be
following two other Presbyterian Church in America pastors
in that role, would be engaged ni a valid Christian ministry,
preaching and teaching theWord, and was assured thathe
would have full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine

of our Church, annuallyreporting to the Presbytery, and the
Stated Clerk has been authorized to make a marginal notation
in the minutes indicating this correction..
**2023-11: Apr 29–30, 2022** ( _BCO_ 13-6) — Incomplete record
of examination of TE transferring into Presbytery.
**Response:** Teaching Elder [Name Omitted] was
examined under Section 13-6 of the Book of Church Order

before the Candidates and Credentials Committee and on the

floor of Presbytery on his views on theology, the Sacraments,
and church government, and the examination was sustained.
The Stated Clerk has been authorized by the Presbytery to
make a marginal notation in the minutes indicating this
correction.

**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

<a id="ga52-p742"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=744 printed_page=742 -->

**Response:** Teaching Elder [Name Omitted] was
examined under Section 13-6 of the Book of Church Order

before the Candidates and Credentials Committee and on the

floor of Presbytery on his views on theology, the Sacraments,
and church government, and the examination was sustained.
The Stated Clerk has been authorized by the Presbytery to
make a marginal notation in the minutes indicating this
correction.
**2023-12: Apr 29-30, 2022; Aug 12-13,2022; Oct 28-29,**
**2022** ( _BCO_ 46-6) — No record of dismissal from original
presbytery.
**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets the errors. All TEs were dismissed from
their previous presbyteries in good standing; the Clerk failed
to note those details in the minutes. The Presbytery will be
diligent to include this information in the minutes in the
future.

**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that Teaching Elders [Names Omitted] were each
dismissed as members in good standing from their respective
presbyteries - Calvary for Radcliff, Suncoast for West, Great
Lakes for [Name Omitted], and South Coast for [Name
Omitted] - under Section 46-6 of the Book of Church Order,
and the Stated Clerk has been authorized to make a marginal
notation in the minutes indicating these corrections.
**2023-13: Aug 12-13, 2022; Oct 28-29, 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-3; 367; 38-1; 40-2; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) — Minutes provide an
incomplete record of cases of discipline, and complaints
received. Minutes indicate commission received confession of

a TE and imposed censures of deposition from office and
indefinite suspension from the sacraments. This may have
been a _BCO_ 38-1 case without process but there is no
indication; it appears from the AC report that this commission
was retroactively authorized and therefore had no authority to
conduct the case or impose censure; also no indication
procedures in _BCO_ 36-7 were followed; also not clear if
accused was a church officer; confession is not in minutes.

<a id="ga52-p743"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=745 printed_page=743 -->

**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets its error. This was a case without
process. The Presbytery erred in its initial handling the case
and these errors were reported by the Administrative
Committee to the floor of Presbytery. The Presbytery chose to
ratify the actions of the judicial commission and the case
continued to the deposition of Mr. [Name Omitted]. The
recitation required by _BCO_ 36-7 was read to Mr. [Name
Omitted], but the Clerk failed to note that in the minutes of
the Presbytery. Mr. [Name Omitted] then chose to associate
with another branch of the Church, severing his relationship
with the PCA. The Presbytery will endeavor to avoid such
irregularities in exercising discipline in the future.
**Rationale:** There is no record that the relevant

minutes have been corrected or that the appropriate
documents related to the 38-1 matter have been attached to the

minutes.

**Response:** As noted in the Presbytery's response to
the 50th General Assembly, the Presbytery confirms that the
matter involving Teaching Elder [Name Omitted] was a case
without process under Section 38-1 of the Book of Church
Order. The Presbytery acknowledges that errors were made in
the proceeding, including in the establishment of a judicial
commission under Chapter 15, as is reflected in the minutes
of the Presbytery. The Session received the confession of

[Name Omitted], and a copy will be maintained in the records
of the Presbytery. The Presbytery also affirms that the censure
of deposition was administered to [Name Omitted] in
accordance with Section 36-7. As is noted in the minutes and

the response, [Name Omitted] subsequently associated with
another branch of the Church. The minutes of the Presbytery
have been corrected to reflect this response, and the stated
clerk has been authorized by the Presbytery to make a
marginal notation in the minutes indicating this correction.
**2023-14: Aug 12–13, 2022** ( _BCO_ 21-2) — Presbytery did not
find extraordinary circumstance or record super-majority vote
to waive six-month requirement to be taken under care.
**Response:** South Texas Presbytery concurs with the
Exception and regrets its error. The statement concerning the

<a id="ga52-p744"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=746 printed_page=744 -->

waiver of the six-month requirement included in the minutes
was inadequate and conclusory. The vote to take Mr. [Name
Omitted] under care was unanimous, but the Clerk failed to
record that fact. The Presbytery will endeavor to be more
precise in its record keeping in the future.
**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected toreflect
that the Presbytery deemed extraordinary under Section 18-2
of the Book of Church Order the situation of [Name Omitted]
with respect to his being a member of the congregation the
session of which provides his endorsement and, after
determination by the Candidates and Credentials Committee
of the requirements under section 19-16, voted unanimously
to approve his previous experience as equivalent to an
internship, and the Stated Clerk has been authorized to make
a marginal notation ni the minutes indicating these
corrections.

**2023-15: Oct 28–29, 2022** ( _BCO_ 21-1) — No record that
transfer candidate received a call or that the presbytery
approved the call.
**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to reflect
that the call of Teaching Elder [Name Omitted] to [Church
Name Omitted] in [Name Omitted] was reviewed by the
Candidates and Credentials Committee and presented to the
Presbytery, found in good order, deemed for the good of the
Church under Section 46-6 of the Book of Church Order, and
placed in [Name Omitted]'s hands, who found it acceptable,
and the Stated Clerk has been authorized to make a marginal
notation in the minutes indicating this correction.
**Rationale:** There is no record that the minutes have

been corrected.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that the call of Teaching Elder [Name Omitted] to

[Church Name Omitted] in [Name Omitted] was reviewed by
the Candidates and Credentials Committee and presented to
the Presbytery, found in good order, deemed for the good of
the Church under Section 46-6 of the Book of Church Order,
and placed in [Name Omitted]'s hands, who found it

<a id="ga52-p745"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=747 printed_page=745 -->

acceptable, and the Stated Clerk has been authorized to make
a marginal notation in the minutes indicating this correction.
**2023-17: Oct 28–29, 2022** ( _BCO_ 21-1) — No record that
candidate received a call or that the presbytery approved the
call.

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to reflect
that the call of [Name Omitted] to [Church Name Omitted] in

[Name Omitted] was reviewed by the Candidates and
Credentials Committee and presented to the Presbytery, found
in good order, deemed for the good of the Church under
Section 46-6 of the Book of Church Order, and placed in

[Name Omitted]'s hands, who found it acceptable, and the
Stated Clerk has been authorized to make a marginal notation
in the minutes indicating this correction.
**Rationale:** The minutes need to include appropriate
correction

**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to
reflect that the call of [Name Omitted] to [Church Name
Omitted] in [Name Omitted] was reviewed by the Candidates
and Credentials Committee and presented to the Presbytery,
found in good order, deemed for the good of the Church under
Section 46-6 of the Book of Church Order, and placed in
Weller's hands, who found it acceptable, and the Stated Clerk
has been authorized to make a marginal notation in the
minutes indicating this correction.
**2024-06: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4) —
Minutes of commission not entered in Presbytery minutes,
and these must be presented for review next year.
**Response:** Minutes of the commissions to ordain
and/or install Teaching Elders [Names Omitted] were entered
ni the Presbytery minutes in 2024.
**2024-07: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-12; _BCO_ 18-6) — No
record of annual reports received on every intern and on
candidates.

**Response:** The requirement for annual reports to the
Presbytery from all interns and candidates was not satisfied in
2023 and the pres byte ry feels it is impracticable and
unrealistic to seek to secure such reports at this time. Annual
reports to and reports at each stated meeting of the Presbytery

<a id="ga52-p746"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=748 printed_page=746 -->

were presented in 2024. In addition, the directory has been
corrected to accurately reflect all interns and candidates.
**2024-08: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-4; _BCO_ 8-7) — No record of
receiving reports from teaching elders working outside the
bounds, and no record of Presbytery approval of TE laboring
outside bounds.

**Response:** The requirement for annual reports to the
Presbytery from all teaching elders laboring out of bounds
was not satisfied in 2023 and the Presbytery feels it is
impracticable and unrealisticto seek to secure such reports at
this time. Annual reports were presented in 2024. In addition,
the directory has been corrected to accurately reflect the status
of all Teaching Elders laboring out of bounds.
**2024-01: Jan 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — All
specific requirements of licensure exam not recorded.
**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to reflect
that the examination of [Name Omitted] on January 28 on the
Book of Church Order encompassed knowledge of the
government of the Presbyterian Church in America as
outlined in the _BCO_ and that the examination of his Christian

call and experience on August 13, 2022 included his
inwardcall to preach the Gospel such that, with the recitation
of his fulfillment of the other requirements recorded in the
January 2023 minutes, all requirements for his licensure had
been completed, and the Stated Clerk has been authorized to
make a marginal notation in the minutes indicating this
correction.

**2024-02: Jan 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.
**Response:** The minutes have been corrected to reflect
that all requirements of the ordination examination of [Name
Omitted] to the office of minister were properly sustained in
accordance with the Book of Church Order. These

requirements included: (i) Demonstration of a sufficient
knowledge of the Greek and Hebrew languages, the
Candidates and Credentials Committee having reviewed and
accepted the man’s seminary studies wherein these subjects
were included; (ii) Preaching of a sermon before the
Credentials Committee of the Presbytery on April 30, 2022;

<a id="ga52-p747"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=749 printed_page=747 -->

(iii) Preparation of exegetical and theological papers,
reviewed and accepted by the Candidates and Credentials
Committee on January 28, 2023; and (iv) Satisfaction of
examinations of his Christian experience (conducted prior to
the acceptance of his transfer from the Central Carolina
Presbytery on April 30, 2022), and his knowledge of the Book
of Church Order and the government and discipline of the
Church (sustained at the April 30, 2022 Stated Meeting, as
noted in the minutes). The Stated Clerk has been authorized
to make a marginal notation in the minutes indicating this
correction.

**2024-03: Apr 29, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) —
Incomplete record of ordination exam.
**Response:** The minutes have been correctedto reflect
that al requirements ofthe ordination examination of [Name
Omitted] to the office of Teaching Elder under Section 21-4
of the Book of Church Order had been satisfied, including
specifically fulfillment of the requirement for knowledgeof
the Greekand Hebrew languages (which was subsumed ni the
statement ni the minutes that "... [Name Omitted] has
completedal the requirements for ordination", the Candidates
and Credentials Committee having reviewed and accepted on
April 28, 2023 histheological studies that included these
subjects), and theStated Clerk hasbeen authorized to make a
marginalnotation in the minutes indicating this correction.
**2024-04: Apr 29, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-1; _BCO_ 13-3) — Meeting
attendance roll list church with less than 350 members with

three or more ruling elder commissioners present with no
record of which two are voting.
**Response:** The commissioners from [Church Name
Omitted] and [Church Name Omitted] eligible to vote on
behalf of their sessions have been identified. The minutes

have been corrected and notated. Beginning with the August
2024 Stated Meeting, the attendance roll distinguishes
between ruling elder commissioners and other ruling elders
attending the meeting.
**2024-05: Aug 12, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-2; _BCO_ 15-1; _RAO_ 16-3.e.4)
— The Ministerial Relations Committee acted as a

commission without a quorum.

<a id="ga52-p748"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=750 printed_page=748 -->

**Response:** The Ministerial Relations Committee
under the Presbytery Standing Rules adopted by the full
Presbytery "has the authority to act as a commission . . . in
dissolving pastoral relationships . . . where the minister and
congregation formally concurn in such dissolution
and...pastoral problems are not involved." The Presbytery has
received minutes of the electronic votes taken by the
committee, as is permitted under the Standing Rules adopted
by the full Presbytery, on May 10 and June 23, 2023 with
respect to Teaching Elders [Name Omitted] and [Name
Omitted]. The minutes of the Presbytery Stated Meeting on
August 12 have been corrected to note these minutes and the
Stated Clerk authorized to make a notation of this correction.

No separate minutes or the August 11 committee meeting and
the action with respect to Teaching Elder [Name Omitted]
were kept and the committee report of its action relled on the
provision of the Standing Rules. (There is no record in the
minutes of the absence of a quorum for this committee
meeting.)

**2025-08: Jan 23, 2024** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of sixmonths membership for candidate.
