**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-04: Jan 25, 2022; Apr 26, 2022; Aug 23, 2022** ( _BCO_
18-2) — No record of six-months membership for candidate.
**Response:** We agree with the exception. In the future,
we will add the following language to our minutes, “[This
candidate], having met the requirements of _BCO_ 18-2 and
having been examined/questioned in accord with _BCO_ 18-3
was received under care of SEAL Presbytery, as moved by the
Committee and approved by Presbytery.”
**Rationale:** Presbytery must report that the minutes
have been corrected.

**Response:** At the October 22, 2024 meeting of
Presbytery, the previously adopted minutes were corrected
with the following language: "Mr. [Names Omitted], having
met the requirements of _BCO_ 18-2 and having been
examined/questioned in accord with _BCO_ 18-3, was received
under care of SEAL Presbytery, as moved by the Committee
and approved by Presbytery."
**2023-05: Jan 25, 2022** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of
examinations (Under Care / Internship).
**Response:** We agree with the exception. In the future,
we will add the following language to our minutes, “[This
candidate], having met the requirements of _BCO_ 18-2 and
having been examined/questioned in accord with _BCO_ 18-3
was received under care of SEAL Presbytery, as moved by the
Committee and approved by Presbytery.”
**Rationale:** Presbytery must report that the minutes
have been corrected.

<a id="ga52-p750"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=752 printed_page=750 -->

**Response:** At the October 22, 2024 meeting of
Presbytery, the previously adopted minutes were corrected
with the following language: "Mr. [Names Omitted], having
met the requirements of _BCO_ 18-2 and having been
examined/questioned in accord with _BCO_ 18-3, was received
under care of SEAL Presbytery, as moved by the Committee
and approved by Presbytery."
**2024-01: Jan 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2) — Presbytery did not
make record of the trial parts omitted in an extraordinary case
of licensure.

**Response:** At the October 22, 2024 meeting of
Presbytery, the previously adopted minutes were corrected
with the following language: "All portions of _BCO_ 19.2.a-f.
were omitted in the licensure of Dr. [Name Omitted]."
**2024-02: Mar 7, 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-3.c.1) — The portion of the
call for the meeting stating the purpose of the meeting is not
recorded verbatim in the minutes of the meeting.
**Response:** We understand this exception. The stated
purpose of the meeting was included in Appendix B. In the
future, we will include this language in the minutes
themselves.
**2024-03: Dec 14, 2023** ( _RAO_ 16-3.c.1) — The portion of the
call for the meeting stating the purpose of the meeting is not
recorded verbatim in the minutes of the meeting.
**Response:** We understand this exception. The stated
purpose of the meeting was included in Appendix B. In the
future, we will include this language in the minutes
themselves.
**2024-04: Jan 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-10) — No record of 60-days
notice of dissolution.

**Response:** [Church Name Omitted] petitioned
Presbytery for its dissolution at the October 25, 2022 meeting.
The action taken by Presbytery at the January 24, 2023
meeting was the fulfillment of the request after 60-days
notice.

This was our Presbytery's response to 2023-14, which was
found satisfactory by the 51st General Assembly: "We agree
to this exception. This action was in response to a request
received from [Name Omitted] PCA on October 24, 2022. We

<a id="ga52-p751"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=753 printed_page=751 -->

took final action at our next stated meeting, and the property
and members were transferred to the care of the Session of

[Name Omitted] PCA. We failed to include this
communication in our minutes/records. We will correct our

actions in the future."

**2024-05: Apr 25, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of
endorsement by candidate’s Session.
**Response:** Presbytery noted that the requirements of
_BCO_ 18-2 were fulfilled for this candidate. Historically, the
documentation of those requirements has not been recorded in
Presbytery's minutes. To correct all previous records of
Presbytery in this regard would be unnecessarily burdensome,
when we believe this language is sufficient.
**2024-06: Apr 25, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of sixmonths membership for candidate.
**Response:** Presbytery noted that the requirements of
_BCO_ 18-2 were fulfilled for this candidate. Historically, the
documentation of those requirements has not been recorded in
Presbytery's minutes. To correct all previous records of
Presbytery in this regard would be unnecessarily burdensome,
when we believe this language is sufficient.
**2024-07: Aug 25, 2023** ( _BCO_ 15-1) — No record of
commission proceedings/minutes.
**Response:** We agree with this exception. We will
include the minutes of this Commission in the minutes of our

October 22, 2024 minutes as an Appendix.
**2024-08: Oct 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4; _RAO_ 16-3.e.5) — Stated
differences not recorded in the minister’s/candidate’s own

words.

**Response:** This candidate had no stated differences
with our standards. _BCO_ 21-4 does not seem to require such a
statement on the part of the candidate or of Presbytery if such
differences do not exist. If desired, Presbytery can add the
following language in the future: "This candidate had no
stated differences with our standards."
**2024-09: Oct 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4) — No record of requiring
statement of differences with our Standards.

**Response:** This candidate had no stated differences
with our standards. _BCO_ 21-4 does not seem to require such a

<a id="ga52-p752"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=754 printed_page=752 -->

statement on the part of the candidate or of Presbytery if such
differences do not exist. If desired, Presbytery can add the
following language in the future: "This candidate had no
stated differences with our standards."

**2024-10: Oct 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-2.f) — No record of
requiring statement of differences with our Standards.
**Response:** This candidate had no stated differences
with our standards. _BCO_ 21-4 does not seem to require such a
statement on the part of the candidate or of Presbytery if such
differences do not exist. If desired, Presbytery can add the
following language in the future: "This candidate had no
stated differences with our standards."
**2024-11: Oct 24, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4) — No record of requiring
statement of differences with our Standards.

**Response:** This candidate had no stated differences
with our standards. _BCO_ 21-4 does not seem to require such a
statement on the part of the candidate or of Presbytery if such
differences do not exist. If desired, Presbytery can add the
following language in the future: "This candidate had no
stated differences with our standards."

**2024-12: Dec 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-12) — Business conducted
that was not stated in the purpose of the called meeting.
**Response:** No business was conducted with this item.
It was merely an announcement. In the future, we will avoid
giving any informational updates not stated in the purpose of
the called meeting.
**e.** **That the 52nd General Assembly:**

Find that the 30 September 2024 letter from TE
Lupton, RE Nusbaum and TE Eide is not a report of an
“important delinquency or grossly unconstitutional
proceedings” ( _BCO_ 40-5).

**Rationale:** The report by TE Lupton, RE Nusbaum
and TE Eide accurately summarizes the facts, showing that
SEAL Presbytery requested jurisdiction and Philadelphia
Presbytery remitted the case in accordance with _BCO_ 32-9.
While the report is credible, the 52 [nd] General Assembly, based
upon the facts presented, finds no sufficient evidence that
SEAL Presbytery’s actions rise to the level of an important

<a id="ga52-p753"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=755 printed_page=753 -->

delinquency or a grossly unconstitutional proceeding under
_BCO_ 40-5.

77. That the Minutes of **Southern Louisiana Presbytery:** _63-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **Apr 27, 2024; Oct 26, 2024;**
**Nov 5, 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **General 2024; Jan 27,**
**2024.**

**c.** Be approved with exception of substance: **None.**
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-01: General 2022** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record of annual
report(s) of TE(s) laboring out of bounds.
**Response:** SOLA Presbytery apologizes for not
including annual reports from our TE’s laboring out of bounds
in our Minutes. It is our practice to hear reports from our
ministers and churches at the end of our meetings. At that
designated time our ministers laboring out of bounds give a
report before presbytery. We will make an effort to make note
of the reports when they are delivered by our Ministers
laboring out of bounds going forward.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record or actions. Presbytery has not recorded its receipt of
reports from out of bounds ministers.
**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2022, noting the absence of recorded annual reports
from Teaching Elders (TEs) laboring out of bounds, as
required under _BCO_ 8-7. We also recognize that previous
responses to similar citations were deemed unsatisfactory by
the General Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's
Report to understand the rationale behind those findings.
To address this exception and rectify past
deficiencies, the presbytery has taken the following actions:
1. Identification of TEs Laboring Out of Bounds: We
compiled a comprehensive list of TEs currently laboring out
of bounds. These include: [Names Omitted].

<a id="ga52-p754"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=756 printed_page=754 -->

2. Policy Implementation: To prevent future lapses,
the presbytery noted that our standing rules require all
ministers laboring out of bounds to present a report on their
work at our October stated meeting. The Stated Clerk has been
retasked with notifying TEs of this requirement annually and
ensuring the reports are presented for presbytery review.
3. Response to Prior Unsatisfactory Findings: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses lacked
specific detail regarding our actions to correct this deficiency.
The presbytery regrets these oversights and has addressed
them by ensuring that subsequent years include detailed
records of each TE's report and the presbytery's approval or
follow-up actions.
The SOLA Presbytery commits to ongoing
compliance with _BCO_ 8-7 and requests that the General
Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are available to
provide additional documentation or clarification if needed.
**2023-02: General 2022** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; 40-1) — No record of
review of records of church Sessions.

**Response:** SOLA Presbytery apologies for failing to
note the work of our Shepherding Committee who handles the
Review of Sessional Records on behalf of SOLA Presbytery.
It is our usual practice for the Shepherding Committee to take
up the records at our Stated January meeting, review the
records, and then, hand the records back at our Stated April
Meeting. Last year the Committee was able to take up all
records and redistribute them from January to April. It was a
simple oversight on the Clerk’s part to not make note of this
in our minutes. We will attempt to do better in the future.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record or actions. Presbytery has not recorded its review of
Session records for 2022. Presbytery is required to review the
records of lower courts annually and record in its own minutes
whether it approves, disapproves or corrects the records in any
particular ( _BCO_ 40-1, 40-3).
**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2022, noting the absence of a recorded review of

<a id="ga52-p755"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=757 printed_page=755 -->

church Session records as required under _BCO_ 13-9.b and 401. We further recognize that previous responses to similar
citations were found unsatisfactory by the General Assembly,
and we have consulted the Clerk's Report to understand the
deficiencies identified therein.

To address this exception and resolve past
shortcomings, the presbytery has taken the following steps:
1. Review Process in 2022: SOLA Presbytery did
review presbytery records under the oversight of our
Shepherding Committee as outlined in our Standing Rules. It
was simply a failure of our clerk to note that the records were
reviewed and returned with the findings of our review
committee to the individual sessions at our April 2022
meeting.
2. Subsequent Compliance: In order to fix our
delinquency we have amended our April 2022 minutes to
reflect the report that was given to presbytery, noting that no
exceptions of substance were found by our committee in any
of the records in the minutes and were thus returned to the

sessions without need for reporting back.
3. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
deemed unsatisfactory due to lack of specific documentation
of which Sessions were reviewed. The presbytery has
addressed this by ensuring that the minutes for subsequent
years will include detailed records of the review process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
fulfilling its oversight responsibilities under _BCO_ 13-9.b and
40-1 and respectfully requests that the General Assembly find
this response satisfactory. We stand ready to provide
additional details or documentation upon request.
**2023-03: Jan 22, 2022** ( _BCO_ 19-2) — Incomplete record of
licensure exam: no statement of Christian experience and
inward call.

**Response:** The Clerk of SOLA Presbytery apologizes
for failing to include this information in the Minutes. This is
always the first part of the floor examination that we approve
and I simply left it out. We will make every effort to include
this information in the minutes in the future.

<a id="ga52-p756"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=758 printed_page=756 -->

**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record. Presbytery has not recorded the receipt of a statement
of Christian experience and inward call from the licensee.
**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
January 22, 2022, stated meeting, noting that the record of a
licensure examination is incomplete under _BCO_ 19-2 due to
the absence of statements regarding the candidate's Christian
experience and inward call. We also recognize that previous
responses to this citation were found unsatisfactory by the
General Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report
to identify the deficiencies therein.
To address this exception and rectify past
shortcomings, the presbytery offers the following:
1. Details of the January 22, 2022, Examination:
Upon review, the presbytery confirmed that the Candidate
was examined for licensure at the January 22, 2022, meeting.
The examination included questions on his Christian
experience and inward call, as required by _BCO_ 19-2, and he
provided satisfactory responses. However, these components
were not explicitly recorded in the original minutes. To
correct this, the presbytery amended the January 22, 2022,
minutes at its meeting to include: "The presbytery examined
Candidate Charles Marchman on his Christian experience,
noting his testimony of conversion, and his inward call."
2. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
deemed unsatisfactory due to lack of an amended record. The
presbytery has addressed this by amending the 2022 minutes
with specific details and establishing a clear process for future
licensure exams.

The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
conducting and documenting licensure exams in full
compliance with _BCO_ 19-2 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation or clarification
if needed.

<a id="ga52-p757"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=759 printed_page=757 -->

**2023-07: Aug 2, 2022** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — Presbytery did not act
to dissolve a TE’s call and place him on the rolls without call
after his church left the denomination and he left the field.

**Response:** The Clerk of SOLA Presbytery apologizes
for failing to include this action in our minutes. It was an
oversight on the clerk’s part. We will make every effort to
include all actions of the Presbytery in our minutes in the
future.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record. Presbytery has not recorded the dissolution of the TE’s
call.

**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
August 2, 2022, stated meeting, noting that the presbytery
failed to act under _BCO_ 23-1 to dissolve the call of Teaching
Elder [Name Omitted] and place him on the rolls without call
after his congregation, [Church Name Omitted], withdrew
from the Presbyterian Church in America and he ceased
serving that field. We also recognize that previous responses
to this citation were deemed unsatisfactory by the General
Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report to
understand the deficiencies identified.

To address this exception and correct past
shortcomings, the presbytery has taken the following steps:
1. Circumstances of the 2022 Omission: In June

2022, [Church Name Omitted] voted to leave the PCA, and
TE [Name Omitted] subsequently left his position as pastor of
that congregation. At the August 2, 2022, meeting, the
presbytery was informed of these events but failed to take
formal action to dissolve his call due to a lack of familiarity
with the process to handle these sorts of cases. The minutes of
that meeting do not reflect the required action under _BCO_ 231. We apologize for this failure.
2. Corrective Action: At our April 2025 stated
meeting, the presbytery will address this omission by voting
to dissolve TE [Name Omitted]'s call to [Church Name
Omitted], effective July 3, 2022. This action will be recorded
in the minutes at our April 2025 stated meeting

<a id="ga52-p758"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=760 printed_page=758 -->

3. Verification of Status: TE [Name Omitted]
received a call to serve as the minister at [Church Name
Omitted], Louisiana and remains a Minister in Good Standing
in our presbytery. WE recognize the formal error of approving
this call without first dissolving TE [Name Omitted]'s call
with [Church Name Omitted] and we will not make the same
mistake again.
4. Procedural Safeguards: To prevent future lapses
under _BCO_ 23-1, the presbytery will make sure to
appropriately label TEs according to their status in their
church. In addition, we will revise our standing rules to clarify
which committee is responsible for overseeing the dissolution
of calls.

The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
upholding _BCO_ 23-1 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
available to provide additional documentation or clarification
if requested.
**2023-10: Oct 22, 2022** ( _BCO_ 5-9) — Presbytery organized a
church that had not elected ruling elders or called a pastor.

[attached Dec. 19, 2022 commission minutes].
**Response:** The commission minutes unfortunately
were incomplete and we apologize for not completing them
prior to filing our RPR report last year. Five men were duly
elected and installed as Ruling Elders and three men were duly
elected and installed as Deacons by the church. Please forgive
our failure to include that information. We will make every
effort to correct this failure in the future.
**Rationale:** Presbytery’s response does not record the
names of officer candidates and all necessary steps for officer
election and examination.

**Response:** The SOLA Presbytery acknowledges the
exception of substance cited by the Committee on Review of
Presbytery Records (RPR) for its December 19, 2022
commission meeting to organize [Church Name Omitted] in
St. Francisville, Louisiana as a particular church under _BCO_
5-9 without evidence of elected ruling elders or a called
pastor. We also recognize that previous responses to this
citation were deemed unsatisfactory by the General

<a id="ga52-p759"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=761 printed_page=759 -->

Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report to
address the deficiencies identified therein.

To resolve this exception and correct past
shortcomings, the presbytery provides the following:
1. Circumstances of the October 22, 2022,
Organization: At the October 22, 2022, meeting, the
presbytery voted to organize [Church Name Omitted] as a
particular church. At that time, the congregation had not yet
elected ruling elders or called a pastor due and was going to
be doing so at a called meeting prior to the Commission's
meeting on December 19th.
2. Corrective Action via Commission: On December

19, 2022, a pres byte ry commission met fora particularization
service. The commission oversaw the election of 5 Ruling
Elders ([Names Omitted]) and facilitated the call of TE [Name
Omitted] as pastor, with the congregation voting to approve
both actions on that date. The commission's report, adopted at
the January 28, 2023 stated presbytery meeting has been
amended to reflect this action taken by the commission of
SOLA Presbytery.
3. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
unsatisfactory due to a lack of detail on corrective actions. The
presbytery has addressed this by providing specific actions
taken on December 19, 2022, amending the 2022 minutes, and
establishing a preventive process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
organizing churches in accordance with _BCO_ 5-9 and
respectfully requests that the General Assembly find this
response satisfactory and we are available to supply additional
documentation if needed.
**2023-11: Oct 22, 2022** ( _BCO_ 21-9) — Presbytery installed a
pastor without having received or approved a call.
**Response:** The commission failed to note that the TE
did receive a call and it was placed in his hands. Please forgive
this failure on our part. We will make every effort to include
this information going forward.

<a id="ga52-p760"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=762 printed_page=760 -->

**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
actions ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not approved the
terms of call for the TE.

**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 22, 2022, stated meeting, noting that the presbytery
installed Teaching Elder TE [Name Omitted] as pastor of

[Church Name Omitted] Church without having received or
approved a call, in violation of _BCO_ 21-9. We also recognize
that previous responses to this citation were deemed
unsatisfactory by the General Assembly, and we have
reviewed the Clerk's Report to address the deficiencies
identified.

To resolve this exception and correct past
shortcomings, the presbytery offers the following:
1. Circumstances: At the October 22, 2022, meeting,
it is noted that SOLA Presbytery approved the petition of

[Church Name Omitted] church to be particularized as a
congregation of the PCA. The Presbytery voted to receive

[Church Name Omitted] and a commission was appointed by
the Moderator for the threefold purpose of particularizing

[Church Name Omitted] Church, approving the call of TE

[Name Omitted] and examining the Ruling Elders to be
elected by the congregation. There was a discussion at that
time about the irregularity of approving the particularization
of [Church Name Omitted] without the pastoral call being
approved by Presbytery.
2. Corrective Action: At that time we understood that

since TE [Name Omitted] had already been approved to
organize the church plant and since he was already a member
in good standing of our presbytery that the irregularity of the
request could be rectified prior to the commission meeting on
December 18, 2022. Therefore, prior to the December 18,
2022 commission meeting, [Church Name Omitted] voted to
call TE [Name Omitted] as their pastor. At that meeting the
commission of SOLA Presbytery reviewed this call and
approved it as part of its actions on behalf of presbytery,
however, this action was not reflected in the minutes

<a id="ga52-p761"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=763 printed_page=761 -->

presented to Presbytery. Therefore, we have corrected the
minutes to include the commission's approval of TE's Shaw's
call to [Church Name Omitted] Church.
3. Verification: The presbytery confirmed with

[Church Name Omitted] that the congregation indeed voted to
call TE [Name Omitted] on December 19, 2025, and TE

[Name Omitted] accepted the call. This supports the
retroactive approval and ensures compliance with _BCO_ 21-9.
4. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
unsatisfactory due to "failure to document the call's approval"
and "lack of corrective action". The presbytery has addressed
this by retroactively approving the call, amending the
minutes, and establishing a preventive process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
adhering to _BCO_ 21-9 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation or clarification
if needed.

**2024-04: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; _BCO_ 40-1; 40-3) — No
record of review of records of church Sessions.

**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2023, noting the absence of a recorded review of
church Session records as required under _BCO_ 13-9.b and 401. We further recognize that previous responses to similar
citations were found unsatisfactory by the General Assembly,
and we have consulted the Clerk's Report to understand the
deficiencies identified therein.

To address this exception and resolve past
shortcomings, the presbytery has taken the following steps:
1. Review Process in 2023 (Same as the process in
2022): SOLA Presbytery did review presbytery records under
the oversight of our Shepherding Committee as outlined in our
Standing Rules. It was simply a failure of our clerk to note
that the records were reviewed and returned with the findings
of our review committee to the individual sessions at our April
2022 meeting.

<a id="ga52-p762"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=764 printed_page=762 -->

2. Subsequent Compliance: In order to fix our
delinquency we have amended our April 2023 minutes to
reflect the report that was given to presbytery, noting that no
exceptions of substance were found by our committee in any
of the records in the minutes and were thus returned to the

sessions without need for reporting back.
3. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
deemed unsatisfactory due to lack of specific documentation
of which Sessions were reviewed. The presbytery has
addressed this by ensuring that the 2023 (and subsequent
years) include detailed records of the review process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
fulfilling its oversight responsibilities under _BCO_ 13-9.b and
40-1 and respectfully requests that the General Assembly find
this response satisfactory. We stand ready to provide
additional details or documentation upon request.
**2024-05: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record of annual
report(s) of TE(s) laboring out of bounds.
**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2023, noting that the presbytery's minutes for 2023
do not include annual reports from candidates under care, as
required by _BCO_ 18-6.
To address this exception, the presbytery provides the
following:
1. Circumstances of the 2023 Omission: The

presbytery currently has two candidates under care: Candidate

[Name Omitted] and Candidate [Name Omitted]. Upon
review by consultation with the chair of the Candidates
Committee these candidates submitted annual reports to the
Candidates Committee throughout the year and the
Candidates committee reviews those reports and reports on
their review at the October presbytery meeting. These actions
were not recorded in our minutes due to a clerical oversight
by our Clerk.
2. Corrective Action: the commission of presbytery
assigned to deal with the exceptions of substance have

<a id="ga52-p763"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=765 printed_page=763 -->

amended our minutes to reflect this report delivered at our
October 2023 stated Presbytery meeting.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
overseeing candidates under care per _BCO_ 18-6 and
respectfully requests that the General Assembly find this
response satisfactory. We are prepared to provide copies of
the 2023 candidate reports or additional documentation if
needed.
**2024-06: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-6) — Annual reports of
candidates not included.

**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 28, 2023, stated meeting (p. 42), noting that the
minutes lack a record of the ordination questions from _BCO_
21-5 being administered to Teaching Elder [Name Omitted]
during his ordination service.
To address this exception, the presbytery provides the
following:
1. Circumstances of the November 12, 2023,
Ordination: On November 12, 2023, TE [Name Omitted] was
ordained as a teaching elder during a church service held at

[Church Name Omitted], in accordance with _BCO_ 21-5. The
presbytery's commission, appointed at the October 28, 2023
meeting, conducted the service, and the ordination questions
from _BCO_ 21-5 were administered to TE [Name Omitted],
who answered them affirmatively, but the minutes of that
commission meeting incorrectly say that the questions from
_BCO_ 21-9 were administered.

2. Corrective Action: The commission of SOLA

Presbytery empowered to address this deficiency has
confirmed with the commission members that during the
November 12, 2023 service, TE [Name Omitted] was asked
the ordination questions from _BCO_ 21-5, including his
affirmation of PCA doctrine, discipline, and ministerial
duties, and he responded affirmatively. The commission has
amended the minutes of their commission to reflect this

correction by adding: "During the ordination service for TE

[Name Omitted] at [Church Name Omitted] on November 12,

<a id="ga52-p764"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=766 printed_page=764 -->

2023 the commission administered the ordination questions
per _BCO_ 21-5, which he answered in the affirmative."
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
conducting ordinations in accordance with _BCO_ 21-5, within
the context of a church service, and respectfully requests that
the General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation, such as the
commission's report or service order, if needed.
**2024-01: Apr 22, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of sixmonths’ membership or endorsement by candidate’s Session.
**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 28, 2023, stated meeting (p. 33), noting that the
minutes lack a record of Mr. [Name Omitted]'s six-months'
communicant membership in a PCA church or endorsement
by his Session, as required by _BCO_ 18-2, prior to his being
taken under care as a candidate for the gospel ministry.
1. Circumstances: At the October 28, 2023, stated
meeting, the presbytery voted to receive Mr. [Name Omitted]
as a candidate under care. As reported by our candidates
committee, it was determined that Mr. [Name Omitted] had
not been a communicant member of [Church Name Omitted]
at least six-months prior to October 28, 2023. The Presbytery
acted, upon the recommendation of the Candidates
Committee to approve Mr. [Name Omitted] as a candidate for
gospel ministry given his previous labors in the ministry and
his Session's endorsement prior to October 2023. These facts
were inadvertently omitted from the minutes due to clerical
oversight.
2. Corrective Action: The commission of SOLA

Presbytery assigned to respond to these exceptions has
confirmed with the Candidates Committee of Presbytery and

[Church Name Omitted] that Mr. [Name Omitted] was
endorsed by [Church Name Omitted]'s session and approved
by Presbytery under the exceptional provision of _BCO_ 18-2.
Further we have corrected our minutes of the October 28,
2023 stated meeting to reflect this action.

<a id="ga52-p765"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=767 printed_page=765 -->

The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
adhering to _BCO_ 18-2 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation, including
Session records, if needed.
**2024-02: Oct 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of sixmonths’ membership or endorsement by candidate’s Session.
**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 28, 2023, stated meeting (p. 33), noting that the
minutes lack a record of Mr. [Name Omitted]'s six-months'
communicant membership in a PCA church or endorsement
by his Session, as required by _BCO_ 18-2, prior to his being
taken under care as a candidate for the gospel ministry.
1. Circumstances: At the October 28, 2023, stated
meeting, the presbytery voted to receive Mr. [Name Omitted]
as a candidate under care. As reported by our candidates
committee, it was determined that Mr. [Name Omitted] had
not been a communicant member of [Church Name Omitted]
at least six-months prior to October 28, 2023. The Presbytery
acted, upon the recommendation of the Candidates
Committee to approve Mr. [Name Omitted] as a candidate for
gospel ministry given his previous labors in the ministry and
his Session's endorsement prior to October 2023. These facts
were inadvertently omitted from the minutes due to clerical
oversight.
2. Corrective Action: The commission of SOLA

Presbytery assigned to respond to these exceptions has
confirmed with the Candidates Committee of Presbytery and

[Church Name Omitted] that Mr. [Name Omitted] was
endorsed by [Church Name Omitted]'s session and approved
by Presbytery under the exceptional provision of _BCO_ 18-2.
Further we have corrected our minutes of the October 28,
2023 stated meeting to reflect this action.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
adhering to _BCO_ 18-2 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are

<a id="ga52-p766"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=768 printed_page=766 -->

prepared to provide additional documentation, including
Session records, if needed.
**2024-03: Oct 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1) — No record of the
reasons why Presbytery considers an out-of-bounds work to
be a valid Christian ministry.
**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 28, 2023, stated meeting (p. 34), noting that the
minutes lack a record of the reasons why the presbytery
considered Teaching Elder [Name Omitted]'s out-of-bounds
work a valid Christian ministry under _BCO_ 20-1.
To address this exception, the presbytery provides the
following:
1. Circumstances of the October 28, 2023, Action: At
the October 28, 2023, meeting, the presbytery approved a call
for TE [Name Omitted] to serve i nan out-of-bounds work as
Executive Director of Reformed Youth Ministries. The

presbytery evaluated the work and deemed it a valid Christian
ministry based on its alignment with the gospel priorities of
the PCA, its founding by PCA Teaching Elders and Members,
and its close collaboration with the PCA MNA Permanent

Committee. However, due to the assumption that approval
implied validity, these reasons were not explicitly recorded in
the minutes.

2. Corrective Action: The Commission of presbytery
amended the October 28, 2023, minutes to include the
rationale for approving TE [Name Omitted]'s call: "The
presbytery considers TE [Name Omitted]'s work at Reformed
Youth Ministries a valid Christian ministry because it
advances the same mission of the PCA and aligns with the
PCA's mission and vision." This amendment is recorded in

Minutes.

The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
properly approving and documenting calls under _BCO_ 20-1
and respectfully requests that the General Assembly find this
response satisfactory. We are prepared to provide additional
documentation, such as TE [Name Omitted]'s report or
organizational materials, if needed.

<a id="ga52-p767"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=769 printed_page=767 -->

**2024-07: Oct 28, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-5) — No record of the
ordination questions being given to a candidate during his
ordination service.

**Response:** The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2023, noting the absence of recorded annual reports
from Teaching Elders laboring out of bounds, as required
under _BCO_ 8-7. We also recognize that previous responses to
similar citations were deemed unsatisfactory by the General
Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report to
understand the rationale behind those findings.
To address this exception and rectify past
deficiencies, the presbytery has taken the following actions:
1. Identification of TEs Laboring Out of Bounds: We
compiled a comprehensive list of TEs currently laboring out
of bounds. These include: [Names Omitted].
2. Policy Implementation: To prevent future lapses,
the presbytery noted that our standing rules require all
ministers laboring out of bounds to present a report on their
work at our October stated meeting. The Stated Clerk is tasked
with notifying TEs of this requirement annually and ensuring
the reports are presented for presbytery review.
3. Response to Prior Unsatisfactory Findings: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses lacked
specific detail or lack of evidence of presbytery action. The
presbytery regrets these oversights and has addressed them by
ensuring the 2023 minutes (and subsequent years) include
detailed records of each TE's report and the presbytery's
approval or follow-up actions.
The SOLA Presbytery commits to ongoing
compliance with _BCO_ 8-7 and requests that the General
Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are available to
provide additional documentation or clarification if needed.

78. That the Minutes of **Southern New England Presbytery:** _61-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **Feb 15, 2024; Apr 13, 2024;**
**Jun 22, 2024; Jul 13, 2024; Oct 19, 2024; Nov 16, 2024.**

<a id="ga52-p768"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=770 printed_page=768 -->

**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **General 2024; Jan 20,**
**2024; Jul 23, 2024; Sep 21, 2024.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: Apr 26–27, 2024** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record that the
presbytery is assured that an out of bounds TE will have full
freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our church. .
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-01: Sep 16, 2023** ( _BCO_ 38-3.a.) — TE improperly
permitted to withdraw from membership and removed from
roll even though he was not (1) affiliated with another branch
of the church and (2) a member in good standing.
**Response:** Southern New England Presbytery does
respectfully disagree with the exception taken by General
Assembly to its September 2023 stated meeting minutes. The
matter taken exception to was Presbytery’s handling of the
removal of TE [Name Omitted]. The exception states that TE

[Name Omitted] “was not affiliated with another branch of the
church.” While the committee bringing the matter of TE

[Name Omitted] to the floor was not aware of any affiliation
TE [Name Omitted] had entered into, during the floor debate
it was discovered that, in fact, TE [Name Omitted] had
affiliated with another branch. We included the name of that

branch in the body of the minutes. Because he had affiliated
with another branch, Southern New England Presbytery does
not believe that it erred in passing the motion.

79. That the Minutes of **Southwest Florida Presbytery:** _63-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024; Jan 10, 2024;**
**Feb 10, 2024; Apr 18, 2024; May 14, 2024; Jul 23, 2024;**
**Sep 14, 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **Nov 12, 2024.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance: **None.**
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-01: May 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record that the
presbytery is assured that an out of bounds TE will have full
freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our church.

<a id="ga52-p769"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=771 printed_page=769 -->

**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with the Exception
of Substance. The Minutes should have noted that the TEs will

have full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our

church. The Presbytery has noted in its record that the TEs
have full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our

church. The Presbytery promises to be more careful in the
future to state in the minutes that out of bounds TEs will have

full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our church.

**2024-02: May 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record that out of
bounds TE is engaged in preaching and teaching the Word.
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with the Exception
of Substance. The Minutes should have noted that the TEs are

engaged in preaching and teaching the Word. The Presbytery
has noted in its record that the TEs are engaged in preaching
and teaching the Word. The Presbytery promises to be more
careful in the future to state in the minutes that out of bounds

TEs will be engaged in preaching and teaching the Word.
**2024-03: May 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 20-1) — No record of the reason
why presbytery considers an out of bounds work to be a valid
Christian ministry.
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with the Exception
of Substance. The Minutes should have noted that the out of

bounds TEs are engaged in valid ministries because the
ministries allow full freedom to preach and teach the Word.
The Presbytery has noted in its record that the TEs are
engaged in ministries that are considered to be valid. The
Presbytery promises to be more careful in the future to state
in the minutes that it considers the out of bounds work to be a

valid Christian ministry.
**2024-04: Sep 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-6) — Installation questions
for assistant pastor addressed to congregation instead of
session.

**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with the Exception
of Substance. The Commission Report should have noted that
the questions for the assistant pastor were addressed to the
Session and not to the Congregation. The Presbytery has
corrected its record. The Presbytery promises to be more
careful in the future to address the installation questions for
an assistant pastor to the Session.

<a id="ga52-p770"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=772 printed_page=770 -->

80. That the Minutes of **Suncoast Florida Presbytery:** _61-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024; Sep 10, 2024;**
**Sep 10, 2024 (ES); Nov 14, 2023.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **None.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: Feb 13, 2024** ( _BCO_ 13-10) — No record of transfer
or dismissal of members upon dissolving a church.
**2025-02: May 14, 2024** ( _BCO_ 34-10) — No record of 2/3 vote
to divest without censure.
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-04: May 10, 2022** ( _BCO_ 20-1) — No record of the
reasons why Presbytery considers an out-of-bounds work to
be a valid Christian ministry.
**Response:** The Presbytery regrets the error of not
noting that the work is a valid Christian ministry. A note was
added to the minutes of May 10, 2022, to read: “The ministry
to which this TE is called is a valid Christian ministry.” The
Presbytery also notes that the citation regarding an “out-ofbounds ministry” in the September 13, 2022 minutes did not
involve the actual approval of a call, but an inquiry by the
Presbytery regarding a TEs activity.
**Rationale:** No reason given for why it is a valid
Christian ministry.
**Response:** Response (2024): The ministry to which
this TE labors out of bounds is with a ministry of [Church
Name Omitted], a member of the Presbyterian Church in
America. That was a satisfactory reason for the Presbytery to
approve the call.
**2024-01: Feb 14, 2023** ( _BCO_ 21-4) — Candidate’s stated
difference(s) not recorded.
**Response:Response:** The candidate coming for
ordination had been licensed at the previous Stated Meeting
of Presbytery where his stated differences were adjudged “as
more than semantic, but not striking at the vitals of the system
of doctrine as set forth in the Westminster Confession of Faith

and Catechisms.” A note was added to the February minutes

<a id="ga52-p771"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=773 printed_page=771 -->

that the candidate’s stated differences had not changed since
he was licensed at the September 13, 2022 Stated Meeting.
1. _WSC_ 60 states that on the Sabbath day we are to
observe “a holy resting all that day, even from such worldly
employments and recreations as are lawful on other days; and
spending the whole time in the public and private exercises of
God’s worship.” While I largely agree with the Westminster
view of the Sabbath, I believe that lawful, restful recreational
activities are permissible on the Sabbath day.
2. _WLC_ 109, in discussing the Second
Commandment, describes this command as forbidding “the
making any representation of God, of all or of any of the three
persons, either inwardly in our mind, or outwardly in any kind
of image or likeness of any creature whatsoever.” I believe
that the formation of certain images of the three persons of the
Trinity in one’s mind is almost impossible to avoid,
particularly as Jesus took on flesh as a human person, and the
Holy Spirit is depicted variously as a dove, tongues of fire,
etc. I don’t find images such as these to be particularly
harmful in things like children’s books, granted that these
images are not to be worshiped nor are they to be perceived
as actual portrayals of the persons of the godhead.

81. That the Minutes of **Susquehanna Valley Presbytery:** _61-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024; Mar 16,**
**2024; May 18, 2024; May 18, 2024; Oct 1, 2024; Nov 16,**
**2024; Nov 23, 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **Feb 10, 2024.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: Sep 17, 2024** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
Congregation concurred with dissolution of pastoral relations.
**2025-02: Sep 17, 2024** ( _BCO_ 46-6) — No record regarding
the reception of a certificate of dismission from transferring
presbytery (may be accomplished through Clerks portal).
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2024-01: Feb 11, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
Congregation concurred with dissolution of pastoral relations.

<a id="ga52-p772"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=774 printed_page=772 -->

**Response:** Unfortunately, we must respectfully
disagree. Our minutes state that the congregation concurred
with the dissolution of the pastoral relationship with TE

[Name Omitted]. From Item 24 in our February 11, 2023
minutes: “A letter was received from [Church Name Omitted]
requesting dissolution of the pastoral relationship between the
church and Associate Pastor TE [Name Omitted]. Due to
finances the church can no longer support two pastors. The
church and TE [Name Omitted] agreed on severance, and the
congregation voted at a properly called meeting on 1/29/23 to
request dissolving the relationship.”
**e.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, therefore new responses shall be submitted**
**to the following GA:**
**2024-02: Feb 11, 2023** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record that the
Presbytery is assured that an out-of-bounds TE will have full
freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our Church.

**Response:** We acknowledge our neglect in this
matter and will be diligent to ensure going forward that men
receiving an out of bounds call have the full freedom to
maintain and teach the doctrines of our church.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1) Presbytery has not recorded receipt of
an appropriate letter of assurance for the out-of-bounds work.

82. That the Minutes of **Tennessee Valley Presbytery:** _63-0-0_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024; May 11,**
**2024; Aug 13, 2024; Nov 5, 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **None.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: Feb 10, 2024** ( _BCO_ 15-1) — No record of
commission report.
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2022-02: Jul 13, 2021** ( _BCO_ 15-1, 2) — An individual may
not take an action on behalf of a court; a commission is
required.
**Response:** The Tennessee Valley Presbytery
acknowledges and apologizes for the mistake. The motion for

<a id="ga52-p773"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=775 printed_page=773 -->

an individual to be empowered to respond to RPR exceptions
was out of order. We will not make this mistake in the future.
**Rationale:** Presbytery has not corrected its error by
ratifying the actions in question.
**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. At this time TVP
has no record of what actions were taken by the individual on
behalf of the Court three years ago or who the invididual was
who took these actions, and therefore we cannot ratify the
actions. We will do better in the future.
**2023-01: General 2022** ( _BCO_ 40-1; _RAO_ 16-10) — Report
of review by ga of 2021 minutes not included in the minutes
of Presbytery, and no response to the Assembly concerning
disposition of the exceptions of substance.
**Response:** We erred by not reviewing the report by
GA of 2021 and by not approving a response by the deadline.
We are responding now.
**Rationale:** _RAO_ 16-10.a — No response to the
Assembly concerning disposition of an exception of
substance.

**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. We are now
beginning to respond to the Assembly’s exceptions of
substance from 2022.

**2023-02: Jan 8, 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-1) — Commissions are
formed to install previously ordained TEs but no commission
reports are found in subsequent minutes.
**Response:** We erred by not including the installation
reports for TE [Name Omitted] and TE [Name Omitted]. TE

[Name Omitted] was installed January 2022, and we do have
his report. TE [Name Omitted] was installed February 2022,
and we do have his report.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review.

**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. Our clerk
responded in 2023, “We erred by not including the installation
reports for TE [Name Omitted] and TE [Name Omitted]. TE

<a id="ga52-p774"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=776 printed_page=774 -->

[Name Omitted] was installed January 2022, and we do have
his report. TE [Name Omitted] was installed February 2022,
and we do have his report.” We have included this response
for approval at our Feb. 2025 Stated Meeting.
**2023-05: Apr 9, 2022** ( _BCO_ 19-7-10) — Presbytery “forms
an internship” for a candidate, but does not approve the nature
of the internship, does not examine the man, and does not
address the intern as required.
**Response:** We erred by not recording the nature of
Mr. [Name Omitted]’s internship, by not specifying that this
internship candidate was examined with regard to his inward
call to the ministry of the Word, and that the intern was
addressed according to _BCO_ 19-10. we did do all of these.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review.

**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. Our clerk
responded in 2023, “We erred by not recording the nature of
Mr. [Name Omitted]’s internship, by not specifying that this
internship candidate was examined with regard to his inward
call to the ministry of the Word, and that the intern was
addressed according to _BCO_ 19-10. we did do all of these.”
We will submit this response to the Presbytery for approval in
February 2025.
**2023-06: Apr 9, 2022; Jul 12, 2022; Oct 18, 2022** ( _BCO_ 182; _RAO_ 16-6.c.2) — No record of six-months membership
requirement and one month application deadline for man
coming under care. This was an Exception of Form also in the
report on the 2021 minutes.
**Response:** We erred by not recording that Mr. [Name
Omitted], Mr. [Name Omitted], and Mr. [Name Omitted]
satisfied all the requirements of _BCO_ 18-2, including the sixmonth membership requirement. They did satisfy those
requirements at that time.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review.

<a id="ga52-p775"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=777 printed_page=775 -->

**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. Our clerk
responded in 2023, 'We erred by not recording that Mr. [Name
Omitted], Mr. [Name Omitted], and Mr. [Name Omitted]
satisfied all the requirements of _BCO_ 18-2, including the sixmonth membership requirement. They did satisfy those
requirements at that time.' We will submit this response to the
Presbytery for approval in Feb. 2025 with the promise to be
more careful henceforth.

**2023-07: Apr 9, 2022** ( _BCO_ 8-7; 15-1; 20-1; 21-1) — A TE’s
status is changed from Honorably Retired to Out of Bounds,
but specific arrangements of call not shown to be approved.
No record that the Presbytery is assured that an out-of-bounds
TE will have full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine

of our Church. No record of the reasons why Presbytery
considers an out-of-bounds work to be a valid Christian

ministry.
**Response:** We erred by not specifying that we did in
fact approve of TE [Name Omitted]’s call, and we erred by
not including that call in the minutes. We erred by not making
sure the call had language guaranteeing that TE will have full
freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our Church. We

erred by not recording the reasons why we consider the work
o fa chaplain t ob ea valid christian ministry
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review.

**Response:** The clerk responded in 2023: “We erred
by not specifying that we did in fact approve of TE [Name
Omitted]’s call, and we erred by not including that call in the
minutes. We erred by not making sure the call had language
guaranteeing that TE will have full freedom to maintain and
teach the doctrine of our Church. We erred by not recording
the reasons why we consider the work o fa chaplain t ob ea
valid Christian ministry.” We will submit that to TVP for
approval in Feb. 2025.
**2023-08: Apr 9, 2022; Oct. 18, 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-1; 23-1) — A
commission dissolves men’s call, but no evidence of

<a id="ga52-p776"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=778 printed_page=776 -->

congregational (or sessional) concurrence, and sometimes the
matter is not included in the minutes of Presbytery.
**Response:** We erred by not including the minutes
from congregational or session meetings where the
dissolution of pastoral relationship for TE [Name Omitted],
TE [Name Omitted], and TE [Name Omitted] were voted on.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review.

**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. The dissolutions
in question did receive appropriate concurrence by the proper
body (congregations or sessions) at the time, but this was not
included in the TVP minutes. We will be more diligent
regarding this matter in the future.
**2023-09: Jul 12, 2022** ( _BCO_ 18-2, 3) — No record of
endorsement by candidate’s session, six-months membership,
charge given to candidate, nor of his answering the required
questions.
**Response:** We erred by not recording that Mr. [Name
Omitted] satisfied all the requirements of _BCO_ 18-2,
including the six-month membership requirement and an
endorsement by the candidate’s session. He did satisfy those
requirements at that time. We erred by not properly recording
that we did in fact give a charge and that the candidate did
answer the questions in 18-3.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _RAO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the
minutes for review.

**Response:** In 2023 the clerk responded: “We erred
by not recording that Mr. [Name Omitted] satisfied all the
requirements of _BCO_ 18-2, including the six-month
membership requirement and an endorsement by the
candidate’s session. He did satisfy those requirements at that
time. We erred by not properly recording that we did in fact
give a charge and that the candidate did answer the questions
in 18-3.” We will submit this response to TVP for approval in
Feb. 2025.

<a id="ga52-p777"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=779 printed_page=777 -->

have at the time and which does satisfy that _BCO_ 5-9.i was

diligent in our responding in the future. We thank the
assembly for its careful review of these matters.

— Notice for called meeting not in order (10-day notice,
verbatim meeting call, etc., not indicated/recorded).
**Response:** TVP Apologizes for the oversight in
failing to include this documentation. Ten day notice of this
meeting was provided via email on March 1, 2023.
**2024-06: Mar 23, 2023** ( _BCO_ 30-1) — Presbytery requires
additional steps after the censure of admonition is
administered.

**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. These steps were
not required by the Presbytery, but TE [redacted] requested
this be part of the restitution. TVP agrees this should not have

<a id="ga52-p779"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=781 printed_page=779 -->

been included in the admonition. The new censure approved
is as follows: Whereas you, TE [redacted], are guilty by your
own confession of the sin of cheating on your ordination
exams, we, the Tennessee Valley Presbytery, in the name and
by the authority of the Lord Jesus Christ, do admonish you for
this violation of our Lord’s commands. We warn you of the
severity of this sin, and we encourage you to take all necessary
steps of restitution.We further exhort you to examine your
own heart and to consider how similar sin may be avoided in
the future. We encourage you to remember that God is faithful
and just to forgive all who confess and that you are counted
as righteous in the court of heaven through faith in Jesus
Christ alone, who loved you and gave himself up for you. May
God bring you restoration, peace, and strength as you continue
to labor in the future as a minister of Jesus Christ.

**Response:** TVP apologizes for this oversight and
thanks the Assembly for its diligent review. We have not been

so in the future.

**2024-01: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-6) — Annual reports of

oversight and will ensure all candidates in our presbytery
make an annual report.
**2024-02: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 10-3) — The Moderator may
be elected for at most 1 year at a time. The Standing Rules
state that he "shall serve until his successor is elected", which
has no limit.

**Response:** Warrior Presbytery apologizes for this
oversight and has begun the process of revising our Standing
Rules to explicitly limit the moderator to, at most, one year of
service at a time.

85. That the Minutes of **West Hudson Presbytery:** _57-0-1_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **General 2024; May 16,**
**2024; Sep 12, 2024; Nov 14, 2024.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **None.**
**c.** Be approved with exception of substance:
**2025-01: Feb 8, 2024** ( _BCO_ 8-7) — No record that TE
laboring as an interim in an EPC church will have the full
freedom to maintain and teach the full doctrine of our church.
**d.** **That the following responses to the 52** **[nd]** **GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-07: Apr 26, 2022** ( _BCO_ 13-6) — No record of which
presbytery TE is transferring from and whether he is a
member in good standing.
**Response:** As far as Presbytery can discern, this is an
error from the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records.
Presbytery did not meet on April 26, 2022.

<a id="ga52-p783"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=785 printed_page=783 -->

**Rationale:** Although the Presbytery did not meet on
April 26, 2022, a commission of presbytery did meet on that
date to examine a TE for transfer (page 2022-20). The
commission minutes fail to record which presbytery the TE is
transferring from and whether he is a member in good
standing.
**Response:** While Presbytery regrets being unable to
locate said minutes last year and is thankful for RPR's help
finding them, Presbytery respectfully disagrees with the
exception. The cited minutes do record the Presbytery of the
Presbyterian Church in Brazil from which the candidate came.
Moreover, _BCO_ 13-6 does not require recording the standing
o fa candidate in the minutes.
**2024-01: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-12) — Reports on every
intern by the committee charged with the oversight of interns
at each stated meeting not included.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with this exception,
though it notes progress has been made from prior years.
Presbytery intends to continue making progress on accurately
reflecting in its minutes its oversight of interns.
**2024-03: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-6) — Annual reports of
candidates not included.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
promises to do better in the future.
**2024-04: General 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-12) — Annual report of
Interns not included.

**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception and
promises to do better in the future.
**2024-02: Feb 9, 2023** ( _BCO_ 19-6) — 3/4 vote for waiving
internship requirement not recorded.
**Response:** Presbytery agrees with the exception. The
record has been corrected to indicate that the approval of the
candidate's prior experience passed without objection.

that [Name Omitted] had been a member of [Church Name
Omitted] since July 2022 and [Name Omitted] had been a
member of [Church Name Omitted] for six years. The
Presbytery promises to be more careful in the future.
**2024-03: Jul 22, 2023** ( _BCO_ 18-2) — No record of sixmonths membership for candidates.
**Response:** The Presbytery agrees with the exception,
has voted to correct its minutes from July 22, 2023, to reflect
that [Name Omitted] had been a member of [Church Name
Ommitted] for seven months. The Presbytery promises to be
more careful in the future.

Report of the Commission for Review of Records from Korean Southwest

**REPORT OF THE**
**COMMISSION OF THE 51** **[st]** **GENERAL ASSEMBLY**

**REGARDING REVIEW OF RECORDS FROM**

**KOREAN SOUTHWEST PRESBYTERY**

The 51 [st] General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America,
without objection, empowered the Moderator to appoint a commission. The
Moderator subsequently appointed RE Paul Goodrich, RE Flynt Jones, TE
Hoochan Paul Lee, RE Frederick Neikirk, TE Stephen O’Neill, and TE Jason
Piland. The Commission met and accomplished its mandates as shown below.
It also determined that the following prefatory material would be useful for the
Presbytery and the Church at large in explaining the context of the
Commission’s actions.

The four items, which came to the Assembly as unanimous
recommendations from the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records
(RPR), are as follows:

1. To review all outstanding exceptions of substance to Korean
Southwest Presbytery's minutes, and to determine which
exceptions Presbytery shall be required to respond to, being
serious irregularities that require review and correction.
2. To review the 2023 minutes of Korean Southwest Presbytery,
determining which serious irregularities shall require review
and correction.

3. To report their findings to Korean Southwest Presbytery 30
days prior to Presbytery's autumn stated meeting.
4. To report their findings to the 52nd General Assembly
through the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records.

Ordinarily, the review of Presbytery records is carried out by the RPR
committee of the Assembly, and not by a commission thereof. So also, all
exceptions of substance to Presbytery minutes ordinarily require responses be
submitted to the subsequent Assembly and corrections be made to the previous
actions where possible. The work of this Commission in reviewing Presbytery
minutes does not change the ordinary procedure by which General Assembly
exercises its right and duty of review and control (BCO 40, RAO 16).

<a id="ga52-p788"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=790 printed_page=788 -->

Ordinary review of Korean Southwest Presbytery’s minutes would
have caused the Assembly to require Presbytery to make numerous corrections
to previous records and actions, which would require a great deal of time and
energy for Presbytery (and Presbytery’s clerk). It was wise for the Assembly,
in this instance, to show grace and fraternal love to Korean Southwest
Presbytery by overlooking some past errors so that Presbytery may focus on
improving things in the future (which it already has demonstrated it is seeking
to do). Simultaneously, it was equitable for the Assembly not to overlook all
past errors, as doing so would be unfair to other Presbyteries, and would
neglect the rights of members of Presbytery and Presbytery’s churches. The
Assembly’s decision to overlook some more minor exceptions of substance,
while requiring responses to others that are of greater consequence, will
promote the welfare of both Korean Southwest Presbytery and the PCA as a
whole.

Therefore, pursuant to the 51 [st] General Assembly’s approval of
recommendations 40.c.1 and 40.c.2 from the Committee on Review of

Presbytery Records, the Commission concludes that the minutes of Korean
Southwest Presbytery: _(6-0-0)_
**a.** Be approved without exception: **None.**
**b.** Be approved with exception of form: **Directory; March 14,**
**2023; September 12, 2023**
**c.** **Be approved with exception of substance to which**
**Presbytery need not respond, but which must be done**
**correctly in future meetings:**
**2024-08: General** — Examinations are either incorrectly
conducted or incorrectly recorded.

i. It is not clear if men are being examined for
ordination, or transfer from another PCA Presbytery,
or transfer from another denomination (see 79.8; 79.9
Credentials Committee; and 79.9 Exam Committee)..
This is important because different examinations are
required in each case (see _BCO_ 13-6; 21-4).
ii. Required elements appear to be missing from
examinations—Experiential Religion, Greek and
Hebrew, Sacraments, theological and exegetical
papers, sermon, and sometimes other things—for
men coming for ordination or transfer from another
denomination.

<a id="ga52-p789"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=791 printed_page=789 -->

**2024-09: General** — No evidence that specific arrangements
of each call are approved by Presbytery ( _BCO_ 20-1, paragraph
2).
**2024-10: General** — No annual reports from candidates, men
serving calls outside the jurisdiction of Presbytery, or men
who are without call ( _BCO_ 13-2; 18-6).
**2024-11: General** — No record of review of records of

church sessions ( _BCO_ 13-9.b; 40-1).
**2024-12: September 12, 2023** ( _BCO_ 23-1) — No record that
Congregation/Session concurred with the dissolution of
pastoral relations.
**d.** **Be approved with exception of substance to which**
**Presbytery must respond and correct their minutes or**
**actions wherever possible:**
**2024-01: General** — Teaching elders are received into
pres byte ry with no evidence o fa call to a definite work ( _BCO_
20-1, paragraph 1). For each man received into Presbytery in
2023, Presbytery must now formally approve their call and
record that call in the minutes.
**2024-02: General** — Presbytery apparently approves calls for
men to work outside the bounds of Presbytery, but does not
record in the minutes why these are needful works and valid
Christian ministries, nor do the minutes demonstrate that there
is assurance the man will have full freedom in preaching and
teaching ( _BCO_ 8-4; 8-7; 20-1) For each man received into
Presbytery in 2023 on the basis o fa call to a workouts ide the
jurisdiction of Presbytery, Presbytery must now approve and
include i nits minutes a statement of why the work is a needful
work and a valid Christian ministry, and how Presbytery has
been assured the man will have full freedom to preach and
teach the doctrine of our Church.

**2024-03: General** — There is no evidence in the minutes as

to whether men examined by Presbytery had stated
differences to the Westminster Standards, and, if they did,
how Presbytery judged those. ( _BCO_ 13-6; 21-4). Presbytery
must now ask each man examined in 2023 if he had any stated
differences to the Westminster Standards, and if he does have
such differences Presbytery must record those differences in

<a id="ga52-p790"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=792 printed_page=790 -->

the man’s own words and judge them according to the _BCO_
and _BCO_ 16-3.e.6.
**2024-04: General** — Men are removed from the membership
of Presbytery apart from methods set forth in the _BCO_ . This
problem also exists in Chapter 5, paragraphs 3-4 of
Presbytery’s By-Laws. ( _BCO_ 30 spells out censures that
Presbytery may apply after a case without process ( _BCO_ 38)
ora case of process ( _BCO_ 31, 32, 34, 35, 36) or via transfer
( _BCO_ 20-9)). Presbytery must either demonstrate that each
man who was removed from Presbytery in 2023 was removed
by a Constitutional procedure or the man must be restored to
the roll of Presbytery until a Constitutional process is
followed.
**2024-05: Bylaws** — Article 22 of the By-Laws contains an
unconstitutional provision. Presbytery may not mandate
ordination after one year of licensure. The requirements of
_BCO_ 20 and 21 must be met.
**2024-06: Bylaws** — Article 23 of the By-Laws contains an
unconstitutional provision. Presbytery may not mandate the
length of training a Session decides to employ for new
officers. It can only review minutes to be sure training and
examinations were accomplished ( _BCO_ 11-4; 24-1).
**2024-07: September 12, 2023** ( _BCO_ 13-10) — No record of
transfer or dismissal of members upon dissolving a church.
**e.** **That the following responses to the 51st GA be found**
**satisfactory:**
**2023-3: Bylaws** ( _BCO_ 24-1) — Ruling elders are called by
the local church and trained per session requirements.
Presbytery does not have jurisdiction to dictate length of
training.
**Response:** We corrected err by revising by-laws.
**2023-4: Bylaws** ( _BCO_ 24-1) — Presbytery does not have
jurisdiction to dictate a vote of confidence for elders/session
members in a particular church.
**Response:** We corrected err by revising by-laws.
**2023-6: Mar 15, 2022** ( _BCO_ 24-1) — The church session has
jurisdiction concerning exams.
**Response:** We corrected err by revising by-laws.

<a id="ga52-p791"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=52 pdf_page=793 printed_page=791 -->

**2023-12: Mar 15, 2022** ( _BCO_ 15-1) — referred t oas a
committee and not a commission.

**Response:** we have erred by misusing committee as
commission.

**f.** **That the following responses to the 51st GA be found**
**unsatisfactory, but not requiring a response in that**
**Presbytery demonstrated in its minutes/responses a**
**commitment to change the patterns that were identified as**

review of records of church session as soon as possible.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
actions ( _BCO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not reviewed session

**Response:** We have erred to include record of review

commissions.
**Rationale:** No record that Presbytery corrected its
record ( _BCO_ 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not submitted the

RE Paul Goodrich (Secretary) Pee Dee Presbytery
